cards.title: [ONBOARDING] Alex Case Study - SID: 58972
cards.text: SID: 58972NAME: oralbCASE: Why do we have a huge drop in VS score?RESPONSE:KWs have a huge amount of landing pages changes which caused the ranks to plummet from the 1st pages to 80+ thus losing all i
cards.html:
SID: 58972

NAME: oralb

CASE: Why do we have a huge drop in VS score?

RESPONSE:

  • KWs have a huge amount of landing pages changes which caused the ranks to plummet from the 1st pages to 80+ thus losing all impression shares.

  • There are also multiple cannibalisation issues for the Landing Pages some of the most dominant KWs would respond to.

  • KWs that were 1st/2nd page fell in ranks past the 2nd page with a very high amount of SV from the total of 930k.

OBSERVATIONS AFTER REVIEW:

cards.title: [Template] Training Call - Volacci (Alex' Onboarding)
cards.text: Call recording: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1e8imiG2CGZ4QrM4UujkbOfPgAHbQ_6W2/viewAudience: TracyPurpose: TrainingCall Summary: Full tool trainingAhref is being used for backlinking.MOZ wasn't use
cards.html:
Call recording: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1e8imiG2CGZ4QrM4UujkbOfPgAHbQ_6W2/view

Audience: Tracy

Purpose: Training

Call Summary: Full tool training

  • Ahref is being used for backlinking.

  • MOZ wasn't user friendly enough.

  • Signals issues - user wanted to hide it or disable.

Customer Questions during the call:

Q1: What separates SEOmonitor from the competition?
A: Daily Ranks for both Mobile and Desktop, no groups/bracket of KWs limitations/no user restriction or cost increase.

Q2: How can I hide or disable Signals?
A: At this point and time we do not have the option to hide the Signals cards or have them disabled however we can pass on the request to our product team.

Q3: What does the Change field do?
A: The change field sort the KWs based on their impact in visibility based on their increase or decreased.

Q4: What does Cannibalization issues mean?
A: When a KW suffers multiple landing page changes or responds to multiple pages.

Q5: What does the Missplelled label mean?
A: It appear in 2 cases:

  • one in which Google explicitly identifies the keyword as a "misspelling", believing the intention of the user is to search for a different query and, in turn, offering results for the said query; an example for this is where we have the "showing results for..." warning at the top (sometimes appearing as "did you mean...").

  • the other one is the one where Google doesn't even give a warning anymore, but just directly provides results for a variation of the search query, as it identifies that as the more relevant one.

Q6: What is the limit to the filters in groups? Can I have 75 filters?
A: At this point and time we do not have a limit on the filters that can be set per group. Only that each additional filter will most likely impact performance when accessing said group.

Issues with the tool during the call: User clicked between page and rank columns and a filter was applied to the group. Fullstory Link, Trello Link.

Call Feedback: Overall the call feedback received was good as first engagement. Points to improve on:

  1. Keeping to the allocated time.

  2. Read up on what the misspelled label means.

  3. Clarify the confusion between unallocated and not provided.

  4. Folder vs Group

  5. header vs. top stats (just so it's consistent to the site and other communication)

  6. "sort by rank" vs. "sort by change"

  7. mobile vs. desktop switch

cards.title: "Updated" versus "crawl time" - for ranks, on keyword page
cards.text: INFO ON THIS CARD IS FOR CS KNOWLEDGE ONLY AND IT WILL NOT BE SHARED WITH THE USERSUpdated (https://take.ms/9WTAq) - is for the users and shows the official time when the update should happenCrawl tim
cards.html:
INFO ON THIS CARD IS FOR CS KNOWLEDGE ONLY AND IT WILL NOT BE SHARED WITH THE USERS

Updated (https://take.ms/9WTAq) - is for the users and shows the official time when the update should happen

Crawl time (https://take.ms/GKQQz) - is for master pass and shows the real time of the crawl. Crawl time can be both before and after the Updated.

Tech should be notified if crawl time is older than 1 week.

But bear in mind that there are some specific cases where the crawl is made less often (most common every 3 days), such as:

  • close variations

  • latest rank 99+

  • search volume <= 100

  • brand keywords

  • swarm doesn't cover for the daily crawl need (for various reasons, resources are not enough for all keywords to be processed on time)

cards.title: (annual) prepaid renewal
cards.text: When a client is running out of credits, we get a message in Intercom, that their credit is not enough for another 2 invoices. - https://take.ms/87Uo49If there's a card on file (and the previous prepa
cards.html:
When a client is running out of credits, we get a message in Intercom, that their credit is not enough for another 2 invoices. - https://take.ms/87Uo49

If there's a card on file (and the previous prepaid was paid through card), you can issue and collect immediately - https://take.ms/mlbLS.

(!keep in mind that when the prepaid invoice is issued, any remaining credits are blocked until then - details here)

E​xisting invoice due to insufficient credits

If there is an existing invoice issued due to insufficient credits and you issue a new prepaid invoice, the consumption invoice has to be voided and regenerated from credits. See video here.

Steps:

  1. Delete the uncollected monthly invoice;

  2. Chargebee will ask you if you want to resubmit (the notification appears at the top of the windowing in the subscription).

  3. YES and now since there are credits in the account, the new bill is automatically deducted from them. ( if the amount is different or if there are multiple voided invoices, the new one will have to be create manually).

Note: A client switched from prepaid to monthly payment - what should we do?

cards.title: (technical) SEO advice requests
cards.text: We still don't give advice, but we will (for) now refer anyone who's asking for technical SEO advice to IO Agency (the guys with TUI and others - Lee and Baba and their teams).Of course, only if it is
cards.html:
We still don't give advice, but we will (for) now refer anyone who's asking for technical SEO advice to IO Agency (the guys with TUI and others - Lee and Baba and their teams).

Of course, only if it is not an actual support question. Don't defer these, as they could bite back. :)

We would need to add advice@io.agency in the conversation and they will take it from there. So rather than our usual "nope", we would say something like the following:

Since we can only offer product-related support and not SEO advice, I'd like to introduce you to IO Agency, who are also power users with our platform and can advise you on the best course of action. Hope this helps!

...and then add them in the conversation directly: http://take.ms/Alap1.

cards.html:
Projected non-brand sessions that can be seen in the Objective Status breakdown is the same metric as Improved Traffic, only measured for the last 30 days.

The Actual non-brand traffic in the Forecast corresponds to the non-brand Organic traffic (Sessions under the graph) in Organic Traffic section, for the last 30 days.

Though currently the "i" says "months that passed" it actually shows data even for just a part of the calendar month up to the current date.

Basically if you're only 10 days into the current month it will take 20 days form the previous month in order to compile the necessary last 30 days timeframe

To be clarified the expected behavior while in the first forecasted month.

cards.title: Adding keywords to a pitching campaign on a different date than the creation date.
cards.text: The pitching campaigns are a one-time crawl; if you add keywords after the campaign was created (on a different date), the crawl for the new keywords will be done on the spot.This means that you'll ha
cards.html:
The pitching campaigns are a one-time crawl; if you add keywords after the campaign was created (on a different date), the crawl for the new keywords will be done on the spot.

This means that you'll have today's ranks, but because you cannot change the timeframe in a pitching campaign, the original keywords will have ranks for the creation date and the newly added ones for the date they were added on.

cards.title: Additional Conversions in Forecast
cards.text: The goals selected in Website Settings are taken into accountThe estimation of the additional conversions is based on the estimated additional visits multiplied by the corresponding conversion rate of
cards.html:
The goals selected in Website Settings are taken into account

The estimation of the additional conversions is based on the estimated additional visits multiplied by the corresponding conversion rate of each keyword included in the calculation.

The "Best Match" option will use the first available conversion rate for each keyword, from the following (in this order): 1) the keyword conversion rate, 2) the keyword groups' conversion rate, or 3) the overall non-brand organic traffic conversion rate.

The last option (3) can be enforced for all the keywords, or a custom one can be set up, if there's also an estimated improvement to the conversion rate in the selected timeframe.

Raspuns Dragos: Se tine cont de goals selectate in settings, se calcuelaza un avg conversion rate pe tot site ul si un avg_conversion rate pe keyword si estimarile sunt facute in functie de cel de pe keyword daca exista, sau cel de pe site daca nu exista, avg sunt calculate pentru goal urile selectate

cards.title: Additional Sessions in Opportunity, in the Rank Tracker, versus Additional Sessions in Forecasting
cards.text: If you compare the additional sessions once in top 3 estimated in the Opportunity Score: https://take.ms/tPkaI to the additional sessions once in top 3 from the "Analyze Calculation" option in Forecas
cards.html:
If you compare the additional sessions once in top 3 estimated in the Opportunity Score: https://take.ms/tPkaI to the additional sessions once in top 3 from the "Analyze Calculation" option in Forecast: https://take.ms/Yb1ZQ you will notice a different number between these sections.

  • Additional Sessions in Opportunity only take desktop ranks (in Difficulty) into account. --> they will be added into the export.

  • Additional Sessions in Forecast also take into account the mobile search % and the CTR curve(s), and you can also include/exclude the year-over-year trend. --> this is the more accurate measure

Extra details: the Opportunity metric is computed as follows

Opportunity = [Potential new monthly clicks] / [Difficulty to rank in Top10]

where

[Potential new monthly clicks] = Search Volume * [CTR(Top3)-CTR(current rank)]

cards.title: Algorithm for MISSPELLING label
cards.text: Automatically applied to the keywords where the search returns one of the messages (verifiable in the SERP):- did you mean: ...-  showing results for...For the rest, the following steps are made:1. th
cards.html:
Automatically applied to the keywords where the search returns one of the messages (verifiable in the SERP):
- did you mean: ...
- showing results for...

For the rest, the following steps are made:
1. the keyword is split in individual words
2. every individual word is searched for in title and description, all through the Top100 results.

If none of the individual words is identified in any of the results, the keyword is marked as Misspelling.

cards.title: Allowed characters for keywords and group names
cards.text: We generally accept all special language characters.The allowed characters are:For Groups/Folders: a-z A-Z 0-9 - _ . $ % @ # & ' : () a-zA-Z\p{L}\p{Mn}\p{Thai} (no / or \ allowed) - no slash / or back
cards.html:
We generally accept all special language characters.

The allowed characters are:

  • For Groups/Folders: 
a-z A-Z 0-9 - _ . $ % @ # & ' : () a-zA-Z\p{L}\p{Mn}\p{Thai} (no / or \ allowed) - no slash / or backword slash \ allowed
  • For Keywords: 
a-z A-Z 0-9 + - . ' $ % @ # () & _ a-zA-Z\p{L}\p{Mn}\p{Thai}/

Extra: Our REGEX works with a 'u' modifier (PCRE_UTF8), validating the input as UTF-8, starting with the detection of unallowed characters.

  • RegEx - Folders: `/[^:()a-zA-Z0-9-_. $%@#&'a-zA-Z\p{L}\p{Mn}\p{Thai}]/u`
  • RegEx - Keywords: `/[^+a-zA-Z0-9-. '$%@#()&_a-zA-Z\p{L}\p{Mn}\p{Thai}\/]/u`

​Special mentions

  • the most recent addition is `p{Mn}` - “\p{Mn} or \p{Non_Spacing_Mark}: a character intended to be combined with another character without taking up extra space (e.g. accents, umlauts, etc.).”
  • the special foreign characters, such as Arabic, fall under the \p{L} code

cards.title: Are the Visibility Metrics, precision metrics?
cards.text: The visibility is not meant to be used as a precision metric, but a reliable metric used to be compared to itself (apples to apples), and to tell the user how well it ranks, not precisely what impress
cards.html:
The visibility is not meant to be used as a precision metric, but a reliable metric used to be compared to itself (apples to apples), and to tell the user how well it ranks, not precisely what impression rate they have.

There's an impression rate (IR) based on the rank, which can’t be scientifically deducted, and its precision is not so important.

What’s really important is:

  • The Visibility Score percentage is 0% if no keyword in the list ranks in top20

  • The Visibility Score percentage is 100% where all the keywords rank #1, and there’s no more space for improvements

  • The Visibility Score is always calculated the same way, for any keyword list, any competitor, any time.

  • Anything in between follows an impression rate curve which we can share with the client (see here) and which is a rough estimation; Its precision is really irrelevant, as the metric itself is not intended to be used independently, but relative to itself in the past or itself for a competitor.

  • Monitoring the Visibility Score's progression gives an accurate, real-time picture of how well the website ranks in Google for a list of keywords.

cards.title: Are we planning to track other platforms besides searchGPT?
cards.text: We plan to track all major similar platforms in the future, based on their market adoption and user requests. This is the main reason we named it AI Search and not ChatGPT tracking: to allow the addit
cards.html: We plan to track all major similar platforms in the future, based on their market adoption and user requests. This is the main reason we named it AI Search and not ChatGPT tracking: to allow the addition of multiple GPT search results.

cards.title: Articles process
cards.text: The prioritized backlog -> What article should I write or update next? What questions should I answer?The research -> Get a deep understanding of the topic you’ll have to present. Plan meetings with t
cards.html:
1. The prioritized backlog -> What article should I write or update next? What questions should I answer?

  2. The research -> Get a deep understanding of the topic you’ll have to present. Plan meetings with the people that can best help you understand the topic and use the Ask Cosmin Anything session.

  3. The outline (1st demo) -> What information should go into the article and how should it be structured?

  4. The first draft ->  Just do it. It doesn’t have to be perfect yet.

  5. The fine-tuning ->  Read the article again, check for mistakes and improvement opportunities. Check if if follows the guidelines and adapt where necessary. Ask your colleagues for feedback. And do your best to create a high quality article that clearly and concisely answer the initial questions.

  6. The test -> Go ahead and ask an Upswinger to read your article and answer your questionnaire. If it still needs improvements go back to step 5 and then repeat the process with another Upswinger.  

  7. The final demo-> Present the article in a demo with Cosmin. The result of the meeting is a Go Live or Not Ready with clear feedback.


There’s going to be a demo session every week where each CS will present the progress on all his unfinished articles: the outline for a new article and the updated versions of previous articles.

cards.title: Articles workflow
cards.text: 3 week systemHave a weekly recurrent 15min stand-up for checking the articles statusEvery 2 weeks, review the backlog and decide on the topics and authors for the next articles that need to be written
cards.html:
3 week system

Have a weekly recurrent 15min stand-up for checking the articles status

Every 2 weeks, review the backlog and decide on the topics and authors for the next articles that need to be written

Once we have the final feedback from Mada, Raluca will handle the rest of the questions.

Q4 articles scheduling

cards.title: CS - support for Sales

cards.html:
CS helps with the existing processes during the Sales vacation; activities to be split between the CS team members:

  • Activity 1:
      1. Keep existing deal talks – leads, new client migrations, etc
      2. Take existing client requests for calls
  • Activity 2:
      1. Take lead demo requests (direct calendar bookings)
        1. Reactive, these leads ask for the call
        2. We updated Cris’ 45’ link to the CS team member link (but can make it 45’)
      2. Check (email) replies on these deals in HS dashboard (these)
  • Activity 3:
      1. Process ICP column in the Sales Pipeline (these, if list view) – in the AM
        1. [NEW] Also use “priority” (low, med, high) – for easier planning of daily activities → examples https://take.ms/jXrQt 
      2. Proactive → Is it worth pursuing? (3+ SEOs, ok site, big agency, etc.)
        1. Research them, then pitch (with your own 30’/60’ link)
          1. Check their FS - ok experience? If N/OK, report bugs and acknowledge the issues in the pitch. - For example Amplifyed.io, no FS
          2. Check their LinkedIn - do they know us from before? Use that in the pitch. - For example iCrossing, an old user
          3. If there is no relevant info, use a more generic pitch. - For example, mickeyllew.co.za - no site added
        2. Move to Outreach done

If anything comes up directly to Cristina’s Calendar, join directly and claim host (Host key 514983).

Documentation for October 2023

cards.title: CS Alerts
cards.text: Don’t assign them to you. Add an internal note, do what needs to be done, close them.Sheet 4GWT Access (GSCImpressionSplit / new parser) revoked for site: “site-name” (# site id - number )Check accoun
cards.html:
Don’t assign them to you. Add an internal note, do what needs to be done, close them.

Sheet 4

  1. GWT Access (GSCImpressionSplit / new parser) revoked for site: “site-name” (# site id - number )

    1. Check account for “site-id - number”

    2. Check Web settings

    3. Check GSC for the site or “site-id - number” (for many campaigns) if it’s connected

    4. If GSC is not found, send email to owner/user

    5. Add note with status in IC

    6. Close alert in IC

  2. GA Access revoked for site: “site-name” (# site id - number )

    1. For user “upswing” inform the person from “upswing”

    2. Close the alert in IC

    3. Check account for “site-id - number”

    4. Check Web settings

    5. Check GA for the URL site if any error or warning is shown

      1. Check the site - View settings - exact GA ID

    6. If GA is not found, send email to owner/user

    7. Add note with status in IC

    8. Close alert in IC

    9. If no “site-id - number” is found in app - check full story (it’s possible that the owner deleted the campaign)

  3. Best Topic Explorer DB different from tld for ”site id - number”

    1. Check account for “site-id - number”

    2. Check “Discover (Research)” for details of campaign configuration

      1. Just as double check: see keywords

    3. Check Full story to see how the owner set up the campaign

      1. If campaign set up global and should be localized: involve sales - send email with owner and site details and afterwards close the alert in IC

      2. If campaign set up correctly: close alert in IC

    4. Close the alert in IC

  4. Exact GA profile not found for SID:”number”

    1. Check account for “site-id - number”

    2. Check GA data

    3. Check URL site added by the client

    4. If GA and URL don’t match, send email/in app (message in IC) to client advising to connect the correct profile. Afterwards close the alert in IC

    5. Deleted?

  5. [ALERT] Brand / Non-brand change: “site-name” (# site id - number )

    1. Check account for “site-id - number”

    2. Check “Understand (Organic Traffic)” for a larger period of time (all keywords, Split Brand and Non-Brand view)

      1. Check Landing Page

      2. Check GA data for the exact dates of brand/non-brand switch dates

      3. If needed, also check GSC for the exact dates of brand/non-brand switch dates

      4. Connect GSC with Landing Page with unallocated traffic

      5. Add details in Brand/Non-brand document in Google Drive

      6. Close the alert in IC

        1. Go on All keywords in Organic Traffic.

        2. Split brand/non-brand.

        3. Scroll in the calendar all the way back to the switch(es).

        4. Switch on to LP view.

        5. Analyse what it looked like before and after the switch (check on one day, in both situations

  6. Re-engage GA connected wizard not finished

    1. Check account for “site-id - number”

    2. Bypass the system using “/my_account/subscription_details”

      1. Skip “connect GSC”

      2. Add competitors (1 or 2)

      3. Add keyworkds (from TE or best from site)

      4. Finish wizard

    3. Sent email to owner/user

    4. Close the alert in IC

  7. [Site in progress alert] Progress delayed 1 hour

    1. Check account for “site-id - number”

    2. Check “Understand (Organic Traffic)” for traffic

    3. Check “Manage (SEO Campaign)” for all kw data to be done

    4. If still in processing (either OT or SEO campaign) - create task

    5. Close the alert in IC

  8. SM App Subscription Error - Limits exceeded!

    1. Usually for accounts with set up limit

    2. Check account for “site-id - number”

    3. Check number of keywords used (in Settings and SEO Campaign)

    4. If the number is:

      1. A total of brand + non-brand, than it’s a fake error

      2. Exceeded: am email is sent to owner/user

  9. Cancel Subscription Reason for User : “e-mail address”

    1. Check details about campaign/user

    2. Sent an email to owner/user asking for feedback (if applicable offer/apply Promo Credits)

    3. Add note in IC

    4. Close the alert in IC

  10. Blocked Manage Site # site id - number - {"site_id":number, "data": ""}

    1. Check account for “site-id - number”

    2. Check “Understand (Organic Traffic)”

      1. If it’s an old site: add Trello task with check list, also add full story details -> add note with Trello task in IC

      2. If it’s a new site: no action is needed

    3. Close the alert in IC

  11. Hard bouncers

    1. Can be:

      1. Email address that are no longer valid

      2. Users that left the company. In this case sent an email to the owner

      3. Fake users. In this case send a message in app

    2. Close the alert in IC

  12. Failed payment or closed account due to exhausting the 5 attempts to pay an invoice (via slack).

a. Check the reason for failing, in Chargebee.

b. Only at the second failed payment alert, you write to the email address from the alert (or if we know some of the members working there - users that we had interaction via email or Intercom, we add their email as well), containing all the necessary info (cause of failed payment, when is next payment & reminder of automatically closing account at 5 failed attempts).

c. If the account got closed:

i. Write to them for feedback (check before if they left any comment in Chargebee and see from there on), ask if there is any feature missing (+ Product portal) & if they need help with exporting data from the account.

ii. If you need to reactivate the account, you email them to confirm the account is active, with the new/current invoice attached, to make sure they have it + if's the case, the new expected payment date (or until when the dunning was paused).

cards.title: CS negative feedback
cards.text: When the CS agent closes a conversation, an automatic feedback request is sent to the client. A simply Yes/No answer has to be given by clicking on it. After doing so, the client is redirected to eith
cards.html:
When the CS agent closes a conversation, an automatic feedback request is sent to the client. A simply Yes/No answer has to be given by clicking on it. After doing so, the client is redirected to either the positive or negative answer landing page and the answer is also visible in Intercom.

If the conversation received negative feedback, it's automatically reopened and the CS agent has to get back to the client and try to explain things better and change the No into a Yes, since after closing the conversation once again, the feedback request will be prompted again.

Usually, the feedback request is sent only once in 24h in the same conversation, even if it's been reopened during that time more than once. The only moment when this rue isn't applied, is when the conversation automatically opened after getting a No.

Conversations that are by Madalina & Raluca don't prompt for the feedback and only them can also add exceptions to other conversations by adding the "skip-feedback-conversation" tag.

All negative feedbacks received are sent directly to the Intercom Conversations QA sheet and then they're being processed during the team meeting.

Negative feedbacks are also available to the management team via email.

cards.text: Our working hours are 10:30–21:30, and they're based on Timetastic.  When Sulejman is off (since his shift is 13:00–22:00), our hours revert to 10:30–18:30. However, the SLA doesn’t account for this,
cards.html:
Our working hours are 10:30–21:30, and they're based on Timetastic.

When Sulejman is off (since his shift is 13:00–22:00), our hours revert to 10:30–18:30. However, the SLA doesn’t account for this, so some conversations may appear as out of SLA. Keep that in mind for the WBR slides.

Extra info: If integration@seomonitor.com is invited to a meeting, then every CS KPI will ignore whatever happens during that meeting and calculate time as if it didn’t happen at all. So feel free to invite it to the recurring meetings.

cards.title: Calling foreign numbers
cards.text: (for quicker support, financial aspects, etc.)Extra info in ​Users phone number.Double check to dial the number correctly, especially if they have a (0) after the country code, that might not be neces
cards.html:
(for quicker support, financial aspects, etc.)

Extra info in ​Users phone number.
Double check to dial the number correctly, especially if they have a (0) after the country code, that might not be necessary. For example, +61 (0)43 468 9002 whould be dialed as +61 43 468 9002.

Then:

  • if it's busy, we call back;

  • if the voicemail enters, we leave a message (including the name, company and issue at hand) and also call back;

  • if a gatekeeper answers (general/reception number), we try our best to get through to the right person;

  • if it disconnects right away, perhaps the number is wrongly dialled or has some typo, so double check (try Googling it even) and try again.

The aggregation system tries to solve the duplicated Search Volumes problem, that distort all data.
If the keywords have 0 SV:

  • they are harmless, don't distort nothing; they can only influence the costs (because they're still tracked for ranks)

cards.title: Can we influence Google Trends through rank tracking?
cards.text: We can't meaningfully influence Google Trends through rank tracking.  Here's why:  1. Scale mismatch: Google processes billions of searches per day. Even if SEOmonitor tracks hundreds of thousands of
cards.html:
We can't meaningfully influence Google Trends through rank tracking.

  Here's why:

  1. Scale mismatch: Google processes billions of searches per day. Even if SEOmonitor tracks hundreds of thousands of keywords daily, that's a rounding error in Google's total search volume. Google Trends shows relative interest normalized across all searches — our volume wouldn't move the needle.

  2. Google Trends filters bots: Google explicitly states they filter out "repeated queries from a single user over a short period of time" and automated/bot traffic from Trends data. Our rank tracking queries come through proxy infrastructure with patterns (systematic querying, no clicks, no follow-up searches) that are trivially identifiable as non-human.

  3. The queries aren't "real" searches: Our rank tracking goes through an internal proxy swarm (monse6-wg.internal) that rotates IPs and handles bans. Google is already well aware this is automated traffic — that's why they rate-limit and ban IPs. Traffic they're actively fighting isn't being counted in Trends.

 In summary, Google Trends data is not affected by rank tracking tools, and at most we could generate around 10 impressions per keyword.

cards.title: Can you filter country level traffic from GA?
cards.text: There are cases when the traffic from Analytics come from multiple search engines (.co.uk and .com), but the landing pages are the same for both search engines.This can at the moment be solved just by
cards.html:
There are cases when the traffic from Analytics come from multiple search engines (.co.uk and .com), but the landing pages are the same for both search engines.

This can at the moment be solved just by one of the below options:


1. client creates a new view, filtered on the UK - then reprocess it in the app - but it would only work from then on, not retroactively
2. select traffic filtering option during the wizard (so for new campaigns only) - can only be chosen if the campaign is localized
3. filtering the traffic from our side, through the tech team - yet localization is again a mandatory criterion

**there is a feature request with the Product team to remove localization as a condition for this process.

​Later edit, 8 Nov, Ana - in the new wizard the Localization option is set when GA and GSC profiles are connected, and only for the City localized campaigns.

cards.title: Cases when conversions/values from GA4 cannot be retrieved through the Google API
cards.text: There's a bug in GA4 API that prevents us from retrieving data for certain goals.Conversions/Values for events with names containing any characters other than "letters, numbers, or _" cannot be retrie
cards.html:
There's a bug in GA4 API that prevents us from retrieving data for certain goals.

Conversions/Values for events with names containing any characters other than "letters, numbers, or _" cannot be retrieved through the API as it throws out an error:

This was reported here more than a year ago here- https://support.google.com/analytics/thread/176551995/conversion-event-api-calls-should-use-event-id-not-name-sessionconversionrate-conversion-event-name?hl=en, and a few other times on stackoverflow, with no resolution for now.

Example from client's campaign:

The client had only the "Free Sign Up" event selected and due to the bug from GA4 API that prevents us from retrieving data for it he got no goals in the campaign.

Once the second goal was added: purchase, the data started coming through the Google API. As they don't have any purchase event conversions and no other events, keeping them all selected didn't affect the resulting data.

By keeping all of them means (both goals) we won't try to fetch individual event conversions:* metrics, but the conversions aggregated total metric, which works fine in the API.

cards.title: Chargebee & SMadmin subscription correlation
cards.text: Types of subscriptions:Prepaid CreditsMonthlyPrepaid creditsWe add "Prepaid credits" from the client's account (owner), in Admin.In case the user asks to top up the credit while still having previous
cards.html:
Types of subscriptions:

  • Prepaid Credits

  • Monthly

Prepaid credits

We add "Prepaid credits" from the client's account (owner), in Admin.

In case the user asks to top up the credit while still having previous credit left, the expected behaviour is for the entire amount to become unavailable until the new invoice gets paid.
This is because the system sums the two amounts (left credit+new credit) on the new invoice, deducts the existing credit and sets as amount to be paid the new credit. So everything that's credit becomes a whole and needs to be paid in full in order to be used.

Monthly

Monthly subscriptions must have the following ID form: “seomonitor-flexible-plan-uid-IDuser-currency”

Overages

In case the customer will need more resources that initially established, an overage plan will be created automatically. The calculation for the overage is as follows: Actual used resources - current plan set resources

Overages are automatically synchronized with the main subscription of the account. Yearly overages don't need to have the order ID set.

How do you know a subscription is set correctly?

  1. The corresponding order ID (which can be found in SMadmin) is set on the subscription plan in Chargebee

  2. The same currency is set both for the plan, the overage and the add-on

The order ID from SMadmin is very important since this is the one that helps the synchronization between the two platforms. If a client doesn't have it's order ID set for his subscriptions and doesn't pay, we won't be notified. And the same goes for them paying on time but the system won't see the payment and will transform the account into an expired one, according to the expiration date set in SMadmin.

Annual - NO LONGER RENEWABLE

Every customer that opts for a yearly subscription, will have the "Seomonitor subscription annual" plan in his Chargebee account. This is a predefined plan “template” for each type of currency selected by the customer with value 0.

When subscribing through the app, an automatic "Add-on" will be created for this subscription. This add-on contain the information about the set value for a special price or resources.

Annual add-ons must have this form: “yearly_[userID]_[number of website]_[number of kws]”

Add-ons can also be created manually, but only in exceptional cases, after having the Customer Service manager's approval.

    • https://app.squadcast.com/service-catalog

Additional, double-check that:

  • Timetastic synced with Google Calendar
  • Calendly/Hubspot understood the time off and no calls can be booked from anywhere

cards.title: Churn Process Documentation
cards.text: The Analysis Process Job-to-be-done: Help us understand how thorough was the investigation and what we can learn from losing the client, so we can change what needs to be changed, in order to not lose
cards.html:
The Analysis Process 

Job-to-be-done: Help us understand how thorough was the investigation and what we can learn from losing the client, so we can change what needs to be changed, in order to not lose clients again for the same reason.

Investigation part:

  • Check Chargebee for notes: - Is this an actual churn or delinquent?

    • Was the cancellation scheduled in advance? Did they leave any note?

    • Is it their first invoice (after the free trial)? Is there a valid payment method on file?

    • If not, what do you see in the last invoices? Do they have a pattern of paying after the dunning/NET30?

  • Look into the Onboarding process

    • Was there any follow up still pending?

    • Introduction demo call - purpose for using SEOmonitor, team details, other tools

    • What trainings have been conducted/were they based on their needs

    • Suitable training to the right people?

    • Was there a follow up for pulse checked

    • Did the team doing SEO change? If so, the new staff was trained?

    • Did we keep in contact with the CEO?

    • LinkedIn contacts and follow up (and/or bounced emails)

    • Has the company suffered any changes?

    • Is the business still running (strong)?

    • Are our main contacts / most engaged users still there?

    • Do the remaining ones have users in the platform? Have they been onboarded?

  • Intercom Conversations: - Did they communicate the need to cancel the subscription / Were there any hard feelings?

    • Check client status in Intercom - how many conv. / period of time

    • Issues mentioned in conversations: how many + subject, is it a new issue or recurring 

    • Feedback negative/positive: was it addressed?

    • Were there identified opportunities for training?

    • Were anticipated any future issues?

    • Support: How did we deliver the information to them?

    • Requests: What kind of needs did they have? Have we met them? How have we let them down, if needed?

  • FullStory : - Were they active in the platform in the last month(s)

    • Check active users

    • Main activities in the platform + time spent

    • Identify potential misuse of the application/opportunities for training

  • Sales Demo:

    • Was there any pre-sales interraction

    • Any specific feedback from the client → See Airtable with Demo feedback

    • Identify clues to what could have prevented the churn (bugs, issues)

Resurrection / Churn Follow up:

After the above investigation, can this client be ressurected? 

  • Send Call/e-mail

  • Start answering questions from churn document

  • Identify possible causes of churn: were these issues reasons for churn before?

  • Identify missed opportunities

→ Prepare the churn document for the client with the information obtained following the above guidelines, see here. And once confirmed for churn add into → Weekly Workshop file.

Then move client in Hubspot to Lost + reason: churn document mentioned above.

  • Prep for future to avoid making the same mistakes

Churn Pipeline

Process feedback:

  • Clear and concise points on the issues customers had (f.x. customer had “ranking” issues, but the real problem was the language we crawled in → keep perceived reason client-side, but add specifics after our understanding)

  • Specific number of days, no rounding/estimations for tasking resolutions

  • Actionable items at the end of each document

  • Draw patterns from the churns, if possible; example:

    • 3 clients churned with speed issues

    • if we have more customers currently with tasks on the same issue → alarm!

  • CS needs to properly understand what the problem is before raising the issue / submitting the task (Emperikal, as example, where it was only after Mada had a call with them, that we understood the issue)

    • Ask questions + get on call(s) asap, if needed + escalate if unclear

Need for internal tool updates/developments (DevOps)

  • In-conversation reply count → CS reply count, similar to what we had developed for the CS score (that measured the in-conversation reply time); count number of CS replies (between client replies)

  • Usersnap task generated → option to automatically create ticket via Intercom (button or smth) for Clickup submission right away, with specific fields (TBD with Engineering + DevOps + Product, in Maintenance process meetings)

    • As soon as a task needs to be submitted, click on the ticket button (and fill in afterwards). → possibility of alert if task left “empty” (TBC)

Future ideas:

  • A specific number of replies in conversations (up to a point), would trigger an alert/internal note → set up a call with the client! → f.x. You have 20 replies in 3 hrs, get on a call

  • Average reply count per conversation → potential for analysis in KPIs → benchmark

cards.title: Closing tickets guidelines
cards.text: Closing tickets - setting expectations in different types of conversationsBug that is blockerThe conversation will be closed only after the bug is fixed.Inform the client that the issue is being taken
cards.html:
Closing tickets - setting expectations in different types of conversations

  1. Bug that is blocker

The conversation will be closed only after the bug is fixed.

Inform the client that the issue is being taken care of.

We won’t snooze these conversations.

Since the issue is critical, it should be done in maximum 48h.

After the task has been moved to Verify, the agent checks it in maximum 1h (use Plecto for triggers).

15min automation in Intercom for verify tasks.

If ok, after moving it to Done, he/she contacts the client and asks for confirmation.

“It was fixed...

Please confirm”

Follow-up after 24h if there’s still no answer and inform them that we’re closing the ticket.

“I’m going to close this conversation. Let us know if you still encounter an issue on this by replying to this thread.”

If not ok, we move it back to “Backlog” and have to work on this with maximum priority. (in maximum 1h change after the maintenance process iteration meeting)

After done, recheck and go through the same previous steps.

  1. Bug with workaround

Definition of closed: only after the CS agents provides & makes sure the client knows the workaround.

Step 1: acknowledge the bug & communicate the workaround & request confirmation

Note: Set expectations that the bug was reported and tell them that we don’t have an estimation.

Step 2: Snooze the conversation for 1day

Never set the expectation that we’ll contact them after solving them. (warning)

Step 3.1: If we don’t have a confirmation/dismissal in 24h we’re going to inform them and to close the conversation.

Example:

“I’m going to close this conversation. Let us know if you still encounter an issue on this by replying to this thread.”

Step 3.2: If we receive the confirmation, we close the conversation.

  1. Product question coming

    1. from agencies

The conversation will be closed when Onboarding considers there's nothing more to be added to the answer & decide to close.

Step 1: Answer the questions.

Step 2: Assign the conversation to the Customer Success team and add a note (if necessary). Inform the client that we’re adding the Onboarding team in the thread.

“Because we have a dedicated team that deals with agencies, we’re also adding them to the thread.”

Step 3: (Onboarding) Add if they contribute to the answer or just confirm that the answer is complete. Mention: “If it’s still unclear we can book a call.”

Step 4: If no immediate answer, you snooze it for 1 day

Step 5.1: No answer: “I’m going to close this”

Step 5.2: If ok/books the call: close the ticket

Step 5.3: If the client continues the conversation with support-related questions, refer back and assign to Customer Service.

“I’m going to let the Customer Service team tackle this question since they’re more effective on this particular need”

    1. Product question with non-agency

The conversation will be closed when Customer Service considers there's nothing more to be added & decide to close.

After answering all the product question we close it.

"I'm going close this ticket for now. But if you have additional questions on this topic, please reopen this conversation."

  1. Feature request or missing features

The conversation will be closed when we've created a case (the request is crystal clear, including the usecase).

Don’t set any follow-up expectations.

Inform the client that we’ve taken the feedback request and added it to the Product’s backlog department and close the conversation of the spot.

  1. Sales inquiries with

    1. agency

The conversation will be closed when Sales considers there's nothing more to be added & decide to close.

CS informs Cristina about the inquiry by tagging her. CS doesn’t answer any financial/contract-related questions.

But CS does answer to product/bugs questions coming from leads.

Should we directly assign the conversation to her?

    1. Sales inquiries with non-agency

The conversation will be closed when Customer Service considers there's nothing more to be added & decide to close.

CS deals through and through with all the questions and requests coming from non-agencies leads.

Are non SEO agencies considered non-agencies?

  1. Financial & other

The conversation will be closed when the CS Manager considers it was handled & should be closed.

Tag all financial conversations in Intercom with the “financial” tag.

  1. If the client has a request that doesn’t need escalation (asks for invoices, of has questions about the subscription, etc) the agent solves the conversation as she sees fit and closes it informing.

“I'm going close this ticket for now. But if you have additional questions on this topic, please reopen this conversation."

  1. If the conversation needs escalation

    1. the CS Manager will be tagged in the conversation and will either guide the CS agent with the next steps or will take over.

The Manager must answer in maximum 12h.

    1. If the Manager has to take over and finalize the conversation, the client will be informed about this aspect.

“I’ve escalated the issue to our manager and she will get back to you with more details.”

The Manager must answer in maximum 12h.

  1. Spam

The conversation will be closed after blocking the contact.

Block the contact and directly close the conversation. No message is needed.

  1. Migrations

The conversation will be closed after finalizing the migration

Following-up

2 types of follow-ups:

  1. During the conversations

    1. Waiting for info from the client

      1. For adding a task - 1st follow-up after 24h. If after 4days we don’t receive any answers, we close it.

      2. For a task that has been solved and can only be tested by the client - use the workaround procedure.

    2. Waiting for internal info

      1. Technical questions - get the answer in max 24h

      2. Info from product - same as technical question

  2. After closing the conversations

      1. If there was a task and we mentioned we’ll inform them. These conversations should be snoozed and look after them closely in Clickup

KPI resolved conversation

KPI: % resolved conversations out of total/day

Resolved conversation = a conversation that has been closed and the feedback request has been sent to the client (in conversations and via email).

  • Know the duration of a resolved conversation, max 7days

  • Tag the conversation as solved in IC after the feedback form is sent

How to calculate: start of the conversation/reopened conversation -> feedback form is sent and conversation is closed

KPI closing time

KPI: avg closing time/day

How to calculate: open conversation - sent feedback form in max 7days

Notes & decisions

Tasks that have been moved to Verify should be checked in max 24h

3 days seen after closing option - is it working?

Flowcharts

https://drive.google.com/drive/u/0/folders/1QVetPsA6wAhMYYmy2ECeSWQY2N2wbwzX

cards.title: Competition Insights data - competitors' keywords
cards.text: The data for the non-brand keywords of the competitors comes from:our database, for the markets where we have Topic Explorer (UK, US, TR, RO)although not publicly marketed on the site or in the app, f
cards.html:
The data for the non-brand keywords of the competitors comes from:

  • our database, for the markets where we have Topic Explorer (UK, US, TR, RO)

  • although not publicly marketed on the site or in the app, for the markets where we don't have a database of our own, we take data from SEMrush (for the customer it's clickstream data)

The estimated visits we show for the competitors are calculated taking into consideration the search volume in the selected period and the daily rank (Click-Through-Rate), as indicated in the tool tip (http://take.ms/XmD45).

Also, this data is crawled daily.

cards.title: Competitors - always added
cards.text: Vali confirmed on a dev-app thread that there is a rule, for some time, that states that there can't be a folder without competitors. If competitors are removed, at least 1 has to be kept to avoid the
cards.html:
Vali confirmed on a dev-app thread that there is a rule, for some time, that states that there can't be a folder without competitors.

If competitors are removed, at least 1 has to be kept to avoid the automatic addition of them.

cards.title: Competitors in weekly report
cards.text: All competitors available in the app in Competition section will be displayed in the report, first the starred ones followed by the rest.
cards.html: All competitors available in the app in Competition section will be displayed in the report, first the starred ones followed by the rest.

cards.title: Competitors limit - why do we have it?
cards.text: You can only add 10 competitors per group, this is limited just to keep the interface usable (speed & general user experience) however, even if you can see only 10 competitors at a time, you can alway
cards.html:
You can only add 10 competitors per group, this is limited just to keep the interface usable (speed & general user experience) however, even if you can see only 10 competitors at a time, you can always change them for your analysis.

Therefore you can add 10 competitors at a time but there's no actual limit as you can remove and add others at all times.

A workaround for customers is to create a smart group with ranks >99 or ranks >10 <11 (with the nominator "OR") and add the competitors there.
﻿Or create a manual group that includes all the keywords from "All Keywords" you can name it differently (the entire portfolio or something like that) and add 10 competitors in the main one and 10 in the other one (you can do this several times if needed).
﻿
﻿Even if you have the same keywords in several groups, they will not be counted twice to your billing, so you can split them this way as the 10 competitors limit it per group, not for all the groups/smart groups.

This happens for new users or new campaigns added from now on (today is the 11th of May 2021).
The reason for this is that the section will be deprecated in a couple of months and completely rebuilt at some point.

Meanwhile, if old users complain about not seeing the Content Tab (due to them adding new campaigns or new users to old accounts) we can manually install it for them. If it's already installed for other campaigns, uninstall it and install it again.
This action can be done by us with masterpass only.

Also, we don't install it anymore for new accounts until further notice.

  • Admin

  • Feedback

  • Downtime

cards.title: Conversations
cards.text: Responsible: CS peeps, Mada (only if needed)Process: Sheet 3Helpful for the priority of the issue: bugs/features priority systemFirst reply:as soon as possible. Keep it under 15’ during the workdays!s
cards.html:
Responsible: CS peeps, Mada (only if needed)

Process: Sheet 3

Helpful for the priority of the issue: bugs/features priority system

  • First reply:
    • as soon as possible. Keep it under 15’ during the workdays!
    • should ideally include an article from the knowledge base, if relevant.
    • should be more than just a canned "I'm checking"; try to build confidence that you understood and set the right expectations or get more info, if not.
  • If it’s something/someone you want to handle, assign the conversation to yourself asap. Then handle it asap. :D

  • Double-check the questions and/or reported issues, if confidence level not at 100%. Always answer the specific question(s).
    • (if relevant, check Fin's answer and the sources provided there)
    • check public resources further: Help.SEOmonitor.com and even seomonitor.com
      • check internal resources: Guru cards, Clickup (documentation and even tasks), even Slack now (with history)
    • if needed, check within the team;
      • if still needed, check with Lead (Mada);
    • for long-term confirmation, you can go on #technical-questions, and/or the Product Backlog meeting (via Mada)

  • Replies within the conversation:
    • when waiting for info from the client, confirm receiving it (asap) and then continue handling it accordingly;
    • offer workarounds, whenever possible;
    • win time wisely, if needed;
    • no SID / campaign name questions (use un-ambiguous words, no internal terms, etc)
    • use any and all available info (other tools included)
    • if they replied just to say thanks or confirm ok on a previously closed conversation, you still need to reply and close again (you can offer additional help, and then close).

  • Escalate: send the team & Lead a message about it on Slack (CS Peeps, Customer Success, or even Revenue, accordingly)
    • any recurrent issues (a few in one day or over a couple of days);
    • data-accuracy issues, especially ranking issues (if diagnosed as such);
    • crises (f.x. frustrated clients).

  • Snooze conversation if you’re pending additional info:
    • from the client, or
    • for the client.
    • snooze with an internal note on reasoning, in case someone else needs to followup.

  • Use internal notes confidently so everyone has the info and the already-done checks at hand, if needed:
    • Clickup task link(s),
    • screenshots or platform links, that you've investigated & are on a good lead,
    • Slack link(s) (or screenshot, f.x. asked on Slack, pending X),
    • updates from devs on tasks (i.e. still pending X, delayed because of Y),
    • even notes for other team members (i.e. for Success or Sales)

  • Only close conversations if you:
    • have provided a solution,
    • it’s a feature request that’s gonna be done sometimes in the future, with Clickup task link (as internal note) and the conversation/client info in the task.
    • Otherwise, leave them open... and feel the time pressure! :))
    • Note: they will get the "Have we solved your problem? Yes/No" question.

cards.text: This can only be manually added because without a Google Analytics profile, we don't have the conversions information.If they ask how to calculate it:The conversion rate represents the total number of

cards.text: create customersee all here name, email, phone (!), addressVAT numberdefault currency: EUR!!! don't forget to add billing infooptional: additional billing contactcreate plan(might not be needed - as t
cards.html:
create customer

  • see all here
    • name, email, phone (!), address
    • VAT number
    • default currency: EUR
    • !!! don't forget to add billing info
    • optional: additional billing contact

create plan

  • (might not be needed - as the plan is usually created automatically - but search for it, to check)
    • name: SEOmonitor Flexible Plan UID XXXXX CURRENCY
      • UID: from smAdmin
      • current MRR: from SEOm
      • currency: (if blank, the default is) EUR; if requested, use USD or GBP

(optional) create coupon

  • for the value you want covered on the first invoice
    • extra details here and here
  • or the 17% off prepaid (annual) one
    • get UserID (from smadmin, see above @ Plan)
    • get timestamp through here
    • fill in details here

create subscription ON the customer

  • see all details here
    • add/find the plan by UID
    • (if applicable) add the coupon
    • add the SEOm Order ID from SEOm admin
  • note: if for an upcoming Prepaid setup
    • select "add to unbilled charges"
    • then remove them
    • it will have the "no invoice for current term" warning at the top
    • extra details on related card here: Prepaid payments - useful info
  • if unbilled present --> make sure the start date is correct (it CAN be set on a past date!)

New: Prepaid invoice via SmAdmin

Take this into account: The option to create prepaid does not work - new ChargeBee account

(Extra) switching the currency

  • If you need to switch the currency on the subscription, f.x. you created it in EUR, but they need it in USD.
    • you also need to create the corresponding plan, with USD at the end (see above, in PLAN) - not always necessary. The tool should automatically create one upon changing the currency in Admin.
    • create a new subscription, in USD, with the new USD plan
    • remove SEOm order id from the old subscription
    • add it to the new one
    • cancel old (EUR) subscription
    • change the currency in ChargeBee, on Edit Customer
  • the customer's subscriptions would now look like this

*Dedicated card here How to change subscription currency

(Extra) paying via card directly

  • create the Customer (see above)
  • then Request payment method & wait for them to add it
  • (once the card is on file), create the Subscription (see above, on the prepaid setup note)
    • note: you can create the Subscription before sending in the Payment method update request.
  • in smAdmin --> on the client --> Prepaid, go and Collect Now
    • double-check the invoice in Chargebee --> should be PAID
    • when it's paid, credits are added automatically --> pls check on the Customer in CB
  • on the Subscription --> "regenerate invoice now" for the current term --> to get the 1st monthly invoice on credits
    • check that the invoice is issued, paid, on 0.
    • check that the next invoicing date is correct.

cards.title: Criteria for predefined groups "exploding" and "tanking"
cards.text: Exploading & Tanking are smart groups automatically created if the option is selected in Wizard, and can be found under the Search Shifts folder.The criteria behind them is as follows:Exploding keywor
cards.html:
Exploading & Tanking are smart groups automatically created if the option is selected in Wizard, and can be found under the Search Shifts folder.

The criteria behind them is as follows:

Exploding keywords - keywords that more than doubled their Search Volume year-over-year

Tanking keywords - keywords that decreased by more than half in Search Volume year-over-year

cards.title: Customer Service Intercom communication guidelines (v 1)
cards.text: How do stakeholders communicate errors in established guidelines when handling Intercom conversations?We use Intercom notes to communicate the observation adding the link to the Guru card; if Guru car
cards.html:
How do stakeholders communicate errors in established guidelines when handling Intercom conversations?

  • We use Intercom notes to communicate the observation adding the link to the Guru card; if Guru card doesn't exist, that's the observation.

  • The Customer Service manager is always tagged in the note.

  • We never attack the person, we always focus on the process that wasn't followed as agreed upon.

  • The Customer Service manager always replies in the conversation with the action that was taken to mitigate the specific error within 7 days.

  • Customer Service agents never reply to these notes.

  • This process should not be a blocker for resolving the ticket. Tackling the client's problem continues in other internal notes.

  • All the tickets that had issues on processes, will be tagged by the Customer Service manager with the "process improvement" tag.

  • An automated solution for identifying unfinished issues will be developed

How do stakeholders communicate errors when handling Intercom conversations without established guidelines?

  • We use Intercom notes to communicate the observation and that we need to agree on how we should manage this better next time.

  • The Customer Service manager is always tagged in the note.

  • That Customer Service manager always replies in the conversation confirming that she added the talking point in the management meeting agenda within 7 days.

  • This process should not be a blocker for resolving the ticket. Tackling the client's problem continues in other internal notes.

When do we snooze?

  • Only when we're waiting for dev input on tasks.

  • Before snoozing, we add an internal note with what we're waiting for.

  • Never snooze for more than 3 days at a time.

  • If I need to snooze the conversation again, we add an internal note.

When do we close Intercom conversations?

  • When we are 100% sure that the client would answer YES to "Did we solve you problem?" question. The client is the person that opened the ticket.

  • If we know that the answer will be NO, the conversation will be assigned to the Customer Service manager and closed by her. This will bypass the automatic reopening.

  • We automatically gather the answer to the "Did we solve you problem?" question.

  • If the answer is NO, the conversation is automatically reopened and the Customer Service manager will be automatically tagged.

  • For any conversation that takes longer than 7 days, this will be handled and closed by the Customer Service manager and a note with the action on how we should manage this better next time.

Closed tickets:

  • bug that is blocker = FIXED

  • bug with workaround = provide & make sure the client knows the workaround + set expectations that the bug was reported, but it's not prioritized

  • product question with agency = when Onboarding considers there's nothing more to be added to the answer & decide to close

  • product question with non-agency = when Service considers there's nothing more to be added & decide to close

  • feat. req. or missing features = when we've created a case (the request is crystal clear, including the usecase) and we set the expectations (see below)

  • sales inquiries with agency = when Sales considers there's nothing more to be added & decide to close

  • sales inquiries with non-agency = when Service considers there's nothing more to be added & decide to close

  • financial & other = when CS Manager considers it was handled & should be closed

  • spam = blocked + closed immediately by CS

Set the expectation that this ticket ended. Close with "I'm gonna close this ticket for now. If you have additional questions, please reopen this conversation."

Kinds of tickets:

  • bugs

  • with workaround (not a blocker) = give workaround + task

  • no workaround (blocker) = wait for resolution

  • product questions

  • agency: answer + reassign to Onboarding

  • non-agency: answer

  • feature requests or missing features

  • make sure to understand the request

  • get the usecase

  • set the expectation: the info has been sent to Product; after/if they implement it, we'll let them know; it's not going to be in the near future, we work on a few things, decided on a quarterly basis.

  • sales inquiries

  • agency: answer + reassign to Sales

  • non-agency: answer

  • financial & other

  • escalate to CS Manager

  • SPAM/junk

  • everything commercial

  • without existing relationship (user).

What should the maximum close time be?

  • 7 days between Open / Reopen - Close

  • After 6 days the Customer Service manager and agent should be automatically tagged in an internal note with a heads up that the conversation will be overdue in 1 day. If this is the case, the client needs to be informed that it's taking longer than usual and the reason for it.

  • This will be measured and reported in 15five and integrated in CS Score

How do we know when some emails received in IC are spam and what do we do with them?

cards.title: Customer Service Intercom communication guidelines (v 2)
cards.text: How do stakeholders communicate errors in established guidelines when handling Intercom conversations?We use Intercom notes to communicate the observation adding the link to the Guru card; if Guru car
cards.html:
How do stakeholders communicate errors in established guidelines when handling Intercom conversations?

  • We use Intercom notes to communicate the observation adding the link to the Guru card; if Guru card doesn't exist, that's the observation.

  • The Customer Service manager is always tagged in the note.

  • We never attack the person, we always focus on the process that wasn't followed as agreed upon.

  • The Customer Service manager always replies in the conversation with the action that was taken to mitigate the specific error within 7 days.

  • Customer Service agents never reply to these notes.

  • This process should not be a blocker for resolving the ticket. Tackling the client's problem continues in other internal notes.

  • All the tickets that had issues on processes, will be tagged by the Customer Service manager with the "process improvement" tag.

  • An automated solution for identifying unfinished issues will be developed

How do stakeholders communicate errors when handling Intercom conversations without established guidelines?

  • We use Intercom notes to communicate the observation and that we need to agree on how we should manage this better next time.

  • The Customer Service manager is always tagged in the note.

  • That Customer Service manager always replies in the conversation confirming that she added the talking point in the management meeting agenda within 7 days.

  • This process should not be a blocker for resolving the ticket. Tackling the client's problem continues in other internal notes.

All commercial conversations should go to the CS lead (Ana).

  • Pricing,
  • Renewals,
  • Paid add-ons (such as Content Writer),
  • Contract Terms & Additional Paperwork,
  • Significant change of plans (like the case of “splitting out” one account into several),
  • M&As

  • etc.

*for now, the criteria is MRR >= 500.

Hand over with something along the lines of:

Please let me loop in our Customer Success team, and they’ll provide all details and followup directly.

cards.title: C​an a user have the data in Data Studio, in the same table (let's say in 2 columns), from 2 different sources (SEOmonitor and GA), using API?

cards.html:
Some sort of data blending, or being able to show the ranking data alongside google analytics data

The data blending in Data Studio can be done only if the 2 sources of the table have a common field (keyword_id for example). As the source for Google Analytics is different to the source for SEOmonitor data, the user cannot have the same table, in Data Studio, with different columns of data.

cards.text: In the details of the parameters of this endpoint, the Daily Visibility for groups is shown.To have the data for a specific group, when the connector is used, the name of the group has to be added the
cards.html:
In the details of the parameters of this endpoint, the Daily Visibility for groups is shown.

To have the data for a specific group, when the connector is used, the name of the group has to be added there (https://share.getcloudapp.com/yAuYr6Dz), in the Group name field and afterward to proceed in creating the report.

Knowing for which group the report is made is up to the user.

The explanation, design of it,  when the connector is made, the group name is added, we find that specific group by name, take the ID and add it to the call that brings the data. All the data/metrics from there (in the report) are for a specific group (or All keywords if the group name is left blank).

Daca autorizezi un connector ii autorizezi pe toti, daca ii dai remove access din google account, le dai la toti remove.

best practice este sa ii dai revoke access din data studio. rapoartele care foloseau data sources create cu acel connector nu vor mai functiona. daca dai din nou pe el, nu o sa mai trebuiasca sa il conectezi cu accountul dar va trebui sa ii bagi api key.

cards.title: Data Studio - Why is there a 1K lines limit
cards.text: Our APIs have certian limitations. We cannot request the database to bring 12K keywords at once, for example, because it will crash, so we have a limit of 1K keywords per call. But that's not of any c
cards.html:
Our APIs have certian limitations. We cannot request the database to bring 12K keywords at once, for example, because it will crash, so we have a limit of 1K keywords per call.

But that's not of any concern for the user, because the Connector brings all the data eventually, just not everything at once. It first requests 1K, then the next 1K and so on till bringing everything.

This happens while the user sees the table as loading on their side. When the processing done, they will be provided with the full set of data.

cards.text: When you create a report, Data Studio pulls in through the connectors all the campaigns it can see at that specific moment.If you then add additional campaigns but use the same report created prior, t
cards.html:
When you create a report, Data Studio pulls in through the connectors all the campaigns it can see at that specific moment.

If you then add additional campaigns but use the same report created prior, there's a process to follow in order to somehow "refresh" the report and reconnect to it.

This action can ONLY be done by the person that created the report in the first place.

STEPS TO FOLLOW: Edit Data Source > Edit Connection > Reconnect > Allow > Go to fields > Done > search the campaign in the Campaign (Parameter) drop-down field
Please check this video: https://monosnap.com/file/g5hJlmTf5huE7kjrfezv6pysxydqwj

Later edit 10.03.2021: the above steps to follow to reconnect the DataSource also apply to the Competition connector when they appear with www. in front (Andrei made some changes and if they have www. in front they won't be pulled in unless you reconnect the datasource)

cards.title: Detection of external links
cards.text: How do we crawl/auto-detect external links directed to our domain? What is the process behind it?The platform detects all articles that have a link to your website, starting 30 days prior to when you
cards.html:
How do we crawl/auto-detect external links directed to our domain? What is the process behind it?

The platform detects all articles that have a link to your website, starting 30 days prior to when you added the website. This process is done once a week, having Ahrefs as a source.

If they are older than that, they should be manually added.

There are certain rules that are taken into consideration:

- the backlink mustn't be in the comment or in the description;

- the social signal must be over zero

Only when they are met does the app recognize the article.

If the ‘bubble’ is red (http://take.ms/zqdHA), you need to go check the list here.

  1. Go to the Keyword page - http://take.ms/SwU1C.

  2. Download SERP from the 99+ device - http://take.ms/4j1tg.

  3. Search for the domain you are tracking, but disregard if it’s only in ads, videos, etc. - http://take.ms/uNJxY.

    1. If found (or the SERP page looks odd) >> Report to DEV >> sends task in Trello. http://take.ms/RVlVP >> add the SERP page to the task + add yourself on the task.

When all are checked, the ‘bubble’ turns green. And it should always be so. :)

*Note: If there are a lot of discrepancies on the same campaign, talk to Dragos directly, as it might be a bigger problem.

Always check the bubble and the page, so that they won't pile up!

cards.title: Different search volumes
cards.text: SEOmonitor search volumes are correlated with the ones from AdWords' Keyword Planner, so they should coincide.The internal database (used for Topic Explorer and the platform, in places) is updated on
cards.html:
SEOmonitor search volumes are correlated with the ones from AdWords' Keyword Planner, so they should coincide.

The internal database (used for Topic Explorer and the platform, in places) is updated on a monthly basis, while in SEOmonitor, we would have the latest available search volume from AdWords, as it is on-demand.

  • Update-ul de AdWords se intampla lunar, incepand cu 24 si dureaza undeva intre 1 si 15 zile, in functie de cand primim cele mai noi date de la Google AdWords despre cuvintele cerute.

Understand shows TE data base (conditiile sunt timeframe mai mare de 2 luni si monthly view - http://take.ms/VUUGn ) Research shows adwords data. Words with N/A are considered less relevant in our TE DB, so you can only check in Research - http://take.ms/G4KLU)

Significant differences can come from other reasons as well:

  1. a localized campaign would show the local search volume in the platform, but the country-level one in Topic Explorer and the Competition Insights magnifying glass

  2. a campaign set on "global" in the platform would have the US search volume in Topic Explorer.

*Note: The localisation-based differences also applies to Keyword Planner - thus, make sure that, when in KwP, you apply the same search variable as in our app (localised search)

  • the search volume in the upper part of the page is for main keywords only

cards.title: Do GSC's clicks come from the paid results as well?
cards.text: NO. GSC is not registering the paid clicks.Clicks = the number of clicks on your website URLs from a Google Search results page, not including clicks on paid Google Ads search results.Impressions: The
cards.html:
NO. GSC is not registering the paid clicks.

Clicks = the number of clicks on your website URLs from a Google Search results page, not including clicks on paid Google Ads search results.

Impressions: The number of times any URL from your site appeared in search results viewed by a user, not including paid Google Ads search impressions.

cards.title: Does SEOmonitor support the use of advanced search operators as keywords? For example, if my keyword was 'site:apple.co.uk consulting services'.
cards.text: SEOmonitor, by design, already does this, automatically, for any keyword that is not yet ranked in top 100. So anytime you see a keyword with 99+, the landing page mentioned for it, is the closest mat
cards.html:
SEOmonitor, by design, already does this, automatically, for any keyword that is not yet ranked in top 100. So anytime you see a keyword with 99+, the landing page mentioned for it, is the closest match for that search, according to Google.

The app even highlights the keywords where no relevant page is detected, beyond top 100, and they can be filtered when no LP is returned: advance filters (or smart group) with 'Warnings' - 'No relevant landing page'.

cards.title: Domain/subdomain/folder rank tracking in SEOmonitor
cards.text: If you track the main domain and select to track subdomains as well, it will take BOTH into consideration and provide the best rank between the two.If you track the subdomain, it will ONLY take the ra
cards.html:
1. If you track the main domain and select to track subdomains as well, it will take BOTH into consideration and provide the best rank between the two.
  2. If you track the subdomain, it will ONLY take the ranks for that subdomain.
  3. If you track a folder/subfolder it will ONLY take the ranks for that folder/subfolder. (as of September 2024)

For more details regarding tracking subdirectories, check this card: How can I monitor the subdirectories of a website?


EXTRA INFO:
From a traffic perspective, they can also connect the Google profiles (GSC&GA) for the folder and have the folder-only distribution of traffic.

cards.title: Downloading SERPs not working for campaigns with changed URL
cards.text: If the campaign has changed URLs, and the new URL is ranking on those days you request the SERP download, the SERP download validation fails (due to the SERP having a different rank for the campaign U
cards.html:
If the campaign has changed URLs, and the new URL is ranking on those days you request the SERP download, the SERP download validation fails (due to the SERP having a different rank for the campaign URL than the stats).

[internal info only] You can download the SERPS from the admin instead, as there's no validation there.

The customer cannot download them while the stats rank does not match the rank we detect on the SERP at the moment of download, and that is by design.

Related task: https://app.clickup.com/t/8696ngkch

cards.title: Email reports - what is the reference for their timeframe?
cards.text: For weekly reports the week day selected for receiving the report is also the reference for calculating the week's timeframe (day_of_reporting -1 day, day_of_reporting -8 days)For monthly reports it's
cards.html:
For weekly reports the week day selected for receiving the report is also the reference for calculating the week's timeframe (day_of_reporting -1 day, day_of_reporting -8 days)

For monthly reports it's always the calendar month, irrespective of the day you selected for receiving it.

cards.title: Estimated additional traffic - Landing Page Opportunity vs Keyword Opportunity
cards.text: Keyword Opportunity formula opportunity = est_traffic/difficultyest_traffic = (CTR[1-3] - CTR[current_rank]) * AvgSearchVolume * YoY * %ClicksOnOrganicLanding Page OpportunityLP_opportunity_new = sum(
cards.html:
Keyword Opportunity formula

opportunity = est_traffic/difficulty

est_traffic = (CTR[1-3] - CTR[current_rank]) * AvgSearchVolume * YoY * %ClicksOnOrganic

Landing Page Opportunity

LP_opportunity_new = sum(kws_opp_new) * nr days in period​
kw_opp_new, is not the 0 si 10 opportunity, it's an intermediate value used to calculate that score

LP_est_traffic = LP_opportunity_new * LP_difficulty_new

Example
For https://wordfinder.yourdictionary.com/scrabble-dictionary/ there are 2 keywords that are ranking​
scrabble dictionary - (id=5649985) -> opp_new = "5793.3"
scrabble helper - (id=5649983) -> opp_new = “0”
nr days in period = 28
LP_opportunity_new = (5793.3 + 0) * 28 = 162212,4
LP_difficulty_new = 1
so
LP_est_traffic =162212,4

Code details (internal use only) https://docs.google.com/document/d/1JE5ZFwnRNkagpWOUG0qTPY1K5yh5YZclJQYpqr4r-Nk/edit?usp=sharing
