cards.title: Fake Search Volumes algorithm
cards.text: Se analizeaza cuvantul hote, pentru SID=118751. se face un call la adwords pt sugestiile “hote”, cu parametru de locatie (in caz ca acea campanie este localizata)2. callul de mai sus o sa returneze ur
cards.html:
Se analizeaza cuvantul hote, pentru SID=11875

1. se face un call la adwords pt sugestiile “hote”, cu parametru de locatie (in caz ca acea campanie este localizata)

2. callul de mai sus o sa returneze urmatoarele rezultate, ca si sugestii

	        - hote in english                - hotera 	        - hotwire deutsch	        - mote hote	        - red hote

3. mai departe, cu primul cuvant din lista de mai sus, se face acelasi call de mai sus, la adwords, in care se cer sugestiile, in cazul nostru, cerem sugestiile pentru hote in english

4. call-ul de mai sus o sa returneze urmatoarele rezultate:

	- hotels	- hote meaning in english	- sab ek jaise nahi hote in english	- kash aap yahan hote in english	- jhoot k paon nahi hote in english	- jhoot ke paon nahi hote in english	- hote english	- jhoot ke pair nahi hote in english

  • pentru cele doua call-uri de mai sus, pe langa sugestii pentru fiecare cuvant in parte, adwords o sa returneze si search trend-ul pe 1 an

5. se ia search trendul pentru cuvantul analizat, adica pentru `hote`

6. se analizeaza search trendul fiecarui cuvant returnat din ultimul call de la adwords

  • daca se gaseste un trend al oricarui cuvant returnat la pct. 4 care sa fie acelasi pe minim 9 luni cu trendul cuvantului initial analizat hote, atunci acest cuvant este analizat mai departe.
    In cazul nostru, am identificat cuvantul “hotels”

7. Sunt analizate cuvintele hote si hotels

  • 7.1 Se identifica serp-ul fiecarui cuvant.

  • 7.2 Se analizeaza doar primele 20 rezultate din serp

  • 7.3 Daca serp-ul lui hote difera fata de serp-ul lui hotels, in proportie mai mica de 20%, atunci:

    • 7.3.1 hote este marcat ca avand “fake search volume”

    • 7.3.2 hote va avea suprascris search trend-ul istoric, pe toate lunile, cu search-ul volume-ul actual.

    • 7.3.3 in interfata, pe coloana de search volume, in locul trendului o sa ii apara un warning care va avea urmatorul tooltip:

	We have identified that this keyword is now reported in AdWords as being aggregated under hotels, which gives it the wrong value: 60,500 monthly searches. We have, in turn, proceeded with giving it another AdWords-based value: the number of monthly impressions that it would get, if you would place the maximum bid on it.

Unde 60,500 monthly searches este search volume-ul cuvantului “hotels”

  • ce inseamna asta: adwords “vede” ca “hote” are acelasi search volume cu “hotels”

  • doar ca cele doua cuvinte inseamna cu totul altceva

  • cu acest algoritm, incercam sa nu atribuim search volume-ul de la hotels catre hote

cards.html:
This filtering option will return a list of keywords that achieved their best rank ever on the last day selected in the calendar.

The pool of keywords taken into consideration will be the one available in the selected time frame, and the displayed rank will be the one corresponding to the last day selected in the calendar.


Keywords will get the Best Rank label every time they reach their best rank, provided that it’s the highest position they ever reached, and they still maintain it on the last day selected in the calendar.

cards.title: Forecast: traffic data shown should be a match to GA traffic data?
cards.text: No, the traffic data in the Forecast is an estimation based on the last 30 days' traffic and Search Volume. If you are doing the forecast in the middle of the month, we estimate the inertial traffic f
cards.html:
No, the traffic data in the Forecast is an estimation based on the last 30 days' traffic and Search Volume.

If you are doing the forecast in the middle of the month, we estimate the inertial traffic for the first month using that data.

More precisely:

  • we take the traffic from the current month and the traffic for the last 30 days
  • and we extrapolate the traffic for the current month by using the number of days left from the current month and the percentage of volume for this month and the previous month.

cards.title: From Annual to Prepaid credits
cards.text: Starting June 2020, instead of offering annual subscriptions with a certain number of resources, we'll be switching to a more flexible way of billing. We'll be using the prepaid credits option.This ap
cards.html:
Starting June 2020, instead of offering annual subscriptions with a certain number of resources, we'll be switching to a more flexible way of billing. We'll be using the prepaid credits option.

This applies to all the existing clients with annual subscriptions (that we're now in the course of migrating to flexible - please see below the email we're using to inform them about this) but also for new clients that want to pay upfront for at least 10x current MRR.

So if we have a client that has a MRR of 500 EUR, he'll be paying 5000 EUR for 12 months. We're be including the 2 months free (17% off).

The client will be paying one 5000EUR invoice each 12 months (in case his resource consumption doesn't change at all) which will be transformed into prepaid credits added to his account.

Afterwards, each month a 0 EUR invoice will also be created. This is actually a normal monthly flexible invoice (where the current used resources are being shown) paid through the prepaid credits that the client has in his account.

The clients will be invoiced automatically when the credits left in their account won't cover the next full month of resources.

We'll only be accepting NET30.

[Switching to prepaid email example]


Hello,
I'm happy to announce that we've migrated your annual subscription to a more simple credit system.
From now on, when you pay in advance, you are credited to your SEOmonitor account and get to have full monthly flexibility on these prepaid credits, all the while keeping the annual discount.

Prior to this, you had a fixed plan of €34500/year (2 months free included). This meant that if you were using fewer resources than the monthly "allowance", you wouldn't get the benefit of a lower price as well. And if you ever needed an upgrade, we would have issued an invoice each month for these extra resources.

We believe in transparency and flexibility at all times, so we had to solve these 2 issues: paying for unused resources and a complexity generated by monthly overages.

Our standard pricing model tackles this by default: you only pay for what you use.

There's no further action needed from your side! We handled it all and this is what happened in the background today:

  • We invoiced you for the same annual amount - see invoice APPE-13576 attached.

  • We added this as credits to your account - you can already see them in the Billing details - https://take.ms/0tKNL.

  • Starting Tuesday (when the renewal was due), you will be receiving monthly invoices paid out of these credits (so they will be at 0, while the credits last).

  • These monthly invoices will include the 17% discount, to account for the upfront annual discount.

Note that you are currently using resources of €1053/month, which means that the credits would last over 12 months. Given this, if you would like to adjust this prepaid amount accordingly, we can reissue the invoice. Just let us know!
If you need to set a monthly limit, you can do so through the Cost Control option in the same Billing menu.

Thanks for taking the time to read this! 

We're here if you have any questions (in-app chat, email, call - whatever works best for you).

All the best,


Extra info (INTERNAL ONLY): Once they are swapped to prepaid, users will receive a notification that their old subscription has ended.
In order to check if they're now on prepaid, open the invoice and if it says prepaid credits and next invoice is 0, they're on prepaid

cards.title: FullStory tracking as an admin (master password)
cards.text: You need to first install a Chrome Extension - TamperMonkey.Go into its Dashboard and then click on the (+) to add a new script - https://take.ms/vvwoh.Download this script Alen made for us: 1.1_fs_in
cards.html:
You need to first install a Chrome Extension - TamperMonkey.

Go into its Dashboard and then click on the (+) to add a new script - https://take.ms/vvwoh.

Download this script Alen made for us: 1.1_fs_internal.js

Open it with any text editor (I'm using Notes here), then Select all + Copy.

Go back to TamperMonkey and Select all of the text in the script editor there (https://take.ms/apZKA) and Paste the one you copied from ours on top of it.

Replace MadaCarla with your name, and the email with yours... to be easy to identify in FS. - https://take.ms/sz4GKJ

File + Save: https://take.ms/qLPIx

Make sure it's Enabled. You can Edit it at a later time through the Installed Scripts dashboard. https://take.ms/AC5fc

Disclaimer: This is only for users @seomonitor.com, in order for the Customer Success department to be able to see the exact experience in FullStory, with the purpose of reporting bugs or issues.

It only works on app.seomonitor.com pages, in Google Chrome.

If you are using multiple computers, you would have to make sure it's active in all of those Chrome instances (if you are logged in with a user, that's not a problem, as it syncs extensions).

cards.title: GSC annotation
cards.text: Lets you know the date since when data is processed with GSCThe date the campaign was created / the GSC was connected and the date of the "GSC annotation" may differ (especially for US and UK projects
cards.html:
Lets you know the date since when data is processed with GSC

The date the campaign was created / the GSC was connected and the date of the "GSC annotation" may differ (especially for US and UK projects) because:

  • we offer the option to reprocess data up to 1 year before

  • you may have connected the Analytics account first

The annotation will be set on the first date there is info in Analytics to be matched with GSC

cards.html:
For campaigns that are set for a specific country/language.

The normal behavior is to connect first one of the main profiles, https://language.domain.com or sc-domain:language.domain.com) and only after to be able to connect another one (like the general one of your website, https://domain.com)

Update after task: this order does not apply to Wizard, where the checks are not the same as in Website Settings, and where the general profile can be connected.

cards.title: Generic aspects
cards.text: Don’t forget Intercom is a live chat. Answer ASAP (in under 15'), no matter if you have the solution right away or not. >> more info hereRemember not to provide the clients with any system architectur
cards.html:
• Don’t forget Intercom is a live chat. Answer ASAP (in under 15'), no matter if you have the solution right away or not. >> more info here

  • Remember not to provide the clients with any system architecture details (such as related to databases, servers, etc). You can say more vague things, like: some campaigns are experiencing delays in processing/displaying data, because we are performing maintenance activities on them.

If it's not a system that has been designed for the client to run their own analysis, we don't tell them to make their own additional checks, if they have data reliability concerns. It's our responsibility.

  • Any feature request should go through Product Portal (via Productboard, through the tags you add in Intercom). Remember to tell them to check, vote, submit new ideas, etc.

  • For important cases, Mada will try to get direct input from Cosmin (& devs, if needed).

  • Any request that is denied, it is to be denied with good reason and as confirmed with the Product Manager. Don't be too blunt.

  • Low and medium difficulty questions (main support, regular bugs) to be handled between the CS peeps.

  • High difficulty / high importance issues (very special distribution, migration aspects, difficult clients, special feature requests, etc) to be handled with Mada.

  • On particular cases and when referred to them by Mada, go to the specific Dev team member and/or Cosmin.

+ You now (late 2018) have the #technical-questions channel in Slack, so use that when needed.

Be curious when you get answers from Dev team.

Understand their answer, question it until you believe it. If you don't understand or don't believe the answer, why would the client?

If you don't understand/trust the answer always escalate. 

  • Use the smadmin tools wisely. :))

  • During workdays, be online (and available) on Intercom, Slack and/or work phone (SEOmonitor Skype). Exceptions: important internal meetings and/or client calls. When you leave work, set yourself Away + Reassigning in Intercom.

  • When you need time off (full or half day/s, specific hours, etc), let us know as soon as possible, so we can redistribute the work.

  • If there are issues and, if dev team confirms it is necessary to have an incident reported, get in touch with Mada (or Cosmin) ASAP to update the StatusPage. Whenever Statuspage sends the alert for "long-time running incident" through Intercom, check with the devs if the problem was solved and have the incident updated accordingly (through Mada).

+ Call me on my cell phone everytime you need my advice. - Mada :)

cards.title: Global localization. Why are the ranks from New York?
cards.text: Global localization is set for the google.com market.New York is the default city for ranks for the US localization and we decided to use it for the Global localization as well (US being the biggest s
cards.html:
Global localization is set for the google.com market.

New York is the default city for ranks for the US localization and we decided to use it for the Global localization as well (US being the biggest search market).

The Search Volumes are the broadest search volumes available (no specific location).  It might not be a sum all countries, but rather another rounding on top of roundings.

As a future development, we're taking into consideration allowing the users to choose the city they want the ranks to be for, when "localizing" a campaign for Global.

Public article on localization here

cards.title: Google Analytics URLs & compatibility with Analytics 360
cards.text: Yes, any type of Analytics profile can be connected within the app, including 360.It just needs to contain the corresponding data and to follow the general cross-check criteria:Please make sure to ass
cards.html:
Yes, any type of Analytics profile can be connected within the app, including 360.
It just needs to contain the corresponding data and to follow the general cross-check criteria:

Please make sure to associate the exact GA profile as the site URL that you've added to the application.
You should be cross-checking with the two below:

  • Admin >> Property >> Property Settings >> Default URL

  • Admin >> View >> View Settings >> Website URL

This way you make sure, that your campaign is set correctly.

Ad-hoc queries of your data are subject to the following general thresholds for sampling:

  • Analytics Standard: 500k sessions at the property level for the date range you are using

  • Analytics 360: 100M sessions at the view level for the date range you are using

Information from Analytics Help

Extra info: When you approach the 10-million-hit limit, Google Analytics starts to warn you and offers a few options to resolve the situation:

  • Buy Google Analytics 360;

  • Send fewer hits;

  • Perform app tracking using Google Analytics for Firebase (or switch to Event tracking using the beta version of Google Analytics App + Web).

Note: From the info received so far, we don't differentiate the 2 profiles when connected to a campaign, and in Organic Traffic the warning is for 10 M hits / month.

cards.title: Historical Ranks Import - expected behavior in SEOmonitor
cards.text: Importing historical ranks can be done 2 ways:Don't overwrite -  data will be added where there is no info, provided that it's older than the date the keyword was added. Any info more recent than the
cards.html:
Importing historical ranks can be done 2 ways:

Don't overwrite - data will be added where there is no info, provided that it's older than the date the keyword was added. Any info more recent than the date the keyword was added will be ignored.

Overwrite

  • If there are values (0 included) the new info will overwrite the existing one (for the moment empty cases have the same behavior as 0)

  • If there is historical data, no matter if imported or coming from SEOmonitor, and a new import is made for a shorter period, the expected behavior is for the data to be overwritten only for that specific period, leaving the rest of the info intact, provided that above conditions are applied for the imported file..

*Subsequently to importing the historical ranks, the "date added" of the keyword will become the first day with data and "date added" for the campaign will become the first day for which a keyword has imported historical data.

**Imported historical ranks will overwrite any data, including SEOmonitor's

cards.title: How SEOmonitor Detects Seasonal Keywords - Revised Explanation - internal documentation
cards.text: [March 14, 2025]SEOmonitor identifies seasonal keywords by looking for predictable patterns of search volume that repeat at similar times across multiple years.How the System Works1. Data Collection a
cards.html:
[March 14, 2025]

SEOmonitor identifies seasonal keywords by looking for predictable patterns of search volume that repeat at similar times across multiple years.

How the System Works

1. Data Collection and Preparation

  • The system analyzes up to 24 months (2 years) of search volume data
  • It needs at least 12 months of data to make a decision
  • The data is organized by month to detect patterns

2. Establishing Thresholds

The system calculates two thresholds (one for each year of data):

  • It finds the median (middle value) of the monthly search volumes
  • It calculates the Median Absolute Deviation (MAD) to understand normal variation
  • It applies a dynamic factor to the MAD (calculated using the chooseMadFactorDynamic method)
  • The threshold is set as: max(median+madFactor*MAD, 50)
  • The 50 value (minAbsolutePeak) ensures small-volume keywords aren't detected as seasonal due to minor fluctuations

3. Finding Seasonal Periods

  • The system looks for consecutive months where search volume is at least 95% of the threshold
  • These months form "segments" - potential seasonal periods
  • For a segment to be valid, it must:
    • Last at least 3 months (minSeasonLength)
    • Be less than 8 months long (otherwise, it's considered year-round demand)
    • Not be part of more than 2 separate seasonal periods in a year

4. Comparing Patterns Across Years

  • The system compares segments from the first year with segments from the second year.
  • For segments to match, they must:
    • Start within 2 months of each other in each year (maxStartDiff parameter);
    • Have lengths that differ by no more than 2 months.

5. The Final Decision

  • If matching seasonal patterns are found across two years, the system identifies:
    • The start month of the seasonal period;
    • The end month of the seasonal period;
    • Potentially a second seasonal period, if one exists.

How to Verify if a Keyword is Correctly Identified as Seasonal

To check if the system correctly identified a keyword as seasonal:

  1. Gather 24 months of search volume data for the keyword.
  2. For each 12-month period:
    • Calculate the median search volume
    • Determine the typical variation (MAD)
    • Set a threshold that's either significantly above the median or at least 50 searches.
  3. Identify periods where search volume exceeds or nearly exceeds this threshold for at least 3 consecutive months.
  4. Check if these periods appear in similar months across both years.
  5. If matching patterns are found that start within 2 months of each other and are of similar length, the keyword is correctly identified as seasonal.

This approach ensures that the system identifies genuine seasonal patterns while filtering out random fluctuations or one-time spikes in search interest.

The Three Seasonal States

  1. In Season (KW_IN_SEASON = 1)
    • This is when the current month falls within the seasonal period
    • Example: If a keyword is seasonal from June (6) to August (8), and the current month is July (7), it's "in season".
    • Has the highest importance value (3)
  2. Approaching Season (KW_APROACHING_SEASON = 0)
    • This is when the next month will be the start of the seasonal period
    • Example: If a keyword is seasonal from June (6) to August (8), and the current month is May (5), it's "approaching season"
    • Has medium importance value (2)
  3. Out of Season (KW_OUT_OF_SEASON = 2)
    • This is when the keyword is neither in season nor approaching season.
    • Example: If a keyword is seasonal from June (6) to August (8), and the current month is October (10), it's "out of season"
    • Has the lowest importance value (1)



Shape-up here: https://app.clickup.com/2179830/v/dc/22gqp-15507/22gqp-136972
Seasonality algorithm: https://app.clickup.com/2179830/v/dc/22gqp-22832/22gqp-144112

cards.title: How accurate is the Traffic with SEO estimation, in Forecast, for pitching?
cards.text: Since it is a proprietary algorithm we use for this, I can tell you it is calculated using data from the CTR of the website, the search volume of the keywords and their current ranks.The CTR in the Fo
cards.html:
Since it is a proprietary algorithm we use for this, I can tell you it is calculated using data from the CTR of the website, the search volume of the keywords and their current ranks.
The CTR in the Forecasting area of the app, it's SEOmonitor's own data, updated regularly and based on statistical analysis.

But unfortunately, can't tell you an average or percentage for its accuracy, as it is an estimation (not to mention that there are also exterior factors, that can influence traffic - google updates, changes in search trends, economic changes etc.).

And the more the target for the visibility is met (so reach the ranks set by you in Forecast), the better the estimation will be reached.

cards.title: How are features added? Product Board process and feature request methodology
cards.text: The Product Portal will be less relevant for a while - rather than encouraging clients to go and check/vote features in PP, we will verify for them their feature requests in Product Board.Not all feat
cards.html:
The Product Portal will be less relevant for a while - rather than encouraging clients to go and check/vote features in PP, we will verify for them their feature requests in Product Board.

Not all features notified in PB will be accessible to clients on PP.


Request features reach the Product Development team through several channels:

  • as previously, through InterCom tags (you will mark even general feedback on the product as feature requests)

  • sent by customers via Product Portal 

  • Received from different sources and centralized via the new feedback@seomonitor email address

  • Suggestions from the team

(They are all analyzed according to the below process)


Before being analyzed, the insights are in an Inbox.


Once processed, the requests go to Features. (Every Insight is linked to a Feature, more insights could belong to the same feature).

Several drivers (Reliability, UX, jobs to be done, strategy) determine a user impact score, divided by a complexity score and resulting into a general prioritization score. 

According to the prioritization, they receive a queue number, so you can already mention to the client the position in the list.


The Road Map can be consulted for next immediate features.

They are organized on multiple categories: big platform, Topics, systems, Signals, others



Once they are in production/done, you can notify the client.

!!! (to be updated) - in *Product Board* the status will most likely be *In Progress*, while *Done* is to be created in Gitscrum

Thus, check the trello summary, test the feature and add comment in Product Board + notify customer

The trasability and linking to the client are done through Insights. 

There, you will see the total number of clients having asked for a particular feature.

You will be able to access that very conversation to update on the new feature.

cards.title: How can clients tell if they're receiving an API rate limit error?
cards.text: The technical details about rate limit-related data we provide on the responses:For requests failing due to rate limit, you get this response:HTTP Code: 429 (Too Many Requests)Response Body:{    "mess
cards.html:
The technical details about rate limit-related data we provide on the responses:

For requests failing due to rate limit, you get this response:

HTTP Code: 429 (Too Many Requests)

Response Body:

{

"message": "Too Many Attempts.",

"code": 0

}

Relevant Headers:

  • X-RateLimit-Limit - the limit of requests/min
  • Retry-After - (in seconds) wait time until the limit resets
  • X-RateLimit-Reset - exact timestamp when the limit resets

We also respond with rate limit-related headers on successful requests:

  • X-RateLimit-Limit - the max. number of requests/min
  • X-RateLimit-Remaining: - remaining number requests in the interval

These Technical details can be sent to the person or tool handling the scripts to fix and adjust the limit.

cards.title: How can you add two coupons at the same time?
cards.text: To add an additional coupon in Chargebee, follow these steps:Access the specific subscription you want to update.Edit the subscription.Manually add the additional coupon within the edit interface.Impo
cards.html:
To add an additional coupon in Chargebee, follow these steps:

  1. Access the specific subscription you want to update.
  2. Edit the subscription.
  3. Manually add the additional coupon within the edit interface.

Important:
Do not directly use the "Add Coupon" button on the subscription screen. If you do, it will override and remove the initial coupon. This is particularly critical for prepaid plans, as using this option will remove the existing 17% discount.

Note:
This process only works if you're applying two different types of coupons:

  • one as a percentage-based discount (e.g., 17%) and
  • the other as a fixed-amount discount.

For instance, if a client has a prepaid plan with a 17% discount and you'd like to add a fixed-amount coupon, this method will work, without removing the percentage discount.

cards.html:

Most likely the data will differ between the 2 profiles (sessions and transactions) because of the way GA4 is recording sessions, the forecast was based on the UA data so it will be differences in terms of the inertial data for the remaining months.

cards.title: How did we come up with the Visibility Impression Rates?
cards.text: The Visibility metric is more of a scroll-depth analysis (so more rank-like than CTR-like): What do I see if I don’t scroll, what do I see if I scroll once, and so on.We've done an analysis on million
cards.html:
The Visibility metric is more of a scroll-depth analysis (so more rank-like than CTR-like):
What do I see if I don’t scroll, what do I see if I scroll once, and so on.

We've done an analysis on millions of keywords and the anonymized GSC data in our databases around 2020

It’s powerful when used as relative: Visibility is primarily made to be representative relative so in trends (self vs self) and comparisons (self vs competitors) to see current opportunities for growth.

The absolute metric to look at would be the CTR (and the CTR-based "% of clicks on Organic results"). This is based on statistical data:

· We have (for example) April’s Search Volume value (so not the 12-month average Search Volume)

· We have the Desktop and Mobile ranks

· We have the Google Search Console data and the average ranks from there

· We have the SERP features present in the corresponding pages.

So we were able to conclude that: “if I have Maps/Places in the SERP, this would get me this many clicks for the #3 rank”.

Then, with all of these features and the corresponding data, we made the curve, which helped us estimate the remaining clicks on organic results. This then gives the impact in searches (so % out of the Search Volume).

The Challenges of a Visibility metric document can be found here.

This will also bring in more clarity to this question.

Rank filter

  • expected behaviour: rank higher than 4 = better ranks than 4 (1,2,3), not mathematical

Rank change filter

cards.title: How do we calculate Difficulty? (Authoritative Pages)
cards.text: Task: https://app.clickup.com/t/cz07jdHow do we calculate the authoritative pages in the difficulty metric?The short version is:For the Top 100 keywords:we know what's ahead of you (in Top 100)so we c
cards.html:
Task: https://app.clickup.com/t/cz07jd

How do we calculate the authoritative pages in the difficulty metric?

The short version is:

For the Top 100 keywords:

  • we know what's ahead of you (in Top 100)

  • so we can provide info on these - our "classic" SEO difficulty

For 99+ keywords:

  • we don't know what's ahead of you (how low you are past top 100)

  • the only thing we can know is how many LPs are "trying" to rank on the same keyword - other pages like yours

  • so we look for the keyword in the title - full or partial match

  • this is possible only because we have the DB - with LP title and descriptions

  • so it's not easy to replicate (googling "intitle:" could help, but it's expensive to scale)


The steps through which the algorithm goes when performing these checks can be found here: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1T5MLvu3NR8ZFmrPX_tMIZHxDAKpql82krMZjeVhEdDc/edit#heading=h.dm7gvqkq8pjs

Full detailed explanation of the algorithm and process can be found here: https://app.nuclino.com/SEOmonitor/Dev/Dificultate---formula-noua-803c33d6-e7f4-46c2-a2dc-e65c9064d9b4

cards.text: pls edit and translate - Visibility Explain ar trebui sa functioneze identic ca cel de pe view ul de keywords. Performance pozitiv keywordurile care au avut schimbare de rank pozitiva, performance neg
cards.html: pls edit and translate - Visibility Explain ar trebui sa functioneze identic ca cel de pe view ul de keywords. Performance pozitiv keywordurile care au avut schimbare de rank pozitiva, performance negativ keyword urile care au avut scadere de rank , SV performance kw care si au schimbat search volume ul in interval fara a fi agregate de noi sau de google. Resutl base change contine cuvinte ce s au agregat la noi sau al google, cuvinte ce s au adaugat sau sters cu optiunea de a nu se recalcula visibility score.

cards.title: How do we detect if a website is featured in the Local Pack (Maps/Places)
cards.text: First, if the Maps/Places result has a link, it has to match the campaign's URL.For results without links, we're using the result title going through this algorithm:Curation for the result titleRemove
cards.html:
First, if the Maps/Places result has a link, it has to match the campaign's URL.


For results without links, we're using the result title going through this algorithm:

  • Curation for the result title
    • Remove all non-alphanumeric characters
    • Remove words with <= 3 characters
  • We then split the title into individual words, and we count how many are included in the campaign's domain name
  • We consider the campaign as featured if:
    • The concatenation of the title words exactly matches the domain name
    • OR more than 75% of the title words are contained in the domain name (but no less than 2)


IF the above algorithm doesn't match, there's a secondary fallback -> if the campaign homepage title contains the exact same words as the Places/Maps result's title.


*The site's homepage can be checked by hovering over the tab when accessing the tab in the browser.

from https://app.clickup.com/t/8694778dw?comment=90120035149417

cards.title: How do we do the crawl for on-page/index health section?
cards.text: We crawl on a daily basis, as long as the campaign remained active.We crawl and display the first 10k pages in Analytics (from the last 30 days).Their order of display is descending number of sessions
cards.html:
We crawl on a daily basis, as long as the campaign remained active.

We crawl and display the first 10k pages in Analytics (from the last 30 days).

Their order of display is descending number of sessions.

cards.title: How do we estimate the traffic loss in Google index status report?
cards.text: We can find  the Google index status report within the Index Health feature in the Dashboard.Where errors are found, we offer an estimate of the traffic loss.The estimated traffic loss is calculated b
cards.html:
We can find the Google index status report within the Index Health feature in the Dashboard.

Where errors are found, we offer an estimate of the traffic loss.

The estimated traffic loss is calculated based on the visits of the Landing pages, prior to a crawl that returned an error 404. 

It refers to organic traffic, the calculation takes into consideration the GSC clicks.

We're also "spoofing" the location using the UULE document.cookie to set the location.

NOT by IP!

Note: this is mostly internal info, but we can share it if clients need to double-check anything.

The newest version:

This one is looking at the number of results on the SERP page.

The main/parent would have the highest number of results.

AND: a misspelling will never be considered main, it's always child/aggregate.


The initial version:

Later edit: we no longer use the Ads Forecast anywhere.
The main/parent keyword in an aggregation would be the one that has the highest number of impressions in Ads - and this can be checked in the Forecasts tab: https://take.ms/e2vGN

Still valid:
In the Historical metrics tab, you can check if, indeed, those keywords are part of an aggregation. The table there will display only the first keyword you added when doing the research, and the proof of an aggregation comes from having all of the others appear in the pop-up over that keyword.

cards.title: How do you calculate the Revenue/mo displayed in the Opportunity pop-up?
cards.text: The data used to calculate the estimated Revenue/month expected to be received once the keyword reaches the Top3 comes from Google Analytics. This applies to Conversions as well.If the campaign doesn’
cards.html:
The data used to calculate the estimated Revenue/month expected to be received once the keyword reaches the Top3 comes from Google Analytics.
This applies to Conversions as well.

If the campaign doesn’t have Revenue/Conversions for that keyword, we use the Average Order Value from the “Non-brand” GA traffic.

To expand a bit on all the info on the pop-up:

  • the Additional Sessions are based on the CTR difference between the current position and the Top 3
  • if the campaign is set as eCommerce and there’s a GA connected, you have the Revenue via the above Sessions + the GA Conversion Rate;
  • if it’s not eCommerce, instead of Revenue, you have additional Conversions;
  • or additional Clicks, if only GSC is connected
  • the Ads equivalent comes from Ads’ CPC and the corresponding sessions above.

    See also this related card.

cards.title: How does Data Studio pull out the data for eCommerce campaigns.
cards.text: For an eCommerce set up campaign, we have the following:In the Campaign connector the "conversions" metric pulls in the equivalent of transactions and the "goals" metric pulls the goals view.In the Or
cards.html:
For an eCommerce set up campaign, we have the following:

  • In the Campaign connector the "conversions" metric pulls in the equivalent of transactions and the "goals" metric pulls the goals view.

  • In the Organic Traffic connector, there's only one metric for "conversions" that pulls in just the eCommerce view so it's the equivalent of "transactions". There is no field for "goals" in this connector at the moment and the one for "conversions" doesn't pull them either.

This is intended at this point and will be looked into in the future.

cards.title: How does adding/removing keywords influence the Visibility Change?
cards.text: When you remove keywords from a group or archive them all together, the Visibility is recalculated retroactively. This also affects the baseline, so the action has no influence on the Visibility Chang
cards.html:
When you remove keywords from a group or archive them all together, the Visibility is recalculated retroactively.
This also affects the baseline, so the action has no influence on the Visibility Change, as it will be as if the keywords were never tracked.

So the removed/archived keywords will not be present in the performance explainer as excluded keywords, as it's the case for All keywords:

Adding to the group keywords that are already tracked in the campaign has the same behavior.

However, if the keywords are new to the campaign, they will affect the group's performance the same way as for All Keywords.
This means that they will not influence the Visibility historically, and they will be displayed in the baseline explainer.

In the event that the keyword has been tracked, then archived, then reactivated, the Visibility should be calculated retroactively according to the data they stored, and if the selected timeframe includes a day without data, they should be present in the baseline explainer.

________________________________________________________________________________

Can you have a visibility change higher than the VALUE OF THE DAILY visibility?

The answer is yes.

This is because the Visibility Change is performance-related therefore it's calculated for the keywords that were present for the entire interval (at both ends of the timeframe).

In this example, there were 6 keywords added during the selected timeframe that caused a drop in the daily visibility (or chart trend) but did not influence the Change.

So the daily Visibility and the Visibility Change can be calculated for different lists of keywords, depending on the keyword movements throughout time. And the second one is not always the mathematical difference of the first one.

cards.title: How does the Share of Clicks work with timeframes?
cards.text: Why It Works This Way?The Share of Clicks metric is designed to show the current status (as of the end date) rather than a trend over time. The calculation uses the rankings data as of the end date se
cards.html:
Why It Works This Way?

The Share of Clicks metric is designed to show the current status (as of the end date) rather than a trend over time.
The calculation uses the rankings data as of the end date selected in the timeframe, not an average over the entire period. This is why when you select a longer timeframe like "January 1 - March 31", you still get the same result as "March 1-31" - because both queries use March 31 as the reference date for the calculation.

What's the calculation?

The share of clicks calculation in SEOmonitor works as follows:

  1. When the postShareOfVoice method in  SiteController.php is called, it receives a request containing:
    • A start date and end date (in the intervals object)
    • Device type (desktop 'd' or mobile 'm')
    • Other filtering parameters like folder_id and keyword_ids
  2. The controller then creates a CompetitorsHandler instance, passing along these parameters, and calls its shareOfVoice() method.
  3. In the shareOfVoice() method, the handler calls CompareStatsManager::fetchCompareShareOFvoice() with the following key parameters:
    • The site hash and ID
    • Only the end date from the timeframe (not the start date)
    • The keyword IDs
    • Device type

Because only the end date is passed to the database query, not the start date, the expected behaviour is as follows:

    • When selecting March 1-31, you get the share of clicks as of March 31
    • When selecting April 1-28, you get the share of clicks as of April 28
    • When selecting January 1-March 31, you still get the share of clicks as of March 31

Looking at the SQL function select_share_of_voice_v2, it:

    • Calculates estimated visits based on keyword rankings and search volumes
    • Sums these values to get the total traffic for each competitor site
    • Calculates the percentage of traffic (share of clicks) for each competitor
    • But it doesn't take a date range - it's using the most recent data as of the end date


*For the Share of Click formula, check this Guru card.

cards.title: How does the average rank weighted by the search volume work in the Forecast module?
cards.text: It represents the average rank on the keywords' searches.Since the target ranks are a maximum of top 10, the CTR curve becomes less important in this formula, meaning that you can compare average rank
cards.html:
It represents the average rank on the keywords' searches.
Since the target ranks are a maximum of top 10, the CTR curve becomes less important in this formula, meaning that you can compare average ranks to their target ranks instead of Visibility to target ranks.

Let's take the following example:


The average rank represents the average between KW#1 and KW#2 = 6 - which doesn't give us a picture of the searches done on the keywords.

The average weighted rank represents (KW#1 * KW#1's SV + KW#2 * KW#2's SV) / (KW#1's SV + KW#2's SV) - which also takes into consideration the searches done on the keywords.

cards.title: How does the multi-location campaign option work?
cards.text: You can only have multi-locations at a city and region level. For different countries, an entirely different campaign should be added.You can have as many cities and regions you want in a campaign as
cards.html:
• You can only have multi-locations at a city and region level. For different countries, an entirely different campaign should be added.

  • You can have as many cities and regions you want in a campaign as long as it's not doubled - this includes the archived campaigns too. So if you have a campaign for the UK and you have London as location and archive that, you won't be able to add another campaign for London.

  • If users want to have a pool of keywords set for a certain city, different than the archived one users would need to restore the group, archive/remove the old keywords and add the new ones.

  • If there are keywords present in multiple locations and you archive one of the campaigns, only the ones from that campaign will be archived, the other ones won't be affected (even if they're the same keywords).

  • The archiving option will be available as it is for any campaign, the multi-location option should be considered a campaign not a group, the only difference is that it won't be added to the costs if it's at the same country-level.

THE EXISTING CAMPAIGNS THAT ARE ALREADY TRACKED AT A COUNTRY-LEVEL WILL HAVE THE OPTION OF ADDING MULTIPLE CITY/REGION LOCATIONS TO THEM.

cards.title: How does the sorting by Visibility Trend work?
cards.text: The Trend sort from the Keyword Table takes into consideration just the ranks' impact to the Visibility Score change.It is not the same as the one from the Visibility Explainer, which takes into accou
cards.html:
The Trend sort from the Keyword Table takes into consideration just the ranks' impact to the Visibility Score change.

It is not the same as the one from the Visibility Explainer, which takes into account the impact the ranks AND the search volumes had on the Visibility Score change.

This means that in the Keyword Table, you'll have either the positive or the negative changes sorted by the biggest positive/negative impact of the rank changes (and not by the absolute value of the changes).
In the Visibility Explainer, however, you'll have both positive and negative changes, mixed together, sorted by the "total" impact of their changes.

cards.title: How is the Relevance info from Topics calculated?
cards.text: The info from the Relevance column, in Topics (https://take.ms/JqbK2) it takes multiple variables into account, including (but not limited to) the ranks for the results (the keywords in the topic), th
cards.html: The info from the Relevance column, in Topics (https://take.ms/JqbK2) it takes multiple variables into account, including (but not limited to) the ranks for the results (the keywords in the topic), the ranks for the initial query (the topic) and the common landing pages between these.

cards.title: How is the Share of Clicks calculated?
cards.text: The traffic for each domain is calculated using the discounted search volume:avg_search_volume * latest_ctrThis is then divided by the total traffic (the sum of traffic for all domains) to calculate e
cards.html:
The traffic for each domain is calculated using the discounted search volume:

avg_search_volume * latest_ctr


This is then divided by the total traffic (the sum of traffic for all domains) to calculate each one's percentage.


So, the formula for the monthly Share of Clicks value is:

((avg_search_volume * latest_ctr * coefficient)/100 000)*30 

Note:
To check how the Share of Clicks behaves, in reference to the selected timeframe: How does the Share of Clicks Work with Timeframes?

cards.title: How is the tool helpful, or can be used by a publisher?
cards.text: You can use the Research module to figure out which keywords would make sense to focus on for content creation and further filter those keywords that trigger SERP features related to news items. You c
cards.html:
You can use the Research module to figure out which keywords would make sense to focus on for content creation and further filter those keywords that trigger SERP features related to news items.
You could also use the Google Ads Equivalent from the Forecasting module to see if a keyword topic has a high average CPC. A high CPC on search could translate to a high CPM. In this example, the publishers make most of the revenue from Display Ads, and there might be a correlation between high CPC and high CPM.  

In terms of rank tracking, you could only watch for those keywords that you want to focus on, so you don’t invest too much in keywords that don’t drive revenue or where Google Search Console provides good enough data.

As for features that a publisher might find useful:

  1. Keyword Vault: You can add a list of as many keywords as you wish to the Keyword Vault and get SERP, search volume, and ranking data for all of them.
    This list can be kept for monthly updates, providing you with ongoing insights without having to pay extra. The minimum subscription has a generous unlimited tracking option.
  2. Large Exports Available: SEOmonitor enables you to export the top 50,000 ranking keywords of any website for the UK and US markets and the top 1,000 for the rest of the markets. This feature can be particularly useful for news publishers operating in various geographical locations
  3. Website Explorer: This feature lets you analyze all the ranking data of any domain you need. You can see the associated keyword list and their main attributes, look at the Keyword Gap, and evaluate the competition.
    This data is updated monthly, providing fresh insights regularly.
    You can also keep an eye on competitors to see what they are ranking on.

cards.title: How is the traffic growth distributed over the forecasted months?
cards.text: For each keyword we calculate how many additional monthly sessions will be generated if the rank improves (from the current rank to the desired top). -> Now we know how much additional traffic we’ll h
cards.html:
1. For each keyword we calculate how many additional monthly sessions will be generated if the rank improves (from the current rank to the desired top). -> Now we know how much additional traffic we’ll have at the end of the forecast period.

  2. Then we plot the progression on a performance over time curve (default is exponential, but linear or custom are also available) -> that means we start adding the total additional traffic from 0% to 100% in the 12 months (e g: fifth month would get 30% of the total additional traffic that would be generated at the end of the forecast)

  3. Then we apply the seasonality of the keywords on each month.

cards.title: How the pages get assigned to the Brand or Non-Brand list in LP view in the Traffic section
cards.text: If the traffic of a page is generated by branded keywords it will be listed in the Brand segmentIf the traffic is generated by non-branded keywords, the page will be listed in the Non-Brand segment.if
cards.html:
• If the traffic of a page is generated by branded keywords it will be listed in the Brand segment

  • If the traffic is generated by non-branded keywords, the page will be listed in the Non-Brand segment.

  • if there are both branded and non-branded keywords associated with a Landing Page, it will be listed in both segments with the corresponding traffic generated by the branded/non-branded keywords.

In other words, traffic by landing page is the sum of the traffic generated by the keywords associated to the Landing Page (brand, non-brand or both).

If the LP gets traffic generated by keywords from both categories, the list of keywords per LP should only hold those corresponding to the list your checking, so only Brand or respectivelly Non-Brand keywords.

cards.title: How to change subscription currency
cards.text: TO BE DONE ONLY IF THERE'S NO WAY TO CONVINCE THE CLIENT TO STICK TO THE INITIAL CURRENCYWhen the client subscribes to a certain currency & then asks to be updated to a different one, the steps are as
cards.html:
TO BE DONE ONLY IF THERE'S NO WAY TO CONVINCE THE CLIENT TO STICK TO THE INITIAL CURRENCY

When the client subscribes to a certain currency & then asks to be updated to a different one, the steps are as follows:

1. Change the currency in Admin -> Change subscription details -> Currency

  1. Cancel the current subscription in Chargebee -> select Customer -> select Subscription -> Cancel subscription button in the right sidebar.

  1. Change currency in ChargeBee -> select Customer -> Edit Customer button on the right sidebar.

  1. Create a new subscription -> select Customer -> Create new subscription button on the right sidebar.
    (more details here - Creating (manual) subscription for (in-trial) accounts)

  1. Add the plan for the new currency to the subscription.

*Sometimes, the plan for the new currency might not be available immediately.
Be patient; it will show up in 30 minutes or so.

cards.title: How to check traffic in Google Analytics (GA4)
cards.text: For any GA report you're checking, you have to apply the below filtering:Session medium exactly matches organicANDSession source contains googleYou will select different report categories depending on
cards.html:
For any GA report you're checking, you have to apply the below filtering:

  • Session medium exactly matches organic

AND

  • Session source contains google

You will select different report categories depending on the metric you're checking.

If you're set for Goals, you'll check Conversions and their corresponding Revenue on Engagement / User Engagement -> Events.
Depending on the setup, you'll compare the total or the sum of each metric for the events you selected to track in SEOmonitor with the Goals/Revenue widget value.

If you're set up as an Ecommerce, you'll check the Transactions and their corresponding Revenue under Monetization -> Transactions.

Sessions will be checked under Traffic Aquisition for both Ecommerce and Goals setup.

Note: It is best to check day by day to avoid sampling.
So, compare one day from GA with one from the platform and/or exports.

cards.title: How to create a coupon
cards.text: S​elect the client you wish to add a coupon to →  the subscription must still be active .I. I​f there is an existing coupon, you might want to first remove it before adding another, in that case:Go to
cards.html:
S​elect the client you wish to add a coupon to → the subscription must still be active .

I. I​f there is an existing coupon, you might want to first remove it before adding another, in that case:

  1. Go to Change subscription
  2. Make sure all information is filled in.

O​nce step 2 is completed go to→ View details → Remove + Remove

II. Create the new coupon

  1. Go to Product Catalog → Coupons (Open in a New browser window)
  2. Upper right corner → Create coupon
  3. Name the coupon so you can easily find it later (get UserID from smadmin, get timestamp through here) + ID will be created automatically.
  4. get timestamp through here
  5. fill in details here
  6. Select Discount Type → Always will be Fixed
  7. Keep in mind the currency set for this account, select accordingly
  8. Select the amount you want to apply the coupon for
  9. Apply on → Always Invoice Amount
  10. Duration Type → usually limited to the number of months you apply it to
  11. Validity refers to the period this coupon will be available to be applied to a subscription NOT for the validity period offered on the coupon
  12. Maximum Redemptions → always 1
  13. Save + Check if the coupon was created

III. After the coupon was created it needs to be added to the subscription

  1. Go to → Change Subscription
  2. Add coupon
  3. Select coupon ID here + Add coupon
  4. Apply changes immediately
  5. Add to unbilled charges
  6. Change subscription
  7. Preview changes + Confirm
  8. Double check on Customer Details if the coupon was added successfully

→ In case you with to change Next Billing to an earlier date

  1. Subscription → View Details
  2. Go here to Change Next Billing
  3. Select Date, place Comment (initials) + Change→ Confirm
  4. Check under Invoices if the new invoice was generated
  5. If the invoice has amount 0, it means that our coupon was applied successfully

https://www.loom.com/share/7b195d49dabb473da5c8c3ac87cae02c

cards.title: How to create a manual prepaid invoice in ChargeBee
cards.text: To create a manual prepaid invoice in ChargeBee, use the Quick Charge option after you select the subscription.If the client still has credit, you must add that amount to the new one. This is because
cards.html:
To create a manual prepaid invoice in ChargeBee, use the Quick Charge option after you select the subscription.

If the client still has credit, you must add that amount to the new one.
This is because the Quick Charge feeds from the credit.

e.g.

Existing credit = EUR 2300

New downpayments = EUR 1500

Total value for the quick charge = 3800

Beware of active coupons, as they'll also be automatically applied.

cards.title: How to handle leads conversations
cards.text: Who is considered a lead?Sales lead: any agency that doesn't already have an account with SEOmonitorCustomer Service lead: any brand or non-agency that doesn't have an account with SEOmonitor.How shou
cards.html:
Who is considered a lead?

Sales lead: any agency that doesn't already have an account with SEOmonitor

Customer Service lead: any brand or non-agency that doesn't have an account with SEOmonitor.

How should Customer Service handle lead conversations?

For Sales Leads we directly tag Sales in the conversation and let her take over. We won't offer any details. Sales will start and finish the Sales process (negotiation, procurement process etc.)

Any conversation started by a Customer Service Lead has to be carried out by the Customer Service Department throughout the entire process:

  1. Subscription Negotiation

  2. Procurement Conversation - where they add us as suppliers and we do the same with them in turn

  3. Product features presentation - send over personalized Onboarding decks

  4. Contract / Service Agreement details

  • establish if they have specific or desired legal topics that don't align with out T&Cs and we should know about

  • desired payment option

  • desired currency (EUR is standard for a different one confirm with Manager)

  • desired payment period (NET 30 is our standard, for any other period confirm with Manager)

How can you tell you're dealing with a Sales Lead?

1. Know the basics about who we target and what qualified lead means.Lead Qualification

2. Check their website if there is SEO or Digital Marketing Agency; if yes then tag sales manager.

! If you're not sure if someone is a lead and you need confirmation, go to your Manager. She/he will check in Clutch (and when Judge is complete in the Sales Hubspot Pipeline) !

Migrations and leads

When a lead decides to migrate, the Customer Service Department takes over the conversation and follows the steps in the Migrations Procedure.

After the migration is finalized and confirmed, the Customer Service agent will tag Sales letting them know it's done and they can start asking for payment of the first invoice.

Financial issues and leads

For all Sales Leads conversations, the Sales department will have to create the subscription and send over the first invoice to the client and make sure they pay. After this payment is done, the lead transforms into client = agency which means that the Customer Service Department will deal with any requests from this client and escalate accordingly to the Onboarding Department.

For all Customer Service leads, as previously mentioned, the Customer Service Department will handle all types of issues that are mentioned by these entities.

Renegotiations & New contracts

  1. Big media groups (f.x. Mediacom, Publicis):

If they want to sign a new contract for a different location with different teams, we consider this client a new Sales lead and immediately inform the Sales Department by tagging them in the conversation or sending an email.

If they want to renegotiate the existing contract (so they don't want anything new) then we inform the Onboarding & Retention Department to take over the conversation.

  1. Agencies

All renegotiation topics for agencies should end up in the Onboarding & Retention Department.

  1. Brands & non-agencies

All renegotiations for brands and non-agency clients will be taken care of by the Customer Service Department.

cards.text: In order to see a newly added template in one of our Data Studio connectors (we already had for Forecast and we recently added for Organic Traffic) you would have to revoke the access for that specifi

If the user wants them to be applied from now on, the campaign should NOT be processed.

cards.title: How/what can we check when there are "fake Search Volume" cases?
cards.text: You make the check in Google Ads --> Keyword Planner --> Get search volumes and Forecast https://take.ms/Wfv1VIn Forecasts, use 100 for the value and select "exact match" (check this video to learn ho
cards.html:
You make the check in Google Ads --> Keyword Planner --> Get search volumes and Forecast
https://take.ms/Wfv1V

In Forecasts, use 100 for the value and select "exact match" (check this video to learn how - https://take.ms/g1Rd7).
It's a high-enough bid to return maximum of impressions.

That should be the Search Volume value for a keyword that displays a notification saying that it's wrongfully aggregated under a certain keyword, so we replaced the Search Volume with another AdWords metric, "the number of monthly impressions that it would get if you would place the maximum bid on it"

!!! Note! This is not the same case as the one with "dangerous keywords" - the ones reporting 0 SV.

cards.title: H​ow to look into a campaign with high unallocated traffic - 2 different scenarios
cards.text: T​here can be multiple reasons why a campaign can have a higher unallocated traffic. H​ow do we check the un-allocated traffic?L​ooking first in the app into the Traffic Module, we can identify the pe
cards.html:
T​here can be multiple reasons why a campaign can have a higher unallocated traffic.

H​ow do we check the un-allocated traffic?

L​ooking first in the app into the Traffic Module, we can identify the percentage of unallocated traffic (rule of thumb: under 30% unallocated is ok):

A​fter that, to investigate what was not distributed (allocated), we go on All keywords and look into the Landing Pages view:

Note that the correct way to investigate is to look at it on a day-by-day basis!

O​n the Landing Pages view, we check the pages that have under Keywords: N/A (-). These are pages that we could not match from GA to GSC, so the traffic data on them is not distributed into keywords.

To be able to understand better why these pages weren't matched, we can then export the data from Traffic Module for GA, GSC and GA/GSC together. We place all the information in different tabs of an excel to be able to compare the information regarding the LPs:

Please see b​elow an example of two scenarios that we can identify by looking into the LP's with N/A.

1​. When a large number of sessions being registered to subdomains:

For example app.futrli.com, app.futrli.com.au or do.futrli.com → out of a total of 205 for the day only 106 sessions could be distributed (by matching the connected GSC and the LP data in it)

→ Solutions in this case would be:

  1. Connect additional GSC accounts, for each subdomain, or a sc-domain: profile;
  2. Change the GA with one that is filtered to bring only the traffic for the main domain;
  3. A third option would be for us to filter the traffic in SEOmonitor directly (only the main domain). Please bear in mind that this would cause the totals to look different than in GA.

Depending on what their desired outcome is (and the goals with the client or how they want to manage the campaign), they can go for the 1st option if they need to have the full traffic (all subdomains) distributed. The 2nd option (or the 3rd option as backup), if they only need to track the traffic for the main domain.


2​. Product pages (either too old or too new content) - that Analytics still sees, but they’re no longer (or not yet) present in GSC – and pages that seem to not be organic traffic, such as /checkout or /cart.

The two main issues here are:

  1. There are sessions on LPs that should not be considered Google Organic traffic (that’s what we take from GA: google+organic) and “mixed” in that section of GA, for example: pages like /basket or /checkout. These aren’t in GSC, because they also aren’t (or shouldn’t be) indexed:(no organic query would lead us to that. ( you can perform the search on Google with site:domain.com/checkout )
  2. Some product pages (either too old or too new content) - that Analytics still sees, but they’re no longer (or not yet) present in GSC. Some of them also returning 404s :

→ Solutions in this case would be:

  • Either they filter those /basket or /checkout pages directly in GA (if they have access to the client’s account or if they can advice the client to do so) and then connect the updated profile & view;
  • Or we could exclude them from the distribution on our side;
  • The 404s would need to be corrected on their side, we can exclude them, but not “in bulk”.

See here:

and then

cards.html:
Google is testing indenting search, results snippets from within the same domain name. The indented results simply bring together pages ranking for the same or similar keywords.

cards.title: Intent classification
cards.text: The logic behind intent classification is based on how competing pages rank in the SERP for that keyword.We classify each landing page in the top results as informational, commercial, or transactional
cards.html:
The logic behind intent classification is based on how competing pages rank in the SERP for that keyword.

We classify each landing page in the top results as informational, commercial, or transactional). Then we convert to percentages and apply this rule:

  1. Informational wins only if it’s MORE than commercial + transactional combined
  2. Otherwise, if commercial > transactional → commercial
  3. Otherwise → transactional



Why the stricter bar for informational? Most SERPs naturally include some informational results (Wikipedia, blog posts, how-to guides). Without this threshold, a keyword with 40% informational + 35% commercial + 25% transactional would be wrongly labeled “informational” — even though 60% of the SERP serves a purchase-oriented intent.

The rule ensures “informational” only wins when it truly dominates the SERP, which makes the label more actionable for content strategy decisions.

cards.title: Is the Longtail Effect included in the total Addidtional Session?
cards.text: In the Scenario, the top stats Additional Sessions includes the extra traffic the Longtail Effect can bring.In the detailed calculation Additional Sessions means total additional sessions estimated to
cards.html:
In the Scenario, the top stats Additional Sessions includes the extra traffic the Longtail Effect can bring.

In the detailed calculation Additional Sessions means total additional sessions estimated to be gained by working on that keyword, longtail effect included, and then the Longtail column shows how much exactly from that total the effect is contributing with.

cards.title: Is the Objective affected by adding or removing keywords?
cards.text: The Objective will take into account the keywords you have when setting it.However, when you remove keywords, you have a warning that looks like this: https://take.ms/iQws6When you remove keywords the
cards.html:
The Objective will take into account the keywords you have when setting it.

However, when you remove keywords, you have a warning that looks like this: https://take.ms/iQws6

When you remove keywords the actual traffic would no longer be the same, because if you're not working on the same keywords as estimated, the traffic will no longer be the same either.

cards.title: Is the SGE (AI Overview) considered for the % Clicks and the Forecast?
cards.text: YES.Like all other SERP features, the SGE is also considered when calculating the % Clicks on organic (SumCTR) and subsequently in the Forecast.[internal info]Its impact on the calculation is currentl
cards.html:
YES.

Like all other SERP features, the SGE is also considered when calculating the % Clicks on organic (SumCTR) and subsequently in the Forecast.


[internal info]
Its impact on the calculation is currently 25%, based largely on the values we had from PLAs (which are similar in size).

We must collect more data before calculating instead of estimating % Clicks with the AIO presence.
However, as it is not so widespread yet, it should not affect too much.

We’ll probably be able to assess its impact reliably in 2 months or so. [current month: June]
If we have GSC data on enough keywords with AIO and the search volume in Google Ads for the same month, in August, we could calculate the impact of the data collected for July.

More details on how each of the SERP features are discounted for the % Clicks calculation are in the link below:
 Sum CTR (discount) values for each SERP feature

cards.html:

  • solutia nu e simpla aici, cu atat mai mult, acesta nu este un task de mentenanta. Stim de problema, ea o sa mai apara daca criteriile de mai sus sunt indeplinite, dar nu se poate ceva rapid

  • ce pot face aici, daca mai apare problema, e sa ii sterg cuvintele din discover si sa incerce cu mai putine cuvinte.

cards.title: Knowledge base (articles)
cards.text: Types of articles:feature definition / how it works / FAQ (scurt): explanations, algorithms, metrics how to use it: steps, guide, actual functionality (clicks, hovers, etc)use case: how to use it prac
cards.html:
Types of articles:

  1. feature definition / how it works / FAQ (scurt): explanations, algorithms, metrics

  2. how to use it: steps, guide, actual functionality (clicks, hovers, etc)

  3. use case: how to use it practically, how to get insights (understanding)

Examples for the 3 above on a feature: - BCB:

  1. what it is, how we do it;

  2. how to set a target;

  3. how to win pitches.

General guidelines

  • short, concise, clear;

  • structure, structura, capitalize;

  • first to understand the "storyline", create the context;

  • no fluff: can we do without this? qualifiers, filler phrases, etc.

  • to be malicious with the competitors, to an extent :D

  • like we answer to a client, in a medium email, instead of conversational;

  • the articles have feedback! :D

  • the more the better, so an Operator can do the job without us.

Screenshots

  • clear, with arrows, etc;

  • choose the positive ones (more green than red);

  • choose the ones with full data: VS, traffic, etc;

  • use inspect element, if needed;

  • use large accounts: upswing (if we don't need kws - because they are in Romanian) or shopify, made, etc.

  • the price should always have this form: €x/month

BEGINING

  • the first paragraphs must keep them interested, not to be bored;

  • even if we like it, do no make the intrigue very long;

  • what is the pain point?

THE END

  1. for the first type >> as logical as possible

  2. for second type >> "what's next" links;

  3. for third type. >> either to "related" or to what we want them to read or to the catchy ones, so they'd read more.

>> we leave the user create by their own the necessary knowledge, if we manage to captivate them to read more articles

Updating articles

Whenever you have to update an article, please follow these 3 steps:

  1. Create a document in Google sheets in this folder and start writing. After finishing, inform the Manager to review it.

  2. After receiving the feedback and implementing it, update the article in Guru.

  3. After updating the Guru card, you can now also update the article in Intercom which goes public.

Never update directly the Intercom articles!

cards.title: LP Override (subdomain with main domain)
cards.text: We have the option to override the LPs in the Admin Section of the Account Settingsin LP Override you can use the following formula:  replace-domain:domain_to_replace.com:new_domain.comExample: replac
cards.html:
We have the option to override the LPs in the Admin Section of the Account Settings
in LP Override you can use the following formula: replace-domain:domain_to_replace.com:new_domain.com

Example: replace-domain:quote.salongold.co.uk:salon.gold.uk
The above example allows us to distribute the sessions from the subdomain (quote.etc) in the main domain.

We can extend the override to multiple domains as well.
a: subdomain 1
c: subdomain 2
b: main domain

Example: replace-domain:a:b, replace-domain:c:b

Exponential speed growth has a slower start with a significant increase towards the end of the timeframe, while linear maintains a constant growth.

IC convo: https://app.intercom.com/a/apps/yjda4i5x/inbox/inbox/3164900/conversations/26401656529

If you look in the (i) for this "Performance in time" indicator, you'll see that the linear and exponential options reflect the speed with which the target is obtained. This does not mean that the increase in traffic will be linear or exponential, but that the forecast is based on achieving the SEO goal in the given timeframe: Current state (0%) >> Desired state (100%).

The target you have set up (top 5 for the 1st-page keywords group and top 10 for 2nd-page keywords group) remains the same over the selected 12 months and this is what gives the “endpoint” for the forecast. We are seeing different growth curves, accordingly, from the current point (forecast start) to the end point.

You can crosscheck by analysing the area that the graph covers, below the dotted (improved) traffic line, for each of the two instances.

Later EDIT:

Over the same timeframe (be it 6 months or 12 or something else), the linear performance speed will make up for more traffic than the exponential one. (think of the area that the dotted graph has under it in each of the two cases)

Check here why Exonential is the default option.
