cards.title: Low Relevance label algorithm
cards.text: The calculation of keyword relevance happens only when adding the keyword, and it is based on the set of existing competitors.If the keyword is added when the campaign is created, the competitors on w
cards.html:
The calculation of keyword relevance happens only when adding the keyword, and it is based on the set of existing competitors.

If the keyword is added when the campaign is created, the competitors on which the relevance metric is based, are the initial competitors of the campaign. (check here for more details: https://app.getguru.com/card/TaKxAgyc/The-Low-Relevance-Label?q=relevance)

The algorithm is as follows:

  • we set the max relevance value, which is equal to 21 * number of competitors + 1

  • we calculate the relevancy for the keyword which is number of competitors * 21 - sum(21 - keyword rank on competitor/2)

    • if the competitor rank is >= 21, then the relevance score is maximum

  • We calculate the percentage by doing kw relevance/max relevance

  • the value is saved on the keyword database entry.

  • in the interface, the low relevance label is applied if all following conditions are true:

    • relevance percentage > 0.92

    • latest rank > 20

    • best rank > 20

    • keyword is "main" or not aggregated


-EXAMPLE-

Let's assume we have 1 keyword that we just added, and a pool of 3 competitors on which we want to calculate the relevance. The keyword's ranks on each competitor are : my_site ➝ 19, comp1 ➝ 15, comp2 ➝ 36, comp3 ➝ 99+

  • We pull the competitors and calculate the max relevance score which is 21 * (competitor count + 1) = 21 * (4 + 1) = 105

  • The formula for the keyword's low relevance score is as follows

    • competitor count * 21 - sum(21 - [rank/2 or rank or 21]) - we use rank/2 if it is my site, 21 if it is my site and rank >= 21 and rank if it is not my site

    • In this case, we need to do the following:

      • 4*21 - [(21 - 19/2) + (21 - 15) + (21-36) + (21-100)] = 106,5

  • We calculate the percentage

    • the value above/max_relevance - in this case: 106.5/105 = 1.01

  • If percentage > 0.92 and latest_rank > 20 and best_rank > 20 and the keyword is not a child in an aggregation group, then the low relevance label is applied.

cards.title: Magic Score Criteria
cards.text: The number of kws added by magic can differ from the estimated number given in wizard because we exclude irrelevant keywords. In order to determine if they are irrelevant, we need to process them so w
cards.html:
The number of kws added by magic can differ from the estimated number given in wizard because we exclude irrelevant keywords. In order to determine if they are irrelevant, we need to process them so we cannot know from the start the exact number of keywords that will be left.

The "magic score" is the estimated visits for that keyword, which is a function of the

  • keyword rank,
  • search volume
  • and impression rate.

The number of magic kws is limited to 1K. This information is also visible for the user, in the wizard.

cards.title: Magic keywords mechanism
cards.text: Faci magic (le poti lua din adwords/semrush etc.) iei cuvintele, le stockezi in db.La finalul procesarii, incepi sa faci cleanup - adica incerci sa stergi cuvintele care nu sunt relevante pt el. Sterg
cards.html:
• Faci magic (le poti lua din adwords/semrush etc.) iei cuvintele, le stockezi in db.

  • La finalul procesarii, incepi sa faci cleanup - adica incerci sa stergi cuvintele care nu sunt relevante pt el. Stergi din alea venite din magic keywords (adica din punctul anterior). Aici nu le stergi efectiv ci analizezi situatia. Ex: ai 1000 de cuvinte din magic, in cleanup ajungi la concluzia ca stergi 990 din ele, dar inca nu le stergi

  • Pe ex. meu, am 1000 de cuvinte - sa zicem ca sterg 990. raman cu 10. 10<50. Deci dupa presupusa stergere raman cu mai putin de 50 de cuvinte (inca nu le-am sters)

  • Atunci, ordonez cele 1000 de cuvinte dupa regula din task. Vindex desc, rank ASc, SV ASC. (vindex = o formula in functie de sv si rankul cuvantului)

  • din astea 1000, ordonate dupa algoritmul de mai sus -> las doar 50. Pe restul cuvintelor din magic le sterg, in afara de astea 50

https://app.clickup.com/t/3eh7vz

https://take.ms/9QFrp

cards.title: Maintenance Process v 6.0
cards.text: Maintenance Process - Started on 22 January 2020Who can add tasks and howThe CS department either directly from the Intercom conversation.Or directly from Cluckup if the request has come from a differ
cards.html:
Maintenance Process - Started on 22 January 2020

Who can add tasks and how

  1. The CS department either directly from the Intercom conversation.

Or directly from Cluckup if the request has come from a different communication channel.

First prioritization is made by the CS agent that adds the task. This agent will be assigned to the task throughout the entire process in order to also inform the client.

  1. Automatic tasks such as “Campaign blocked in processing” will be added automatically in the “To do” column. These tasks appear as being created by Raluca.

These are considered to be a top priority, this is why they have been added in this step in the process.

  1. DevOps for internal projects.

  2. Dev - if after fixing multiple tasks have observed an opportunity to fix the root problem.

Who estimates and prioritizes and when

Daily estimation and prioritization - 5:30 PM 30’ meeting.

The technical team lead (Vali) estimates and adds the info on the task and also assigns the dev that will be working on the task.

If the tech team lead doesn’t know how to estimate or has some questions regarding that task, he will escalate and ask for help in estimating and finding the best dev to solve the task.

Then, the CS Manager (Raluca) and the tech team lead (Vali) decide which tasks will be done the next day having in mind the daily and weekly hourly budget.

Hourly maintenance budget: 30h/week - 6h/day

If while estimating the tech team lead and the CS manager observes there are more tasks with the same issue, they group them.

Who can work on tasks

Any dev that is available and has the knowledge to solve the task.

Platform

Mihai N.

Tavi

Vali

Marius

Dragos

Data Studio

Andrei P.

Topics

Mihai Stefan

Signals

Liviu

The dev that works on the task has to log the time.

Types of tasks

We’re still using this prioritization.

Critical:

SLA 48h - from adding to backlog to verifying

Priority in Clickup: Urgent

Due date on task: 48h

Major:

SLA

Priority in Clickup: High

Minor:

Priority in Clickup: Normal

The structure of the task

Internal pre-release notification required - check if

Was it reported in a Trial account? - check if a Trial has mentioned the issue and have it as priority 0

Is a code review necessary? (it will be marked in by the team lead)

Impact

Steps to reproduce

Expected behaviour

Extra info (*optional*)

Stages a task can go through

Backlog:

  • All types of tasks are added in the backlog

  • Newly created tasks

  • Tasks that have been moved because they are waiting for info from the client or internally

  • Tasks that have been moved from Verify because the fix wasn’t correct

  • Tasks that have been moved from “To do” because they haven’t been finalized and the dev isn’t working on them anymore

  • News tasks that have appeared after solving the initial task.

To Do:

  • Tasks that have been estimated and prioritized

In Progress:

  • Tasks that are in progress

Verify:

  • Tasks that have been solved

CS SLA:

  • 15min for blockers/crisis tasks - an automated email will be sent to customer.service@seomonitor.com informing that the task has been moved to Verify and a CS agent will have to check if the issue has been solved (we still need to iterate on this)

  • 24h for the rest

Closed:

  • Tasks that have been verified by CS and confirmed that the fix is up and running.

Metrics (KPIs dashboards)

Critical tasks SLA: 48h (end-to-end)

CS Verify tasks: 24h

CS Verify Critical task: 15min

SLA/week

Out of SLA Avg.

#4 iteration

Always set start date on tasks

Start date - due date

If task with waiting for info, we receive the info and then we change the start date to present

Analyse all out of SLA tasks

Backup for devs found in

Devs that know that the issue is going to be solved in >48h

If an issue is solved by devs simply inform the client

Waiting for info for more than 2 days

Deprioritize

Change label and start date

After the client answers - change the start date

#5 iteration

Am creat label-uri noi pe care va rog sa le utilizati accordingly:

waiting for info - intern

waiting for info - client

If a task is in waiting for info >2 days we only change the start date.

back to backlog - in momentul in care intoarcem un task din verify in backlog

incident related - in cazul in care task-ul e afectat de un incident; se adauga pe task-uri numai de devi;

#6 iteration

The “Verify task” notifications in Intercom are now live.

Checking the task doesn’t have to be done instantly but in a timely manner, so that we respect the 48h SLA for critical tasks.

If the task is not critical, you can take longer.

Leave note in the Intercom conversation with your action: “checked/I’ll check it in xmin.”

The tasks that have been created by Devs still have to be verified by them and moved to Closed.

#7 iteration

When we have >10 critical tasks in backlog, we inform Dragos

There will be a spike in maintenance for them.

#8 iteration

Tasks coming from leads, trials and demo accounts will be prioritized by Mada/Raluca and confirmed if they are indeed critical

#9 iteration

There will be a spike in maintenance after a bank holiday from now on, whenever needed.

The rule with "at 10 critical, Dragos will be informed (notification)" remains.

There will also be tasks on the (public) incidents.

#10 iteration

Tag “couldn’t reproduce” is in place.

We’ll add them to tasks where we/devs couldn’t reproduce the issue and keep an eye on them in the CS KPIs Dashboard.

#11 iteration

If a recurring bug appears, create the task and tag it with: "repeating bug"

Link all the previous tasks to this one.

Have a report in Maintenance with these tasks.

Each week, during the maintenance meeting, discuss each task and decide what needs to be done/changed

Tasks will then be closed and moved accordingly.

cards.title: Maintenance Tasks Prioritization Rules
cards.text: URGENT (ex critical)To Be Analyzed in 24hTo Be Done in 48hDue date - This gets added automatically if you add the "defect" tag, if it doesn't get added, select two days from now with the exclusion of
cards.html:
URGENT (ex critical)

  • To Be Analyzed in 24h

  • To Be Done in 48h

  • Due date - This gets added automatically if you add the "defect" tag, if it doesn't get added, select two days from now with the exclusion of weekends, National holidays or company-wide vacations.

  • No need for Product Input (unless the devs ask for it)

WHAT TASKS ARE CONSIDERED URGENT?

  • App availability issues - login issues, downtime, E500, signup issues, can't move past wizard

  • Ranking issues - no ranks for today, discrepancies between the tool and the SERP

  • Blocked (initial) processing campaigns

  • Visibility, keywords processing issues

  • A functional bug from our trial clients - they have a limited amount of time to check the platform

NOTE: The bugs that affect ALL the users should be urgent and sent to the dev-app slack channel with tags for the developers as well.

HIGH (ex major)

  • To Be Analyzed in 24h

  • To Be Done in 7 working days

  • Due date - This gets added automatically if you add the "defect" tag, if it doesn't get added, select seven days from now with the exclusion of weekends, National holidays or company-wide vacations

  • No need for Product Input (unless the devs ask for it)

WHAT TASKS ARE CONSIDERED HIGH?

  • Anything that affects the understanding and learning of the platform. (tooltips, popups)

  • Other data reliability and integrity issues (other than the ones in critical: besides the visibility, ranking and blocked campaigns) - non-time-sensitive aspects, f.x. traffic.

  • Any functionality not working at all or certain abnormally (buttons, charts, etc)

NORMAL (ex minor)

  • Moved to the Backlog Column by Product

WHAT TASKS ARE CONSIDERED NORMAL?

  • Feature requests

  • Nice to have requests

  • Minor issues that don't affect the usage of the platform

  • TASKS ARE ADDED IN THE REPORTED ISSUES COLUMN FOR EVERY SPECIFIC BOARD

  • THEY ARE MOVED IN TO DO >>> IN PROGRESS >>> VERIFY - ONLY BY THE DEVS

  • ONCE IN VERIFY, THEY ARE CHECKED BY QA AND MOVED TO COMPLETED

  • ONCE IN COMPLETED THEY ARE CHECKED AND CLOSED BY CS IF THEY'RE CLIENT RELATED TASKS OR BY THE DEVS/QA FOR THE SYSTEM RELATED TASKS

  • THE DEV-OPS TASKS ARE ADDED IN THE REPORTED ISSUES COLUMN, PRIORITIZED BY THE DEVS AND CHECKED BY CS (IF CLIENT RELATED) ONCE THEY'RE IN COMPLETED.

cards.title: Maximum limit for keywords to track
cards.text: ​From a technical point of view, tracking more than 100K keywords in a single campaign would not be something we encourage, as we cannot guarantee a good experience with that many keywords. At least f
cards.html:
​From a technical point of view, tracking more than 100K keywords in a single campaign would not be something we encourage, as we cannot guarantee a good experience with that many keywords. At least for the time being, though we are always working on improving.

From a group/subgroup perspective (the keyword research and organisation itself), it might also be easier to have them spread over multiple campaigns. For example, some of our clients have separated the campaigns across the different site categories.

cards.title: Media value and CPC display
cards.text: The Media value is displayed based on the keywords list of that selected timeframe.Later Edit (from tech): i.e, it doesn't take into consideration the calendar per say but rather the pool of keywords
cards.html:
The Media value is displayed based on the keywords list of that selected timeframe.

Later Edit (from tech): i.e, it doesn't take into consideration the calendar per say but rather the pool of keywords that was tracked during that period.
Example: if there are no changes on the campaign (keywords added or deleted) the values won't change if you change the dates in the calendar but if you add the campaign in March and then add additional keywords in June and compare the timeframes from March and June, you will see a difference in display.


The value may be different between all keywords, smart groups and groups, as the keywords in each group may change

Can be shared with customers.

For the smart groups - only the last 30 days will be taken into consideration.

Media Value is the monthly cost that should be consumed in GoogleAds to get the estimated current traffic generated by the monitoring keywords in that group. That is, the estimated monthly traffic * avg. CPC. The estimated monthly traffic generated by the monitored words is the sum of the estimated monthly traffic for each keyword. The current monthly traffic generated by a keyword is estimated based on your current rank and search volume. Each rank has an estimated CTR, and by multiplying that CTR with the search volume, the estimated traffic will be calculated.

cards.text: The Title and Description will no longer be updated for keywords that are on 99+Because, they no longer appear in the SERP, they will remain with the same title and description they had before droppin

The Title and Description will no longer be updated for keywords that are on 99+
Because, they no longer appear in the SERP, they will remain with the same title and description they had before dropping on 99+ when they were present in the SERP.

cards.title: Migration procedure (updated February 2024)
cards.text: SLA: 7 days maxMost recent duration estimates (Feb'24) at CS:5' / campaign for the migration3' / campaign for the checksMost recent tracker (C1'24) - here:with 4h Dev tasks.Initial CommunicationReques
cards.html:
SLA: 7 days max

Most recent duration estimates (Feb'24) at CS:

  • 5' / campaign for the migration
  • 3' / campaign for the checks

Most recent tracker (C1'24) - here:

  • with 4h Dev tasks.

Initial Communication

  • Request coming in through Sales (new client) or CS (existing clients).
  • CS conducts a 15-minute call to explain the procedure.
  • Prerequisite(s) for the call and the migration:
    • active subscription with SEOmonitor,
    • the tool they're migrating from,
    • how many campaigns and keywords,
    • devices.
  • CS sends a follow-up email summarizing the discussion and includes a recording of the call (for future reference).
Clients may provide CSVs directly or authorize API access.

Migration via CSV (directly by the CS team)

  1. File checks
    • The file names should include the exact URL and location.
    • Data contained: keywords, ranks, dates, and groups (also known as tags in other tools).
      • The frequency of the ranks (dates) does not impact the migration process overall.
      • But if less than daily (i.e. weekly or monthly), the historical Visibility will be affected.
    • Separate CSVs for desktop and mobile.
      • If only one CSV is available, desktop ranks will be uploaded for mobile.
    • Check received CSVs for accuracy (multiple dates, wrong ranks, etc).
    • Curate CSVs, if necessary, to meet migration requirements; for example, add a delimiter if there are no templates, or clean up the rows, as we have a 10k row limit.
    • If the CSV is larger than 100 MB (for example, we know STAT has large CSVs) or there are several CSVs for the same campaign, upload them as a zip archive.
  2. Migration
    • Access the owner's account, with impersonate from SMadmin.
    • Go to Campaign Settings and add /migrations at the end of the URL, like this: https://app.seomonitor.com/v2/account/company/migrations.
    • Click on Import Campaign.
    • Select the corresponding template, or manually map the CSVs, if there's no template.
    • Upload each individual CSV.
    • Check the import and the campaign.
      • check if the ranks are imported altogether,
      • plus take some random keywords, if you suspect any issue.
  3. Inform the client once done.
    1. if all files have been sent at once, inform them after it's fully done.
    2. if they send the files in batches, confirm per batch.

Migration via API (CS team through Dev team)

This is NOT preferable, as it can cost them extra, and other tools can have more limits via API.
  • what tool do they need to extract from,
  • credentials: username and password,
  • number of domains and keywords,
  • how much historical data,
  • task in clickup for Devs.

2021 update: obsolete as of the above 2024 ones

FOR INTERNAL PURPOSES ONLY:

STEPS TO FOLLOW:

  1. Add the user's email address and name from here. Ask the user to fill in the first two columns from the blueprint by making sure to use the provided URL as shown here.
  2. After user has confirmed that he added all the campaign URLs and markets, sync data from here.
  3. Then go back to the user and ask him/her to upload the CSV documents with the keywords & ranks for each of the devices. *NOTE: the CSVs they upload should contain only the necessary info: ranks, date, keywords and/or groups (bearing in mind that if a keyword should be present in more than 1 group, you can add all the names of the groups in the same cell separated by comma, slash or semicolon).
  4. The next step is for the blueprint to be populated both with the links to the files and the data found in those files (the yellow area).
  5. After this, the client needs to check the accuracy of the data we found and validate it accordingly by double clicking in the cell and selecting YES/NO.
  6. Following a positive validation, we need to click on "Import" here and follow the steps from the tool.
  7. After importing the first campaign the rest of the campaigns will be done automatically, since this is basically the template for the migration. Once this is done, you'll see the green area of the blueprint being populated, these wili be the data we'll be showing in the app.
  8. After this step, we'll need to check if there are any differences between the yellow area and the green one and validate it.
  9. If this validation has been a positive one from the CS side, the client will also have to do a final validation.
  10. The last step, after the client's validation, is closing the migration from SEOadmin.

FOR CLIENT ONLY, TEMPLATE FOR THE FIRST EMAIL SENT

This is the first step when doing a migration, here's a template to help you send the first email.

Getting back to you with the details regarding the migration process as promised:

  • initially you will receive 2 emails from us that will give you access to a folder in drive, which is the actual interface from your end, for the migration of the campaigns (you can find the emails in your inbox already).
  • the first step you need to do is to fill in the first two columns in the blueprint you see in that folder (in order to select the Google market, please use the provided URL and copy clipboard exactly as it is there).
  • once you finished filling in the blueprint, please get back to me to confirm this, so I can update the info on our end.
  • after I've updated it, you can go in the 'Import file' folder, where you can find new folders for each of the campaigns you added in the blueprint;
  • you then go in turn, into each of the folders for the campaigns, and upload the CSV documents with the keywords & ranks for each of the devices that you've exported from the previous tool (STAT in this case). We recommend that the CSVs you upload, contain only the necessary info: ranks, date, keywords and/or groups (bear in mind that if a keyword should be present in more than 1 group, you can add all the names of the groups in the same cell (separated by comma, slash or semicolon).
  • once you've uploaded all the CSVs, simply go back to the Blueprint and on the 'Client Validation' column select from the drop-down yes or no, as a final ok, before we migrate the provided data.
  • after positively validating the migration of the campaigns in the blueprint, please let me know, so I can move the process in its final phase.
  • after a couple of checks on my end, I will get back to you so you can also validate everything.

During this process you will be able to see and access these campaigns in your account, but please bear in mind that you shouldn't modify them until the process is finalized.

cards.title: Migration tool .Zip uploads
cards.text: The migration tool now supports uploading ZIP archives. They can contain multiple CSV files or just one archived large file.There are a couple of caveats on importing ZIP files:ZIPs MUST:Be ZIP archiv
cards.html:
The migration tool now supports uploading ZIP archives. They can contain multiple CSV files or just one archived large file.

There are a couple of caveats on importing ZIP files:

  1. ZIPs MUST:
    1. Be ZIP archives (no other archive format is supported);
    2. Only contain CSV files;
    3. Have the CSV files in the root of the archive (no folders, sub-folders, nothing).
  2. CSVs MUST:
    1. Have a valid CSV format;
    2. Have the header on the first row;
    3. Only contain text (as in not binary data);
    4. Be encoded in UTF-8 (no UTF-16 support);
    5. Have enclosures (cells are surrounded by enclosing characters - quotes are strongly recommended) if they have non-standard delimiters (eg.: Tabs, Semicolons, any-other-character-some-creative-n*tjob-thought-it-would-be-cool-to-use-as-a-delimiter).
  3. Even though you can reduce huge CSV files to a few MB by archiving them, please take into account that the migration processes still need to go through their entire content and might crash for large amount of data, so eliminate as much unnecessary data from them as you can.

After uploading one of the files, wait for the validation to finish (there's no visual indicator, but it's happening behind the scenes).

Continue with uploading the second file only after/if you get the "validated" message for the first (it can take up to 2-3min for large files).

cards.title: Migrations - how to avoid the Invalid keywords problem.
cards.text: The "invalid keywords" issue appears when the data exported from the other tool is not encoded as UTF-8.Even if you later save it as a UTF-8, the original CSV not saved correctly from the other tool w
cards.html:
The "invalid keywords" issue appears when the data exported from the other tool is not encoded as UTF-8.
Even if you later save it as a UTF-8, the original CSV not saved correctly from the other tool will cause this issue because it will double some special characters that cannot be noticed in a larger pool of keywords.

We now have a new checkbox in the Migration Tool wizard on step 1 (Keyword Column). It is named "Convert UTF8 to Win1252" and if checked it will perform one additional operation on the keywords text fixing this exact issue.

  • THERE'S NO WAY TO TELL IF IT'S CORRECTLY SAVED BUT IF WE GET THE INVALID KEYWORDS ERROR IN THE PARSED FILES WE CAN APPLY THIS CHECKBOX

DO NOT USE THIS CHECKBOX FOR REGULAR CSVs AS IT MIGHT AFFECT THE ENGLISH CHARACTERS.

cards.html:

cards.title: New site checks
cards.text: List (<5k visits): here; Full list: here.Responsible: any CS (+ involve Mada if issues or high profile)(Normal) flow: Sheet 1Should be done right when the site is added.If still processing (traffic or
cards.html:
List (<5k visits): here; Full list: here.

Responsible: any CS (+ involve Mada if issues or high profile)

(Normal) flow: Sheet 1

  • Should be done right when the site is added.

  • If still processing (traffic or keywords), do what you can at the moment and come back later for the rest of the checks.

  • If unsure about anything, mark “In progress at CS” and ask.

  • If the same problem occurs over a few different accounts, just one to be “sent to Trello” and report the others SIDs in the same task.

  • If something recurring impacts the first impression (trials) or they can’t work (clients), raise the red flag and ask! You could be noticing something very important!

  • You can also switch to the ones with less than 10K visits every now and then, just in case any large site is being pitched and we might miss it.

  • For content, check in Ahrefs (radu@upswing.ro >> Radu@SEOmonitor): Backlinks >> New backlinks >> last 30 days

  • Is done by CS

  • Status linked to Bosulica

  • 3 options for CS/admin

Steps:

  • Check account for “site-id - number”

  • Check Unallocated percentag

    • GA vs GSC analysis

    • -> recommendations

    • Or

    • -> CS corrects LP, queries, HP

  • Check Conversion type

  • Check if brand name is correct - Also filter any brand misspellings from the non-brand OT

  • Check SEO opportunities

  • Check Relevant high opportunity kw

  • Check Auto-detected articles - Just for large sites or big brands, where there should be articles.

  • Check if Traffic charts is ok - Including all mini-charts

  • Check that All core indicators are shown

cards.title: New situation or corrections
cards.text: (questions, issues, etc.)When faced with something that feels completely new to you...First check in Guru, in multiple ways, to make sure that there is nothing related to that already posted or a rele
cards.html:
(questions, issues, etc.)

When faced with something that feels completely new to you...

First check in Guru, in multiple ways, to make sure that there is nothing related to that already posted or a relevant guideline for how to proceed.

Then ask your colleagues, maybe they have extra info.

Only after being sure this is a new situation, escalate to manager, to guide you from there.

Tag the manager in the conversation, with a brief comment on the issue/question at hand, as you understand it. If advised so (by manager), you can (also) tag Cosmin in the conversation.

Manager researches - with Cosmin (and the devs, if needed).

You get a solution or advice on what to tell the client.

This should be documented in Guru right away and sent for Verification to manager


* If any of the replies through Intercom is unsatisfactory and you get advised on how to better answer or follow up, document this in Guru.

cards.title: Non-ranking keywords retuned in lists they don't belong to - Website Explorer - non-db market
cards.text: For the campaigns set on non-database markets, the data for the Website Explorer comes from SERPstat.Sometimes it can happen that in the Overlapping list there are keywords the tracked website doesn't
cards.html:
For the campaigns set on non-database markets, the data for the Website Explorer comes from SERPstat.

Sometimes it can happen that in the Overlapping list there are keywords the tracked website doesn't rank for or in the Non-Overlapping list there are keywords for which the researched website doesn't rank for.

This is the raw data SERPstat give us, and we don't have control over what they show. We could try to filter it, but it might cause bugs and inconsistencies.

A workaround for these cases would be for user to save those keywords in a list in Research to get accurate data and monitor it monthly at no extra cost.

cards.title: Owner A​ccount blocked
cards.text: I​f a user leaves the company and their email address is set as an "owner" account, once this user's access is blocked (the user is removed) they cannot login anymore with that email address. By doing
cards.html:
I​f a user leaves the company and their email address is set as an "owner" account, once this user's access is blocked (the user is removed) they cannot login anymore with that email address.

By doing this we ensure all the other users will continue to have access to the company account.

The account is not deleted but it will be set with "Owner Removed" and this will be visible in Admin. We can still access it with Impersonate but not login with Materpass.

cards.title: O​rganic Traffic analyses - different scenarios
cards.text: When there is Unallocated traffic in the campaign higher than 30% (our generic rule of thumb is that if it’s below 30%, it’s good enough) there might be some adjustments that can be made to reduce it.
cards.html:
When there is Unallocated traffic in the campaign higher than 30% (our generic rule of thumb is that if it’s below 30%, it’s good enough) there might be some adjustments that can be made to reduce it.

Accessing the Misconfiguration you might be able to identify these issues:

W​e have pages that can be solved directly by the client in their Google Analytics account:

→ The “not set” page: portugalstore.fpf.pt/(not set)/ can be solved in Google Analytics by following the steps from the Google Article.

→ Also, pages like: portugalstore.fpf.pt/pt/checkout/shipping/ or portugalstore.fpf.pt/pt/checkout/success/ could be filtered directly from Analytics; they will most likely not show up in Search Console at any time, because they are not accessed as a result of an organic google search (one can see here that they are not indexed).

Another few ways to reduce the Unallocated are:

→ Tracking sub-domain traffic data:

The page: aeg.com.pt/search/, which shows up with 63 sessions, is not indexed in Google.

There is a subdomain with shop.aeg.com.pt that shows up on that query. In this case if you need to track the subdomain traffic data as well, you'll need to connect a GA with data on both domains & the corresponding GSC profiles.

→ The landing pages that cannot be matched because they contain a query parameter, in this case "modelo" :

Can be distributed by a custom setting in the Organic Traffic Settings:

→ Advanced → Remove query params: modelo.

This will distribute the traffic from them into the main /configurator page and it would fix these 96 pages and their 781 sessions.

cards.title: Partner Dashboard
cards.text: When a user is also getting commission (Partner or Affiliate), they get access to a special "Partner Dashboard" in the Settings menu, containing all of the corresponding details. Each Partner/Affiliat
cards.html:
When a user is also getting commission (Partner or Affiliate), they get access to a special "Partner Dashboard" in the Settings menu, containing all of the corresponding details.

Each Partner/Affiliate has one or more promo code(s) that will be used by their clients/leads. This promo code needs to be used right when the leads open their SEOmonitor account (and not afterwards). If the code is used at a later date, no retroactive comissions will be issued

[internal note] If the code is added at a later time and the affiliate/partner asks for a recalculation of the commission due, a maintenance task is to be created (that calls for a script developed by Vali).

There are 2 ways of claiming the comissions:

  1. they can withdraw it by uploading an invoice in the Partner Dashboard section --> the user has to click the Withdraw button and upload the invoice with the exact value of the Unclaimed Commission.

  2. our recommendation (and preferred option) for them is to opt for getting the unclaimed commissions as a discount for their future invoice(s); it would be added as "promo credits" through Chargebee.

[Internal note] all invoices uploaded by our partners are sent to Loredana. She can check if the invoice is correct and can give an estimated date for the payment.

The most common error on the invoice are the company details. The invoice must be issued to Bunt Studio, complete details below:

Bunt Studio SRL

5-7 Dimitrie Pompeiu Bvd, 2nd district, Bucharest

VAT: RO21181716

​Additional info (in case they request it): Bank Name- Banca Transilvania

IBAN EUR - RO64BTRL04704202E03204XX

SWIFT code - BTRLRO22

cards.text: Only requests coming via the email address that asks for it.And always send the new pass to that email address (not in the chat).First ask for a new password to be generated & tell them to check junk/
cards.html:
Only requests coming via the email address that asks for it.

And always send the new pass to that email address (not in the chat).

  1. First ask for a new password to be generated & tell them to check junk/spam.

  2. Log in with their email address + master pass.

  3. Then "log in as user".

  4. Go to Account Settings.

  5. Old pass: current master password.

  6. New pass model: DayoftheweekXX, for example Wednesday10.

cards.title: Performance in Competition
cards.text: We can't calculate the performance for competitors.When calculating the Visibility Performance you don't take into account the baseline changes (deleting kws etc.), you compare the final value with th
cards.html:
We can't calculate the performance for competitors.

When calculating the Visibility Performance you don't take into account the baseline changes (deleting kws etc.), you compare the final value with the initial one.

But this can be done only for your kws, not for your competitors.

This is why the correct terms are:

Visibility Trend = for competitors

Visibility Performance = for me

cards.html:
When you "Mark as Done" a pinned/saved signals card, it turns grey and gets "archived" in the same list as the other card that are saved but not done yet.


If you want to completely remove a saved/pinned signals card, simply un-mark it as done, go to the cards that are not saved from "Top Insights" and un-save the one that you wish to completely remove.

cards.html:

cards.text: Customer Serviceup to 500 EUR/ 2 mthsOnboardingresurrection (max 3 mths/ 1000EUR)﻿Salesthe value of the ongoing subscription in other toolscredit as a sales tool max 1000 EURProductdefine it as it com
cards.html:
Customer Service

Onboarding

  • resurrection (max 3 mths/ 1000EUR)﻿

Same ChargeBee steps we’ve been applying.

cards.title: Rank Tracker - Best Rank Warning/Star Conditions
cards.text: The conditions for the way the warning icons and stars are added are the following:Warning tooltip:If the current rank is in top 10, and more than 5 positions away from the all-time best rank.If the c
cards.html:
The conditions for the way the warning icons and stars are added are the following:

Warning tooltip:

  • If the current rank is in top 10, and more than 5 positions away from the all-time best rank.
  • If the current rank is in top 11-20, and more than 10 positions away from the all-time best rank.
  • If the current rank is in top 21-99, and more than 30 positions away from the all-time best rank.

Bolded Star

  • appears when the best rank ever achieved (while tracked with SEOmonitor) is happening right now

No Warning/Star:

  • appears when the keyword's best rank has been 10 or below (10 to 99+) and never was in top 10

cards.title: Rank change and Rank change absolute in the new Rank Tracker v2
cards.text: Rank change absolute can be obtained by using combined filters.For example, if you want to see a rank change (trend change in the keyword table) bigger than 3 positions be it positive or negative (abs
cards.html:
• Rank change absolute can be obtained by using combined filters.
    For example, if you want to see a rank change (trend change in the keyword table) bigger than 3 positions be it positive or negative (absolute 3), you can use this filter:

And the result would be this:

  • Rank change - the operator functions mathematically so if you select a rank change lower than 3, you will be getting every change (trend change in the keyword table) lower than the number 3 - this includes changes of +1 or +2 as they are, mathematically speaking, lower than 3.

    If you'd like to see just keywords that had rank drops lower than 3 and more you can use the rank change lower than -3 which will give you the results of all keywords that dropped 3 or more positions:

cards.title: Ranks related messages
cards.text: "For any SERP changes, we do have automatic processes in place, to show us anomalies in the SERPs and proceed with fixing them immediately. So this would be just "business as usual”: when we’d detect
cards.html:
"For any SERP changes, we do have automatic processes in place, to show us anomalies in the SERPs and proceed with fixing them immediately. So this would be just "business as usual”: when we’d detect huge increases or drops in the number of keywords in our database that have a specific SERP feature or sudden drops in ranks, or when we'd detect known issues on specific features (for example, keywords like "restaurants near me" no longer featuring a map). Due to the nature of the process, SERP features and the page "reading" are prone to error, as Google often changes the HTML of the SERP."

"These changes in the page don't all roll out at the same time: different SERP features get updated at different times, even different combinations of features, different markets, and so on. We have multiple systems in place to ensure both that "all ranks ready daily at 8AM" (99%) SLA is met and that these ranks have full integrity (are correct). However, there are thresholds that trigger our alarms for what seem to be updates, and sometimes they are not met. So we manually go in and update the systems as soon as we or you guys notice anything off. We know this isn't perfect, and we're always improving to get as close to that as we can."

cards.title: Reactivate account from Chargebee
cards.text: Whenever we reactivate an account and we don't want to bill the customer on the spot and send the newly generated invoice, please select the option "Reactivate as trial". Another way to do this withou
cards.html:
Whenever we reactivate an account and we don't want to bill the customer on the spot and send the newly generated invoice, please select the option "Reactivate as trial".

Another way to do this without sending the invoice, would be to delete the email address and set Autocollection to OFF.

In case we do want to generate the invoice at the same time we reactivate the account, then we don't use the trial option upon reactivation.

cards.title: Reactivate subscription when the credit is not yet paid
cards.text: When you reactivate a subscription that got cancelled because it wasn't paid, there will be a message saying a certain amount will be invoiced.https://take.ms/tDrFyBut this will not be the case, becau
cards.html:
When you reactivate a subscription that got cancelled because it wasn't paid, there will be a message saying a certain amount will be invoiced.
https://take.ms/tDrFy

But this will not be the case, because you will select the option "add to unbilled charges".
And once the subscription reactivated, you will delete the unbilled charges that will get created.

Subsequent to cashing the credit, you will manually invoice the consumption for the unbilled period.
This will mean either or both the value of:
- any invoice that was issued related to the prepaid subscription and voided pending for the credit to be paid
- any unbilled consumption related to the period since the prepaid subscription started up to the most recent billing date

To issue the invoice first select the subscription by clicking on "view details", then "add one-time charges or add-ons" (right sidebar)
https://take.ms/SmF66
https://take.ms/xPvjd

Make sure next billing date is in accordance with the billing cicle.

cards.title: Research Database
cards.text: We have a vast database of keywords, coming from different sources (over the years), from multiple markets. We currently serve 4 main markets: US, UK, RO, FR. For the other markets, we are just servin
cards.html:
We have a vast database of keywords, coming from different sources (over the years), from multiple markets.

We currently serve 4 main markets: US, UK, RO, FR.

For the other markets, we are just serving monthly tracking for the user's keywords, using the Keyword Vault

Talking about keyword research, they are organized by domain and landing page. The ranking data comes from the Google SERP, and the search volumes from Google Ads.

The algorithm for topical relevance (how we're showing related keywords) is based on a few factors, including the rank, the number of landing page occurrences in the pool of results, and some mathematical calculations to order the top results.
We exclude keywords based on (low) search volume, aggregations, brand status, and misspellings.

__________

Internal only

Sources: Google Ads suggestions, traffic data, DataForSEO.

cards.title: Resources added throughout the month
cards.text: If the client is tracking a certain number of resources and then during the month they add more and keep them, at the beginning of the next billing cycle they will pay for the current resources, inclu
cards.html:
If the client is tracking a certain number of resources and then during the month they add more and keep them, at the beginning of the next billing cycle they will pay for the current resources, including the new added ones + the ones added mid cycle.

For example:

At the start of the current billing cycle the client paid for 5 campaigns.

At some point, during the cycle, they added an extra 3 campaigns. They used these 3 campaigns (for which they did not pay at the beginning of the month) for the rest of the month.

At the start of the next billing cycle they will have to pay for the 8 currently active campaigns + the 3 campaigns that were used during the current billing cycle, but not paid for at the beginning of the month.

8+3=11

cards.title: Restoring keywords from Trash
cards.text: When keywords are restored from the Trash folder, a group has to be selected for the action to be completed.There isn't an automatic option to restore the keywords to the previously existing groups, e
cards.html:
When keywords are restored from the Trash folder, a group has to be selected for the action to be completed.
There isn't an automatic option to restore the keywords to the previously existing groups, even if those are still in the campaign.

cards.title: Resurrection offer for customers that just closed their account
cards.text: Whenever a customer closes the account, CS gets an Intercom (IC) notification that the account has been canceled.The steps to be done are as follows:check their subscription value and how many resourc
cards.html:
Whenever a customer closes the account, CS gets an Intercom (IC) notification that the account has been canceled.

The steps to be done are as follows:

  • check their subscription value and how many resources they were actually using (if on fixed plans)

  • check IC conversations of all users in the account --> see if they had any feature requests, any complaints, etc.

  • check Salesmachine to see how active they were (as an account, not individual users)

  • offer them 2 or 3x the current consumption in the form of promo credits, that can already be added to the canceled subscription, in Chargebee (CB). Once they reactivate the account, the upcoming CB invoice will be deducted from the promo credits.

Inform them through in-app or email. Also mention any new or up-and-coming features to keep an eye on.

cards.title: SEO Opportunity Score with Explainer
cards.text: newest Help article - https://help.seomonitor.com/en/articles/6222130-seo-opportunityIt is a keyword-level metric that brings all the qualitative and quantitative attributes of a keyword in one metric
cards.html:
newest Help article - https://help.seomonitor.com/en/articles/6222130-seo-opportunity

It is a keyword-level metric that brings all the qualitative and quantitative attributes of a keyword in one metric that helps users sort their keywords based on the opportunity to start optimizing their website for them right now. It works as a percentile, taking values from 0 to 10, where 10 represents the top 10% and 1 represents the bottom 10%. The attributes are:

  • Search Volume

  • Year-over-Year Search Trend

  • % of searches that end up clicking on an organic result (SERP features impact)

  • CTR difference between the current rank and Top3 

  • Difficulty of the website to rank in top 10

How it works: 

  • The metric is calculated real-time and uses all the keyword attributes in SEOmonitor to process 2 key metrics:

    • Potential new monthly clicks when reaching Top 3

    • Difficulty to rank in top 10

  • Potential new monthly clicks =(CTR[1-3] - CTR[current_rank]) * avg_search_volume * YoY * %clicks_on_organic

  • Opportunity Score = Percentile([Potential new monthly clicks] / [Difficulty to rank in Top10])/10 - calculated relative to all keywords in the campaign

Anything else about it:

  • It is also processed at a keyword group level.

    • Group Opportunity Score:  average(Opportunity Score)

  • The metric has an explainer that describes the Difficulty metric value and the

    • Estimated additional Sessions, Conversions and Revenue when reaching the top (if Analytics is connected)

    • Estimated additional Clicks when reaching the top (if only GSC is connected)

  • The Opportunity score is not calculated for misleading keywords with warning label:  brands of others, irrelevant keywords, highly localized keywords -> N/A with tooltip

  • The Opportunity Score for keywords already in Top 3 is 0, as there's no more room to grow, and it is highlighted with a Trophy icon.

cards.title: SERP Download Date Difference
cards.text: This is intended this way and it's internal information that should NOT be shared with the users.We skip crawling for some keywords such as close variants and the ones as shown in the algorithm here.W
cards.html:
This is intended this way and it's internal information that should NOT be shared with the users.

We skip crawling for some keywords such as close variants and the ones as shown in the algorithm here.

When this happens, users will see the SERP download shown as from today's date but we will see the real one when downloading if we are logged in with masterpass.



If ever in doubt, use "impersonate" to see if it's functioning as above.

We only keep SERPs for 20 days, and if you're trying to download one that's missing, the SERP download endpoint logic goes like this:

  • it tries to go a few days back and serve you the first one available before the date you requested

  • if all else fails, it serves you the latest one we have

cards.title: SERP download expected behaviour on the client side - INTERNAL PURPOSE ONLY
cards.text: NOT TO BE SHARED WITH THE USERSFirst, we attempt the day they want (aka current day), let's say the 28th. We try to validate that the position on that SERP matches with our DB and what they see as the
cards.html:
NOT TO BE SHARED WITH THE USERS


First, we attempt the day they want (aka current day), let's say the 28th.
We try to validate that the position on that SERP matches with our DB and what they see as the latest position.

If not, we move to the previous day, in our case, 27.
If nothing is available, we move down all the way to the 23rd, attempting to find a matching SERP.
If we don't have anything for the previous day, we then try to validate the next day, in our case, 29.

So, in extreme case when the client might end up getting tomorrow's SERP, it should actually match what they see in the platform.

*From this task.

cards.title: Subdomains and (sub)folders: What's the difference?
cards.text: Subdomains and subfolders are similar in a lot of ways. They’re both file locations inside of a server’s ‘Home’ directory structure and the Home directory for our purposes happens to be a URL.While bo
cards.html:
Subdomains and subfolders are similar in a lot of ways. They’re both file locations inside of a server’s ‘Home’ directory structure and the Home directory for our purposes happens to be a URL.

While both are content repositories, subdomains are also URLs which means you can access them just like a regular website address.

They’re not just paths within a domain like subfolders are.

What's a "subdomain"?

A subdomain is a partition of a website that operates almost as an independent entity. It is distinct from the rest of a website and typically, its address is to the left of the root URL.

Subdomains are generally used when a portion of a website requires its own server.

Some examples of subdomains: https://blog.domain.com/, https://accounting.yourdomain.com, shop.domain.com

What's a "folder/subfolder"?

A subfolder is a ‘child directory’ (a folder beneath another folder) that lives under a parent (Home) directory, as is a Subdomain.

A subfolder is similar to a subdomain in that it allows you to create categories of content, but they are set up differently on servers.

Unlike subdomains, there is no server partitioning involved with subfolders.

A subfolder is housed on the same server and any of its link juice goes back to the domain.

An example of a subfolder web address is https://www.domain.com/blog.

With this structure, all of the pages associated with DOMAIN’s blog feed the authority of the main domain.

More details on setting your campaign to track ranks for a domain, a subdomain or a folder:

Domain/subdomain/folder rank tracking in SEOmonitor

cards.title: Subscriptions (pricing versions) that are still active in the platform
cards.text: We currently have clients for which the following pricing versions are active, each with their particular rules listed below.Besides Build-Measure, all other options are grandfathered, and no new cust
cards.html:
We currently have clients for which the following pricing versions are active, each with their particular rules listed below.
Besides Build-Measure, all other options are grandfathered, and no new customer has access to them.

  1. "Old Subscription" - the minimum value for this type of subscription is €49
    It's applicable to users that have old_subscription flag enabled OR created before 2020-11-13
    1. min. 300 keywords
    2. base price/keyword = €0.13*
    3. all regular campaigns are billed, even those without GSC connections. The price is €10/campaign
  2. "New Subscription" - the minimum value for this type of subscription is €198
    In between "Old Subscription" and the new types (simple/build-measure) - this applies if no other rule matches:
    1. min. 4k keywords
    2. base price / kw = 0.047*
    3. all regular campaigns are billed, even those without GSC connections. The price is €10/campaign
  3. "Simple Pricing" - the minimum value for this type of subscription is €149.50 (initially "sold" as $ to clients)
    Applies to users created after 2022-10-01 OR manually flagged with simple_pricing=1:
    1. min. 5k keywords
    2. Keywords are billed in 1k batches - at €29.9/1k
    3. only campaigns with GSC connected are billed. The price is €9.99/campaign
  4. "Build-Measure" - it's a "Simple Pricing" type of subscription with additional rules and applies to users having the is_measure_build flag enabled:
    1. €99 base price - give access to the Build version of the tool (unlimited number of draft campaigns)
    2. min. 0 keywords (overrides the simple pricing limit)
    3. usually, users having this flag should also have simple_pricing enabled so that the rest of the simple pricing rules apply
  5. "Build-Measure" - same description as above, but starting with 09.04.2024, the price per 1000 keywords is €39
  6. "Build-Measure" - same description as above, but starting with 30.05.2024, the price per 1000 keywords is €47
    1. starting 07.06.2024 there's a 3000 kw minimum, so they all start at €240/month now (= €99 + 3*€47).

*For non-Simple Pricing accounts, the base price/keyword decreases with volume - as listed here: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1ByPzX0xjYvH09UBo964YmIrlLeEttiyTFtS21Nm4Oug/edit?usp=sharing

cards.title: The "Low Relevance" Label
cards.text: The "Low Relevance" label highlights that the user’s need or intention might not be met by your website’s content, so it’s unlikely that you’d rank for it. We compute this by analyzing both your websi
cards.html:
The "Low Relevance" label highlights that the user’s need or intention might not be met by your website’s content, so it’s unlikely that you’d rank for it. We compute this by analyzing both your website’s and the top competitors’ landing page content (ranks). If neither are ranking, we give the warning that the keyword might not be relevant.

  • For the keywords added during the setup wizard, the “low relevance” label is applied by taking into consideration those competitors added during the setup.
    When competitors are added later on (after the campaign was created), the label is reviewed but only to be removed if it's identified that it's no longer the case to be applied considering the new list of tracked competitors.

  • For the keywords added after the campaign was created, “low relevance” takes into consideration the competitors tracked at that moment (over "All Keywords").

For more information on how the "Low Relevance" label or any other automated label functions, please check here.

NOTE: For the "Automatic Keyword Research" option (previously present in "Research"), the keyword suggestions that are taken from the Search Console (after the match and distribution is made between GA & GSC) do not go through the "magic cleanup", so the only way to signal irellevant ones is through this "Low relevance" label. SEOmonitor checks the competitors' ranks, to ensure the Visibility Score is not affected by very high search volume (broad) keywords that have poor ranks.

cards.title: The Brand Rule and the Brand Group
cards.text: The "brand rule" is taken into consideration only for the brand/non-brand segmentation.The automatic branding of the keyword happens only on time, the time you add the keyword, be it in the wizard or
cards.html:
The "brand rule" is taken into consideration only for the brand/non-brand segmentation.

The automatic branding of the keyword happens only on time, the time you add the keyword, be it in the wizard or directly in the campaign, and the keywords are added to the Brand group if

[You rank first]
AND

  • [The SERP has full-site links (SITE_LINKS_2)]
    OR
  • [The keyword name contains a brand keyword]
    OR
  • [Three domains in top 5 in the SERP are the same]

OR

  • ([SERP contains either KGraph OR Imbricated features] AND [The keyword name contains a brand keyword])

Manual branding/unbranding

If, later on, the keywords in the Brand folder stop complying with the criteria, you will have the option to unmark them as brand.

You can unmark as brand the keywords that are complying with any other criteria but
[You rank first] AND [The SERP has full-site links (SITE_LINKS_2)]
*from here

There is only one keyword that can never be removed from Brand, and that's "domain.tld"

Unmarking as brand will send the keywords to an automatic group called "Unmarked as brand" from where they can be assigned to any other regular group.

On the other hand, if the keywords in All Keywords will start complying with the Brand folder's criteria, you will have the option to mark them as brand, which will automatically send them to Brand.

Both actions are accessible in Edit mode.

*Dev card here. Check before providing info to the client to make sure they're aligned.

cards.title: The filters we use for pulling data from GSC
cards.text: THERE IS A VALIDATION ONGOING HERE FOR THE NUMBER OK KEYWORDS IN GSC WHEN CHECKING WITH SPLIT BY LANDING PAGE AND DAYWe take the data from GSC differently and apply some filters:aggregation type - by
cards.html:
THERE IS A VALIDATION ONGOING HERE FOR THE NUMBER OK KEYWORDS IN GSC WHEN CHECKING WITH SPLIT BY LANDING PAGE AND DAY

We take the data from GSC differently and apply some filters:

  • aggregation type - by page

  • filters ignore pdf

If the filters removed the data comes identically like in GSC.

We ignore the indexed pages from with .pdf extensions from GSC because they will not have any match in Analytics, you cannot have tracking on those pages.

The code for filtering for the GSC call:
{"startDate":"2021-10-01","endDate":"2021-10-31","dimensions":["query","page"],"aggregationType":"byPage","dimensionFilterGroups":[{"filters":[{"dimension":"page","expression":".pdf","operator":"notContains"}]}],"rowLimit":5000,"startRow":0}

cards.title: The validation condition for showing the last month of Trends in the platform
cards.text: The new validation algorithm:As a validation threshold, we still count the keywords with full trends (>=11 months of SV trends and SV > 0)To validate the integrity of last month, we check that:EITHER
cards.html:
The new validation algorithm:

  • As a validation threshold, we still count the keywords with full trends (>=11 months of SV trends and SV > 0)
  • To validate the integrity of last month, we check that:
    • EITHER the number of keywords (with SV>0) is exactly the same as the threshold
    • OR (we have SVs for keywords counting more than 90% of the threshold, AND their average search volume (value) is over 80% of the search volume average over the past 3 years (this is dynamically provided to the DB query, and 3 years is the value for the top stats chart))


For the SV month-by-month chart, we hide the previous month from the chart while the validation described above fails.


The validation rules are similar for the Top Stats YoY, but there's a difference in functionality - as it was already implemented, when the validation fails, we don't show the YoY.

cards.title: Top-ranking keywords automatically added to the Vault
cards.text: As of August 2024, when you add a campaign, we automatically add the top-ranking keywords to the Vault (50K, for our databases). This reduces the daily 100K vault keyword limit (set per account), so d
cards.html:
As of August 2024, when you add a campaign, we automatically add the top-ranking keywords to the Vault (50K, for our databases).

This reduces the daily 100K vault keyword limit (set per account), so during that day, you would only be able to add 50K more per account.

cards.title: Topics - How is the competitor order/sequence calculated (number in front of the name of the competitor).
cards.text: Example: https://take.ms/MI1wwSearch query: organic chickpeas snacksHippeas rank on google for above search query: https://take.ms/9YULvTopics shows Hippeas.com as being 99+ when added to the competit
cards.html:
Example: https://take.ms/MI1ww

Search query: organic chickpeas snacks

Hippeas rank on google for above search query: https://take.ms/9YULv

Topics shows Hippeas.com as being 99+ when added to the competitors. However on a google search for organic chickpeas snacks they rank in the top 5.

The logic in topics is that we look at all of the kws that resulted from the initial search and look at how visible they are for all of them (not just the initial search). Imagine making a group with all of the results and then checking the visibility Hippeas has on that group.

In this particular case, based on all the results found, Hippeas has 4.56% VS and its ranked as 99+.

cards.title: Topics - One keyword search versus bulk/list search
cards.text: When you're searching for one keyword, you get the topic related data with all the keywords related to that topic.When you bulk search several keywords separated by comma, you are only looking for tho
cards.html:
• When you're searching for one keyword, you get the topic related data with all the keywords related to that topic.

  • When you bulk search several keywords separated by comma, you are only looking for those keywords' data and therefore only see the results for the keywords entered.

    NOTE: The reason for this is that it cannot look for 15 topics at once.

cards.title: Trello tasks - submission & status update
cards.text: Template doc: Trello template.txtTitleThe condensed description of the problem. States the problem, not a desired behaviour, nor opinions.(*Both sides agreed to no longer add the SIDs in the title, as
cards.html:
Template doc: Trello template.txt

Title

The condensed description of the problem. States the problem, not a desired behaviour, nor opinions.

(*Both sides agreed to no longer add the SIDs in the title, as it'll appear in the Description.)

Is it a trial account?

Yes/No

Summary (*optional*)

Only if the title is insufficient in describing the problem in brief.

Impact

Accounts affected. Examples:

  • reported in X campaigns, seems to be the same for everyone

  • reported in Y campaigns, but it's not everywhere (f.x. sid 12345 works correctly)

  • reported in 1 campaign, did not test for more

Steps to reproduce

Either from the client directly or from internal users - the raw, unfiltered information.

Link to the campaign, keyword, module, timeframe, etc, that will be followed directly, in order to move on to step 2.

  • ulterior steps, that could not be included in the link, f.x. "click on X button, see popup"

  • additional steps, concisely described

If screenshots are added (monosnap take.ms or others), they should have a mini-description as well - f.x. client-side screenshot take.ms/ClientShot, how it looks on our side take.ms/InternalShot.

Exactly the analysis done to replicate what the client reported.

  • If CS cannot replicate, attach the Fullstory shortlink AND share info on the steps that were used in the attempt. CS guides the Devs what to look for in the FS session.

  • If the correct Fullstory session cannot be identified, describe the problem with the information from the client.

  • If the Devs cannot replicate after following the steps, they ask the CS for more info, if any.

expected behaviour

Briefly tell the story of what the expected behaviour would be, if following the exact steps described above.

If it's unclear what the expected behaviour is, write the possible version in the task and ask "What is the expected behaviour?" on the #Technical-Questions Slack channel. (This shortens the time it would take for Product to answer on it.)

(*Both sides agreed not to add the Actual Behaviour section, as it is what's stated in the title and/or Summary.)

Extra info (*optional*)

This is where assumptions on the user, their behaviour, or related things go.

  • Specify accounts to be tested on - f.x. To be tested on all reported accounts (if it’s not a general problem)

  • Specify the modules/keywords/etc. to be tested/recrawled/etc. on - f.x. Check that the other options work

  • OR you can add a checklist with modules, accounts, keywords, etc.

If there are any potentially related tasks you know, add them here.

If there are files attached by CS (f.x. SERP pages), mention that there are some + Devs look at the attachments.

Extra steps, f.x. the other options seem to not be functioning; if I click on this first, it works.

If there is a Later Edit (DD/MM, owner of change), f.x. if another case appears. Remember to change the Impact, if it changes. Also add a comment with the information + tags, if needed.

(*optional) Just for CS: any info that's relevant for the CS rep(s), but not to the Devs.

Comments

Whenever changing the Status of the task or changing the description.

  • For Devs - when moving it from Analysis (back to Critical/Major/Minor or forward to In Progress) or when moving it from In Progress to Verify.

  • For CS - when moving it from Verify back to Didn't Pass Checks.

If the Description is updated with a Later Edit (Extra Info - Later Edit point in the section above), add a comment - more like a timestamped notification.

cards.title: Understanding CPC data at a KW level
cards.text: Issue - KWs are missing CPC or how do we check if the CPC cost is correct? https://take.ms/p7dRv - app view https://take.ms/aU0JCX - KW plannerCustomer use case: Keywords are missing their CPC cost an
cards.html:
Issue - KWs are missing CPC or how do we check if the CPC cost is correct?
 https://take.ms/p7dRv - app view
 https://take.ms/aU0JCX - KW planner

Customer use case:
Keywords are missing their CPC cost and the Media Value is very low for the account.

Customer response:
Google Adwords has updated its interface and it no longer shows the Avg CPC cost but a min and max. They also have an API that is currently in Beta. We are still using the OLD API which for some keywords does return an AVG CPC cost however it also returns "null" values. There are a few reasons why the API would return null: the KW has a very few searches and far between which no potential for an Ad campaign or a very niche term that does not return values.

cards.html:

cards.title: VS % for keywords + how is the VS maintained/tuned over time? + VS Impression Rates
cards.text: The VS% for keywords are based on rough estimations from GSC data. However, the point is that the metric is not optimized for precision and it is intended to be used relative to itself.This enables th
cards.html:
The VS% for keywords are based on rough estimations from GSC data. However, the point is that the metric is not optimized for precision and it is intended to be used relative to itself.

This enables the user to compare apples to apples

  • If you rank #1 for all the keywords in a group, using our Visibility metric you’ll know that, because you’ll have 100% visibility - so you can’t do better on than that on that group

  • But if you have estimated CTR as visibility metric, you’ll have something like 23% (is that max? how much can I grow there?)

Article that can be share with customers for more details: https://library.seomonitor.com/post/what-seo-success-means-the-visibility-metric-case-and-why-it-matters

Additional articles for why pixel based tracking isn't the best: https://library.seomonitor.com/post/why-pixel-based-rank-tracking-is-not-solving-the-problem-its-making-it-worse

How about using other estimations for VS (CTR%)?
The problem with with estimated clicks (be it in value or CTR%) as visibility metric is that you don’t know where you stand in terms of ranking on a group of keywords. Example:

  • If you rank #1 for all the keywords in a group, using our Visibility metric you’ll know that, because you’ll have 100% visibility - so you can’t do better on that group

  • But if you have estimated CTR as visibility metric, you’ll have something like 23% (is that max? how much can I grow there?)

Later edit:
If users want to see the visibility impression share, we have the list below.
We don't offer it if there's no request but it is available for our own information and in case they specifically ask for it.

https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1v9TDb2tTTK_hwQgFGWOj_n4f6eBTZL2mWeORCrNZSnA/edit#gid=0

cards.title: VS impact numbers /per keyword from API
cards.text: It refers to the reference frame for the numbers indicating VS impact/per keyword (http://take.ms/a89Sd), from the API keyword data endpoint https://docs.seomonitor.com/api/v1/#!/Keyword_Data/get_keyw
cards.html:
It refers to the reference frame for the numbers indicating VS impact/per keyword (http://take.ms/a89Sd), from the API keyword data endpoint https://docs.seomonitor.com/api/v1/#!/Keyword_Data/get_keyword_data_site_id_date_start_date_end

For the keyword level, it is about the rank change and the impact it had on impressions won or lost. So the number is the estimated number of impressions lost or won based on search volume and rank change.

For the group level, that number is the number of impressions won or lost. It translates the Visibility Change from a percentage in actual impressions. To put it simply is [Visibility Change] (%) * [Total Search Volume] of the group.

cards.title: Valoric Coupons: used by Sales & CS with leads/clients
cards.text: !!! These will only be added as discount coupons, not as credits!For the Locked-in compensation:Algo: [nr.months in contract] * [equivalent MRR, based on their current plan resources]Limit: 6 months (
cards.html:
!!! These will only be added as discount coupons, not as credits!

For the Locked-in compensation:

  • Algo: [nr.months in contract] * [equivalent MRR, based on their current plan resources]

  • Limit: 6 months (if the contract doesn’t exceed the typical 1 year) 

For the Welcome stimulus:

  • Algo: [first month training] + [coupon equivalent to their MRR]

  • Limit: 2 months 

For the Resurrection stimulus:

  • Algo: [first month training] + [coupon equivalent to their MRR]

  • Limit: personalized, up to 3 months 

For the Fuckup compensation:

  • Algo: [fuckup] + 1-month on us [MRR]

  • Limit: -

FOR ACCOUNTS SUSPENDED because of not paying:

(delinquent, dunning exhausted --> reactive, not proactive)
!!! only when the users ask for them (if they don't agree to paying the invoice, because the services weren't provided)

  • Algo: [MRR/30] * [inactive days during invoiced period]

  • Limit: up to 1 month (invoicing period) --> more details here

inactive days = days between [cancellation date] and [reactivation date - 1]
hint: you can google the formula, for easier maths :)

cards.title: Visibility 2.0 + Impression Share
cards.text: Visibility 2.0 will have just one simple update: the Search Volume of the keywords will be multiplied with the % of the Searches Ending Up Clicking on Organic Results (basically the sum of the CTRs of
cards.html:
Visibility 2.0 will have just one simple update: the Search Volume of the keywords will be multiplied with the % of the Searches Ending Up Clicking on Organic Results (basically the sum of the CTRs of the top 10 positions for the SERP feature mix of each keyword).

Another way to put it is that in the Visibility 2.0 the search volumes of the keywords are discounted based on the present SERP features. Everything else stays the same, including the impression rates. This keeps the Visibility metric’s definition the same: the impression rate of a website in the SERPs of a list of keywords.

cards.text: -only taking a fraction of their total search volume into consideration in the forecasts--What's this fraction value?-20%-Why?-We don't have an explanation. We could improve this feature by taking SV
cards.html:
-only taking a fraction of their total search volume into consideration in the forecasts-

-What's this fraction value?
-20%

-Why?
-We don't have an explanation. We could improve this feature by taking SV exactly from the location we are taking the rank from (New York, Bucharest, etc). By 20% we intended to heavily discount that SV for which the client does not rank in all location. It was chosen arbitrarily but we want to make it more scientific.

(currently there is a task in Product for this https://app.clickup.com/t/df3r96)

cards.title: Visibility metric vs. percentage of organic clicks
cards.text: The two metrics are not exactly related or influencing one another.SERP data tells us about the quality of the keywords in terms of how many of the searches end up clicking on organic results. The per
cards.html:
The two metrics are not exactly related or influencing one another.

SERP data tells us about the quality of the keywords in terms of how many of the searches end up clicking on organic results. 

The percentage of organic clicks refers to the Top 10 positions and how many clicks they get through. So it's not the rate of clicks for your website, but for all the results on the first page. 

Visibility looks at ranks and search volumes, and it is specific to your website. It considers all your tracked keywords, though, for those starting position 21, your Visibility will be 0.
