cards.title: What and where to check whenever we see traffic drops?
cards.text: We usually notice traffic drops in the SEO Campaign section (http://take.ms/qCVnq), but in order to properly check if there's a problem there are a few steps to follow:check how the distribution looks
cards.html:
We usually notice traffic drops in the SEO Campaign section (http://take.ms/qCVnq), but in order to properly check if there's a problem there are a few steps to follow:

  • check how the distribution looks like in the Organic Traffic section --> split the brand/non brand to see if there's something happening with the brand: http://take.ms/etfRe

  • Select the date prior to the drop in the calendar, go to the Landing Page view and sort the traffic data by Visits, to see which LPs get most visits.

  • Select the day of the drop in the calendar and check which LPs get most visits

  • Export data from GA via API (use the 'i' button), for both of the dates (prior and after the drop) to see how the Landing Pages look like and how they have changed. If the LPs look different, there have been changes in GA and GSC doesn't acknowledge them just yet.

cards.title: What are the changes we show in the Visibility Score Change Explainer? What are the 2 Search Volume-related changes?
cards.text: ​We show 2 main categories:Performance related changes a. rank changes - both +/- , the examples shown are the ones with the most impactb. search volume trend changes - it's the YoY Trend. Even big ch
cards.html:
​We show 2 main categories:

  1. Performance related changes

a. rank changes - both +/- , the examples shown are the ones with the most impact

b. search volume trend changes - it's the YoY Trend. Even big changes in YoY have a small effect on the Visibility Score because the Search Volume used in the algorithm is the 12 month average. Even if the search volume from last month drops to 0, it will only affect 1/12 out of the average volume. Thus, usual changes are minor.

  1. Non-performance related changes/Baseline changes

a. significant, yet abnormal, Search Volume changes from one month to another, following reaggregations by Google

b. keyword additions or removals

Details on the explainer: It should contain the percentages given by at least the rank changes, while the 2 types of Search Volume related changes only appear when it's the case.

The examples are shown like these - http://take.ms/wx26qV

cards.title: What are the time criteria we use when sending email reports to clients? - internal info
cards.text: A scheduled task runs every day at a fixed time and goes through all the active campaigns. it checks each and every one of them and decides if it needs to send a weekly or monthly report for them base
cards.html:
A scheduled task runs every day at a fixed time and goes through all the active campaigns. it checks each and every one of them and decides if it needs to send a weekly or monthly report for them based on their settings and the current date.

The list of campaigns is not ordered explicitly, so it's arbitrary. These are intensive processes, so they get sent when they're ready for all the campaigns and all the clients.

In conclusion, we cannot reliably predict when a report will be sent during the execution of the scheduled event.

cards.title: What can be considered a Maintenance task?
cards.text: all bugs - when the user (client or internal) cannot use the product as expected (already promised by the platform) OR the experience is affected by a problem in the interface, AND there’s no workarou
cards.html:
• all bugs - when the user (client or internal) cannot use the product as expected (already promised by the platform) OR the experience is affected by a problem in the interface, AND there’s no workaround.

  • SLA problems - downtimes, rank delays, rank discrepancy, unusually slow loading times

  • investigations - potential problems, "suspicious" behaviour

  • smadmin tools - ONLY if them missing is a blocker for the team to solve a problem (otherwise, they will be done through a dev Sprint!)

  • issues on internal systems that are not working properly anymore (f.x. Chargebee, CS scores, etc)

Tasks that fall under Maintenance should take under 2 hours (following a Dev lead's analysis). If it's still mandatory, though it would take longer than 2hrs, it should be escalated and clarified with Marius, Dragos, Cosmin & Mada-Carla.

NEW GUIDELINES

There is a daily responsible for Maintenance and it will be announced on Slack, in the #Dev-App channel. They will also be responsible for that channel for the day and answer the questions or re-route to the most appropriate person.

If it is unknown whether an issue is a "bug" or not, the question will be phrased as "What is the expected behaviour?"

Assign tasks that "Didn't pass checks" to Dragos & Marius as well + inform Mada-Carla.

"Telegraphic" tasks (f.x. from Cosmin :)) have to be fully understood - CS should get all of the necessary details when compiling the tasks.

Browser interaction UI/UX tasks (f.x. through popup windows) should have info on the browser, resolution and/or OS.

Tasks with multiple examples may have a checklist with each example that was reported/identified, but leave the examples in the descriptions as well.

Followup card: Trello tasks - submission & status update

cards.title: What counts as Inertial Traffic?
cards.text: The Organic Traffic for the entire website (not just the tracked keywords or the group/s you selected in the Forecast).The inertial traffic shows the traffic that the (whole) site would be getting, if
cards.html:
The Organic Traffic for the entire website (not just the tracked keywords or the group/s you selected in the Forecast).

The inertial traffic shows the traffic that the (whole) site would be getting, if it would not be improving in ranks at all (so not taking rank info into account). It's plotted starting with the last 30 days' traffic (Organic Traffic, that we have from Analytics) and using the Search Seasonality (monthly search volume fluctuations, from Ads).

On "top" of that, we can add the traffic resulting from any desired improvements in Visibility, starting from our (tracked) keyword/group selection (and corresponding targets) in order to get the Improved Traffic.

cards.title: What criteria is the order of the SERP features on the dedicated chart from the keywords sidebar based on?
cards.text: For the SERP feature chart on the keywords sidebar, the SERP features are ordered by their Cumulative Presence Score across the date range.The Score calculation is the following: Sum of daily values w
cards.html:
For the SERP feature chart on the keywords sidebar, the SERP features are ordered by their Cumulative Presence Score across the date range.

The Score calculation is the following:

Sum of daily values where 0=not present, 1=present, 2=present (mine)

    • Higher scores = higher position on the chart
    • AIO features are always moved to the top when present

Each device has its own frequency counts and ordering; more precisely, they are calculated separately.
So, they're completely separate and not dependent on one another.

cards.title: What do we take into consideration for the Opportunity Metric?
cards.text: The problem: It’s hard to take the many attributes of a keyword into account when trying to figure out which keywords could generate the biggest impact in an SEO strategy. That leads to either a lot o
cards.html:
The problem: It’s hard to take the many attributes of a keyword into account when trying to figure out which keywords could generate the biggest impact in an SEO strategy. That leads to either a lot of manual work in spreadsheets or scripts, or simplifying the decision to one attribute, like the search volume. But we all know that’s far from good enough.

The solution: One metric that would encapsulate all the qualitative and quantitative attributes of a keyword for an SEO strategy. These are:

  1. Search Volume

  2. Search Trend (YoY)

  3. SERP features as % of searches that end up clicking on an organic result

  4. Current rank (as in how much better it can get) as [ctr(top3) - ctr(current rank)]

  5. Warning labels for weak keywords where the quality parameters become irrelevant (like brands of others, irrelevant keywords, highly localized keywords) as a % reduction in search volume.

  6. Difficulty to rank in top 10 as top10(1), easy(2), medium(3), hard(4)

cards.title: What does "google_indexed_pages" mean in the API  and as a general notion?
cards.text: As a general notion - The number of pages indexed by Google for that specific searchIndexed pages are the pages of a website that a search engine has visited, analyzed and added to its database of web
cards.html:
As a general notion - The number of pages indexed by Google for that specific search

Indexed pages are the pages of a website that a search engine has visited, analyzed and added to its database of web pages. Pages are indexed either because the website owner requested the search engine to index web pages or through the discovery of web pages by the search engine bot through links to those pages.

In the API call - if it exists, it represents indexed pages for that keyword but it will most probably become deprecated and return null values (according to Marius https://take.ms/pSlyD https://take.ms/dWLvo)

cards.title: What happens to Content section after Beta release?
cards.text: The section will remain available but only for the users active in the last 30 days. The rest of the users (that have access to this feature) will be informed that we are going to decommission it in t
cards.html:
The section will remain available but only for the users active in the last 30 days. 
The rest of the users (that have access to this feature) will be informed that we are going to decommission it in the next 2 months.

cards.title: What happens to the Visibility Score - group level when adding/moving/removing keywords
cards.text: Whenever adding/moving/removing tracked keywords to/from groups, they automatically affect the historical visibility. add keywords - affects the VS of the group where the keywords are addedmove/remove
cards.html:
Whenever adding/moving/removing tracked keywords to/from groups, they automatically affect the historical visibility. 

  • add keywords - affects the VS of the group where the keywords are added

  • move/remove - affects the VS of the group the keywords will no longer be part of

There used to be a notification but it was removed because there was a misunderstanding with the previous confirmation pop-up as the users thought it was a required step/decision to instruct SEOmonitor to update the Visibility metric historically, rather than a normal behavior we just wanted to remind them about.

When archiving keywords, the user will be requested to choose whether they want the VS to be recalculated or not

cards.html:
Once the last campaign of the account is archived, they will receive a pop-up asking if they want to cancel their account or not.

  • If they say "Yes" they will be redirected to "Billing" to cancel the account.
  • If they say "No" the account will remain active

Full behaviour video here: https://take.ms/Ur5uC

cards.title: What historical ranking data can we provide?
cards.text: The data from GA&GSC is not used to calculate the Visibility but the data from AdWords, respectively the Search Volume2. The only historical ranking data we can provide within SEOmonitor is the one th
cards.html:
The data from GA&GSC is not used to calculate the Visibility but the data from AdWords, respectively the Search Volume2. The only historical ranking data we can provide within SEOmonitor is the one the client brings from other tools.

If they do not have this data we do the following:

We provide 30 days of historical ranks for the first experience if the GSC keywords can match the ones in the campaign.

We display those ranks and we calculate the visibility score as well for that period

We calculate the ranks historically using the ranks crawled today and the avg rank historically found in the GSC data by matching them with the ratio between the latest avg rank from GSC and the current rank today

If we don't match any gsc keyword with a tracked keyword we won't apply this, if we match some, then those will use the described algo and the unmatched we'll use the current rank historically

cards.title: What is Clickstream and how do we use it?
cards.text: Clickstream = a record of a user's activity on the web as represented by the sequence of links they click on (including every site and the pages visited on that site, how long the user was on a page,
cards.html:
Clickstream = a record of a user's activity on the web as represented by the sequence of links they click on (including every site and the pages visited on that site, how long the user was on a page, any followed newsgroups, any email addresses in contact with the user).

The clickstream analysis is the process of collecting, analyzing and reporting aggregate data. Since
Clickstream data is becoming increasingly valuable to Internet marketers and advertisers, so the sessions are monitored from behind the curtains, data is gathered, stored and eventually even sold.

Places where we have used clickstream or a reference to it:

  1. When Adwords shows 0
    Search Volumes     (e.g. for 'dangerous' terms) - In such cases, we'll use clickstream data, which is less accurate but better than 0. When clickstream data is used for search volume estimate, a warning icon is placed next to it with details in the tooltip. (NOT for customer - initial clickstream is SERPstat, when no SERPstat info, 2nd clickstream source is GSC; the GSC volumes should also be marked with the exclamation point)
    the rule is: if you don't have SV in adwords -> go to SERPstat if there aren't in -> go to GSC

  2. Our Keyword database was started from clickstream data, then calibrated through a thorough process

  3. We use it in conversations with customers when we want to avoid advertising a competitor (as in bullet no.1 , where we never pronounce SERPstat, we only say clickstream to the customer)


Internal note - We don't use Jumpshot data

cards.title: What is an Organic result in Google?
cards.text: There are a couple of criteria that help us assess whether a result in the SERP is organic or not:Non-paid resultLink to a web pageNot a SERP featureCan be influenced through SEOIs visible without any
cards.html:
There are a couple of criteria that help us assess whether a result in the SERP is organic or not:

  • Non-paid result

  • Link to a web page

  • Not a SERP feature

  • Can be influenced through SEO

  • Is visible without any other click

cards.title: What is the AI Search keyword processing speed?
cards.text: The speed at which keywords are processed depends on the number of keywords being processed simultaneously. Currently, the system can handle up to 500 keywords at once, and each batch can take up to a
cards.html:
The speed at which keywords are processed depends on the number of keywords being processed simultaneously.

Currently, the system can handle up to 500 keywords at once, and each batch can take up to about 5 minutes to complete. For the public release, the plan is to boost capacity to 10,000 keywords at a time.

cards.title: What is the benefit of doing keyword research in SeoMonitor vs. tools like Aherfs and SEMRush (trying to understand the main differences or if it's just a similar tool and workflow)?
cards.text: There are more similarities than differences.But the main differences would be:Keyword curation - Like the fact you wouldn’t see the same keyword counted twice in SEOmonitor if multiple pages of the s
cards.html:
There are more similarities than differences.

But the main differences would be:

  1. Keyword curation - Like the fact you wouldn’t see the same keyword counted twice in SEOmonitor if multiple pages of the same website rank in the Top 20 or the inclusion of brand keywords which usually influence top stats.
    It removed that extra step that you need to do in Spreadsheets.
  2. Free monthly rank tracking. After you’ve built a list of keywords, you can track your own rankings every month for unlimited keywords.
  3. Philosophical/business model. Semrush and Ahrefs could be thought of as a database that monetizes access to the same instance of the data to everyone (hence the costs of multiple users and the 500$ / month fee for using the API). Our philosophy is that once one of our customers acquires data from SEOmonitor, it’s theirs, so we don’t charge access to it.
    API, multiple users, and Looker and Sheets connectors are included in the minimum subscription.

It's probable that both Semrush and Ahrefs have a much more comprehensive dataset than ours, especially in regions that we don’t yet directly cover.
Some of our customers buy SEOmonitor for Rank Tracking and SEO Forecasting and keep a minimum Ahref subscription as well, so it always depends on the use case.

cards.html:
The All-time best rank is the best position ever achieved by a keyword since it started being tracked in SEOmonitor, no matter the timeframe selected in the calendar.

The icon (star representation) is relative to the interval, but it’s still tied to that all-time.

cards.title: What keywords are included in the Visibility Change explainer
cards.text: The Visibility Change explainer only takes into consideration the keywords that had any type and level of influence on the performance during the selected timeframe, be it rank and/or search trends wi
cards.html:
The Visibility Change explainer only takes into consideration the keywords that had any type and level of influence on the performance during the selected timeframe, be it rank and/or search trends wise.

It will exclude the keywords that had no influence, like, for example:
- keywords that had a rank change but didn't make it to Top 20
- keywords that had a Search Volume change but are outside of Top 20
- keywords that are in Top 20 but had no rank and/or Search Volume change

Another category that's fully excluded, regardless of their rankings and search trend is the close variations (childern)

This means that there can be a difference in the number of keyowrds in the analyzed group and the number of keywords in the explainer.

cards.text: There are 2 kinds of meta data (title, description, headings, canonical):written on the page source by the website administrator (extracted from the website pages) - we show it on the LP view on the k
cards.html:
There are 2 kinds of meta data (title, description, headings, canonical):

  • written on the page source by the website administrator (extracted from the website pages) - we show it on the LP view on the kw page (please note that the date displayed in the calendar is the date for the LP screenshot, the crawl for the page is not regular, but client should be informed that crawls are done regularly, yet in batches without a fixed date

  • indexed by Google - sometimes identical to the above, sometimes modified - we display it in the I(i) box in Manage columns - taken daily from the SERP

sometimes the 2 are identical, but they can also differ

cards.html:
Which parts of the tool rely on AI technologies and how?

Magic keywords (voluntary) - using AI in research

AI Clustering (voluntary) - using AI in group structure

Forecast: AI Summary analysis of the forecast (voluntary) , helps to provide context , highlight challenges to the forecast that was configured

cards.title: What's the CTR Value considered for the target rank in Forecast?
cards.text: The CTR value used for the forecasted keywords is an average relative to the selected target rank, weighed against the search volume. The CTR curves are influenced by the combination of features prese
cards.html:
• The CTR value used for the forecasted keywords is an average relative to the selected target rank, weighed against the search volume. The CTR curves are influenced by the combination of features present in the SERP for each keyword, so it's not always the same CTR value applied for a certain target rank.

  • At a keyword level, it depends on how you are ranking now, what the SERPs look like (features present - as those affect the CTRs) and the weigh against the search volume comes in for the Additional Sessions you’d get. Additionally, desktop and mobile would have different curves (values, averages)

Do we take into consideration the keywords that are already in the target rank to the estimation?

Yes, we do!
For example, if you've selected the 1-5 target rank and have a keyword that is on 4 already, the top 1-5 Average CTR is higher than the position 4' CTR, so you can still have growth. (this can be checked in "Analyze calculation" by checking the additional sessions).

cards.title: When do we update Search Volumes from Google Ads?
cards.text: There’s a nonstop process that constantly checks if there are keywords that need an update, depending on the current date and the last available search volume month. (if we’re talking about, for examp
cards.html:
There’s a nonstop process that constantly checks if there are keywords that need an update, depending on the current date and the last available search volume month. (if we’re talking about, for example, Sept 14, 2022, anything that doesn’t have Aug as the last available trend data is sent to be updated.

If the search volume is not available yet, the keyword is flagged, and the recheck is made the next day. The process that updates Search Volumes has a limit of 800 requests/day with a maximum of 800 keywords/request. that means we can update a max of 640 000 keywords/day.

The rank tracker keywords have priority, and the ones in the research databases come second after all keywords in the app have been updated.

We update each keyword individually as we receive the batches from Google Ads. It is possible to have kws with updated SV and others without in the same campaign.

The top stats Search Volume is updated when over 90% of the keywords have the new SV.

cards.title: When do we update the ranks and visibility?
cards.text: The crawl is done over night for both desktop and mobile just so they can be updated by 9:00 A.M for every timezone.If there's a country that has different timezones like the US, it will automatically
cards.html:
The crawl is done over night for both desktop and mobile just so they can be updated by 9:00 A.M for every timezone.

If there's a country that has different timezones like the US, it will automatically take into consideration the specific timezone for the selected location without you setting this up.

cards.title: When does CS respond in Intercom?
cards.text: Always.Only after we've done our part, we can push it to whomever would be in charge for followup.Examples:A lead (non-trial, non-client, non-subuser) writes with any question (support or not)First we
cards.html:
Always.

Only after we've done our part, we can push it to whomever would be in charge for followup.

Examples:

  • A lead (non-trial, non-client, non-subuser) writes with any question (support or not)

    • First we respond to the question.

    • Only after that do we introduce the corresponding Business Developer.

    • After this, we can close the conversation from our side, as there's nothing more for us to be done there.

  • Someone writes directly in Turkish

    • Use Google translate to actually understand their need first.

    • Respond in English, saying that we don't offer direct support in Turkish but, from what Google Translate is providing, we understand that the question is about...

    • We no longer offer support in Turkish, as we don't have the team there.

cards.text: If you've logged in with Master pass + still have those cookies, you won't see the Contact us button. Being logged in with seomonitor.com, bunt.ro, studiorock.ro will also remove the Contact Us button
cards.html:
If you've logged in with Master pass + still have those cookies, you won't see the Contact us button. Being logged in with seomonitor.com, bunt.ro, studiorock.ro will also remove the Contact Us button.

To test for customers, open an incognito window and login with the hash link from intercom: https://take.ms/Szmyj

If the user has any blocking software (JS Blockers, Ad blocker or DNS filtering on tracking tools) the Contact Us button won't work. In cases where we detect it does not work, they will receive a notification to contact us at mailto:customer.service@seomonitor.com.

The general rule is that, if we find a FS for the customer he should be able to use Contact Us.

cards.title: Where & when do we take data for the CTR? How are Estimated Visits calculated?
cards.text: If a website has a GSC connected, we take the data for the CTR, both in SEO Campaign and in Organic Traffic from their GSC profile.CTR will show values only if the keyword has Average Position less th
cards.html:
If a website has a GSC connected, we take the data for the CTR, both in SEO Campaign and in Organic Traffic from their GSC profile.

CTR will show values only if the keyword has Average Position less than 10, otherwise it will appear with "-" or with "N/A".

If GSC is not connected - the CTR in both areas should appear either with "-" or with "N/A".

The CTR is per keyword and does not change based on device.

As for the CTR in the Estimated Visits from the Competition Insights section, we use a standard CTR, based on the position in the SERP (regardless oh whether the GSC is connected or not), but soon it will be upgraded to a dynamic one, based on a more detailed research, taking into consideration SERP features, the market, keyword type and device distribution.

The Estimated Visits are calculated based on one of our algorithms, taking into consideration search volumes, ranks, and a CTR-type of proprietary function. 

  • at the moment we calculate the visits according to the granularity (for daily we use 30 ranks Average, weekly 4 ranks average, monthly 1 rank only)

  • soon we will only use the latest rank

cards.title: Where can you find the Keyword Gap Analysis
cards.text: As of October 2025 when the AIO/AIS Unified tracking has been released, the Keyword Gap Analysis is only available in the Research section, it's no longer available in the Competition section.
cards.html: As of October 2025 when the AIO/AIS Unified tracking has been released, the Keyword Gap Analysis is only available in the Research section, it's no longer available in the Competition section.

cards.title: Where does GSC anonymous data fit into traffic data?
cards.text: Our clients might ask about GSC anonymous data (see here): where does it fit in the traffic data and will the Unallocated traffic increase if there are more GSC queries hidden? The short answer is no.
cards.html:
Our clients might ask about GSC anonymous data (see here): where does it fit in the traffic data and will the Unallocated traffic increase if there are more GSC queries hidden?

The short answer is no. We call it thin traffic and we know that some queries don’t show up in GSC if they don’t have a certain threshold of searches.

cards.title: Which CPC value do we take from Google Adwords (Keywords planner)?
cards.text: We take the Average CPC value - http://take.ms/ck5sC, where average is defined by Google in this article: https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/14074?hl=en.
cards.html: We take the Average CPC value - http://take.ms/ck5sC, where average is defined by Google in this article: https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/14074?hl=en.

cards.title: Which rank do we compute when calculating the Difficulty and Opportunity of a keyword?
cards.text: Both metrics are calculated only based on Desktop data.So if you're on Mobile view and you see either:a trophy for Top 3, but you're outside of the top 3, or that you're in Top 10, but still outside o
cards.html:
Both metrics are calculated only based on Desktop data.

So if you're on Mobile view and you see either:

  • a trophy for Top 3, but you're outside of the top 3,
  • or that you're in Top 10, but still outside of it,

just check the Desktop rank.

(last confirmed in https://app.clickup.com/t/358wjx8)

cards.title: Who can use the Pitching option?
cards.text: Pitching campaigns can be created in any account (trial or paid) that has at least a regular campaign.
cards.html: Pitching campaigns can be created in any account (trial or paid) that has at least a regular campaign.

cards.title: Why Exponential progress as default in Forecast?
cards.text: SEOmonitor's Forecast defaults to an exponential progression in time, from 0% to 100%. There are a few reasons for this, the most important one being that the CTR curves are exponential. So the higher
cards.html:
SEOmonitor's Forecast defaults to an exponential progression in time, from 0% to 100%. 

There are a few reasons for this, the most important one being that the CTR curves are exponential. So the higher you rank, the more exponential the results. 

There are also some operational aspects that can delay initial results (and make them exponential), like identifying, planning and executing SEO actions, be them technical- or content-related. 

And then there are those actions that will usually generate compounded results over time.

Check here: Exponential vs Linear progressions.

cards.title: Why I don't receive email reports for the campaigns I subscribed to?
cards.text: For ranks integrity purposes, the email reports will start to be issued 2 full weeks after the date the campaign was created.
cards.html: For ranks integrity purposes, the email reports will start to be issued 2 full weeks after the date the campaign was created.

cards.title: Why am I getting an empty YOY export from the keyword page?
cards.text: The data in this export is related to the timeframe selected in the calendar.If you only include months that we don't yet have SV data for, like the current and the previous month (usually gets update
cards.html:
The data in this export is related to the timeframe selected in the calendar.

If you only include months that we don't yet have SV data for, like the current and the previous month (usually gets updated around the 24th) the export will come empty.

cards.title: Why am I seeing different ranks in the export from the magnifier versus the app
cards.text: Ranks in the magnifier come from Topics or SERPStat (for non Topic Explorer search engines).Differences can occur since the two apps make the crawls at different times.(significant differences, though
cards.html:
Ranks in the magnifier come from Topics or SERPStat (for non Topic Explorer search engines).

Differences can occur since the two apps make the crawls at different times.

(significant differences, though, should be investigated)

cards.title: Why are AIS responses different for the exact keywords?
cards.text: Unlike Google, AI models almost never give the same response twice in a row. It's supposed to be different by design.Confirmed here
cards.html:
Unlike Google, AI models almost never give the same response twice in a row. It's supposed to be different by design.

Confirmed here

cards.title: Why are there more options for granularity for the Visibility Score in Competition?
cards.text: The sampling in Competition is an old decision. And it was made to give the user a good-enough granularity while keeping a good loading speed. (don't share with the client the whole phrase)There’s a b
cards.html:
The sampling in Competition is an old decision. And it was made to give the user a good-enough granularity while keeping a good loading speed. (don't share with the client the whole phrase)

There’s a big difference between Strategy, where we only provide daily granularity, and Competition. The first one has just one website plotted, while the other one has up to 10. It would also be a mess with a chart that has 10 lines with hundreds of dots each.

One more thing, sampling is not averaging. We just take one day out of 7 (when doing weekly sampling) and one day out of 30 (when doing monthly sampling), more precisley, the Visibility is displayed:

  • daily for timeframes up to 31 days
  • weekly for timeframes between 31 and 99 days - last day of the week
  • monthly for time frames higher than 99 days - last day of the month

cards.title: Why can a website with higher Visibility have lower Share of Clicks?
cards.text: Because one metric uses ImpressionRates (linear) and the other ClickThroughRates (exponential), in some cases, this can happen.The Share of Clicks works with estimated clicks, while the Visibility wor
cards.html:
Because one metric uses ImpressionRates (linear) and the other ClickThroughRates (exponential), in some cases, this can happen.

The Share of Clicks works with estimated clicks, while the Visibility works with an estimated share of impressions.

  • The formula for estimated clicks is:
    [avg search volume] * [YoY search trend] * [% of searches that end up clicking on organic results / aka SERP features impact] * [CTR(rank)]

  • The formula for estimated impressions is:
    [avg. search volume] * [impressionRate(rank)] -> Visibility is [sum(impressions)] / [sum(searchVolume)]

So, different keyword attributes are used in the formulas. That can make them not perfectly correlate, though usually, they will.
It will probably not be the case anymore when we release the Visibility that will take into account “% of clicks”

cards.title: Why can there be major changes in competitors results from one day to the other, for the same query? (Topics)
cards.text: Topics is working with a huge internal database and new ranks are crawled in batches, resulting in some becoming even up to one month old.Seeing that the Visibility Score is updated every time either
cards.html:
Topics is working with a huge internal database and new ranks are crawled in batches, resulting in some becoming even up to one month old.

Seeing that the Visibility Score is updated every time either the rank or the Search Volume is updated, discrepancies between the results from three days ago and today can appear.

Also, discrepancies can appear between the results in Topics and a Google search, as well as between Topics and SEOmonitor, where all ranks are updated daily.

To go with a more precise example, if a query returns 50 keywords and for half of them the ranks are updated today, today I can get a different top of competitors than yesterday, provided there are changes that have an important impact on Visibility.

If two days from now the other half of the keywords get new ranks as well, they can balance back the situation to the results I was getting three days ago.

cards.title: Why can't I find Taiwan as a country on the markets list
cards.text: According to Google Ads (and the Chinese), Taiwan is not a country. Google is a US company, and the US does not recognize Taiwan as a country -> https://take.ms/cq2Qq0So you can find it in the "region
cards.html:
According to Google Ads (and the Chinese), Taiwan is not a country.

Google is a US company, and the US does not recognize Taiwan as a country -> https://take.ms/cq2Qq0


So you can find it in the "regions" list: -> https://take.ms/BNWxk

cards.title: Why can't I restore my keywords to Ungrouped?
cards.text: The archived keywords cannot be sent to Ungrouped upon reactivation because this is an automatic group, with the sole function of picking up the keywords left unnested once a regular group is deleted.
cards.html: The archived keywords cannot be sent to Ungrouped upon reactivation because this is an automatic group, with the sole function of picking up the keywords left unnested once a regular group is deleted.

cards.title: Why can't I select "Use YOY search trends" option in Forecast scenario?
cards.text: Once you've calibrated the Search Volume with YOY for a specific campaign, the "Use YOY search trends" option will automatically be disabled as the your SV already takes into consideration YOY trends
cards.html: Once you've calibrated the Search Volume with YOY for a specific campaign, the "Use YOY search trends" option will automatically be disabled as the your SV already takes into consideration YOY trends

cards.title: Why can't a keyword with a  "news" feature on its SERP get a cannibalization warning?
cards.text: The code says that we consider a keyword as having "cannibalization" issues if both of these apply to it simultaneously:has multiple (twice or more) Landing Page switches during the past 30 daysdoes n
cards.html:
The code says that we consider a keyword as having "cannibalization" issues if both of these apply to it simultaneously:

  • has multiple (twice or more) Landing Page switches during the past 30 days
  • does not have a "news" feature on its SERP

Why the "news" feature?

Having in mind that due to the nature of news being related to recent events, it makes sense for news links to change frequently, so it wouldn't be cannibalization per se.

NOTE: Because there are some cases where it should be marked as cannibalization (keywords are not news related), we will use the new SERP feature position update (it should be launched soon) and only remove from cannibalization those keywords where top stories are ranking above the first two results.

cards.title: Why did we remove the CTR Curve from the Rank Tracker?
cards.text: Because it wasn’t an accurate curve on each mix of SERP features. And we want to deliver the most accurate data we can.We felt that the most accurate data is how the percentage of clicks on organic re
cards.html:
Because it wasn’t an accurate curve on each mix of SERP features. And we want to deliver the most accurate data we can.

We felt that the most accurate data is how the percentage of clicks on organic results are affected by the specific mix of SERP features.

In our research we didn’t have all the SERP features combinations, and many of them didn’t have enough data to draw statistical conclusions.
But we had enough data to compute how each SERP feature affects that percentage on average when present in any mix. That felt more fair to deliver than a CTR curve.

NOTE: THE CTR CURVE IS STILL PRESENT IN FORECAST FOR THE ANALYZE CALCULATION OPTION.

cards.title: Why do I get results researching some keywords individually but not when I add them all to the serach bar - Topics
cards.text: If you search for each keyword, they will be crawled on the fly, only Ranks, without Search Volumes. If you search for all of them, they won't be shown until their Search Volumes get processed and it'
cards.html:
If you search for each keyword, they will be crawled on the fly, only Ranks, without Search Volumes.

If you search for all of them, they won't be shown until their Search Volumes get processed and it's over 50 (for the UK market).
The Search Volume processing could take up to a few weeks

cards.title: Why do I see competitors that I didn't add to my groups?
cards.text: We automatically add the top 5 competitors (the most visible ones) to your groups, you can always remove them and add your own.For "All Keywords" you'll have the competitors you've selected in wizard.
cards.html:
We automatically add the top 5 competitors (the most visible ones) to your groups, you can always remove them and add your own.
For "All Keywords" you'll have the competitors you've selected in wizard.

Also, when we automatically create the campaigns (for migrations f.x) the most visible competitors will be automatically added.

cards.title: Why do I see more Signals cards in Dashboard than in  Reporting
cards.text: In the Dashboard, they're constantly updated, while in Reporting, it only takes one full month.For example, if you have a report to be done for August, the Reporting add-on assistant will only take in
cards.html:
In the Dashboard, they're constantly updated, while in Reporting, it only takes one full month.


For example, if you have a report to be done for August, the Reporting add-on assistant will only take into consideration the signals cards between 01.08 - 31.08. If you have cards for 05.08 - 05.09, they will not be included there, just in the Dashboard

cards.title: Why do I see more sessions in SEOmonitor than in my Google Analytics profile?
cards.text: There have been two instances (so far) when the number of sessions in SEOmonitor is higher than what GA is displaying.The precise filter combination to use in GA:Session medium exactly matches organic
cards.html:
There have been two instances (so far) when the number of sessions in SEOmonitor is higher than what GA is displaying.

The precise filter combination to use in GA:

  • Session medium exactly matches organic

AND

  • Session source contains google

Sampled data

If you’ve been working with larger data sets, you might have noticed that your reports look a little off.
That’s likely because GA4 sometimes uses data sampling to make things run faster by only analysing part of your data instead of the full set.

GA4 has visual cues to indicate when your data is sampled or unsampled.


This info appears next to any default report name in GA, and the drop-down available next to it can provide some additional insights.



While Google samples the data in the Analytics interface, they provide full data through the API (our GA data source). And this is why you'll see higher numbers in SEOmonitor.

*External info sources here and here.

GA4 is summing sessions differently ("incorrectly")

This is an issue widely noted, like, for example, here and here and here (though more often reported as "total" versus "sum by channels").

What happens is that there's a difference in total sessions not only between SEOmonitor and GA but even between the total in GA's interface and the total in their export, which provides data at a hostname/page level.

How can you check if this is the case for you?

  • start by applying the proper filter,
  • add the "hostname" column ("page+query string" can also work)
  • then open the report as an exploration
  • finally, export from exploration and make the total in the CSV

Monosnap Video 2025-04-02 19.2.mp4

While there's no official explanation, there are some hypotheses:

  • The difference in numbers may be a result of hyperlog-like optimization of data aggregation. In other words, plain simple summing might be too expensive for GA4 to conduct in that report, so it uses advanced algorithms that do sums faster but less accurate. (from here)
  • When a user lands on a landing page, a timer starts for 30 minutes of activity known as a Session. During this session, whenever a user opens separate pages, these are called pageviews. So another theory is that the total comes from the actual sessions, while the sessions per page are rather pageviews.
  • In GA4, a session doesn't expire if a user returns to the website from a different source during an active session. It would still be one session, and both traffic sources would be in the same session. (from here)


*External info about GA4's sessions and their challenges here and here.

**As internal info, you might also want to check:

  • How to check traffic in Google Analytics (GA, GA4, GA360)
  • Understand how sampled data can affect your campaign
How to check traffic in Google Analytics (GA4)

cards.title: Why do I see the same seasonality in Forecast scenarios done on different groups of keywords?
cards.text: This happens when the scenarios are created on the same date as the top 10 keywords are taken into account, no matter what groups you select, because these keywords would impact the future organic tra
cards.html:
This happens when the scenarios are created on the same date as the top 10 keywords are taken into account, no matter what groups you select, because these keywords would impact the future organic traffic even if you don't include them in your forecast.

For non-pitching campaigns, the "Search Seasonality" percentage is computed as: [Month "Top10 Keywords SV" / First month's "Top10 Keywords SV"] * 100

Where "Top10 Keywords SV" is the sum of search volumes for the keywords with desktop ranks in top 10 when the Forecast is created.

cards.title: Why do I see traffic differences between SEOmonitor and Analytics?
cards.text: Besides the info in this article, it can be a "google business profile" that's not correctly named. To pull traffic data, we filter on the source=@google ➝ translation: the source name needs to contai
cards.html:
Besides the info in this article, it can be a "google business profile" that's not correctly named.

To pull traffic data, we filter on the source=@google ➝ translation: the source name needs to contain GOOGLE.

If the source does not contain google, we don't pull data on it

cards.title: Why do I still get a warning even if I lower my target to the level the higher target estimated to be achievable?
cards.text: There are instances in the Forecast where setting a higher target returns a warning, saying that you could only achieve a lower target.But if you then select this lower estimated target, you still get
cards.html:
There are instances in the Forecast where setting a higher target returns a warning, saying that you could only achieve a lower target.

But if you then select this lower estimated target, you still get the warning, aiming even lower now.

The estimated average target rank is based on the projected rank averages, which depend on:

  • initial keyword rank
  • target rank
  • Search Volume
  • number of forecasted months
  • progression curves

So, the explanation for the above behaviour is that some of the keywords included in the calculation will never reach the target with the selected speed option, but, depending on where you set the target, the average will change.
The roundings also play a significant role.

cards.title: Why do clients see Different values on Onpage than us internally?
cards.text: When you discuss the values on Onpage with clients, please beware that they don't see the same info as we do with the Masterpass.e.g. masterpass - http://take.ms/Z6CQOregular pass - https://take.ms/ch
cards.html:
When you discuss the values on Onpage with clients, please beware that they don't see the same info as we do with the Masterpass.

e.g. masterpass - http://take.ms/Z6CQO

regular pass - https://take.ms/chGqU

The extra pages (and the reason we don't display) are the 599 errors, which have unknown causes so we prefer to avoid mentioning them.

cards.title: Why do the calibration terms don't match the branded/non-branded keywords in Traffic?
cards.text: The calibration system uses the past 7 days, change the timeframe in the calendar for the past 7 days, and the numbers will match.Internal info:We're getting a smaller interval for performance reasons
cards.html:
The calibration system uses the past 7 days, change the timeframe in the calendar for the past 7 days, and the numbers will match.

Internal info:
We're getting a smaller interval for performance reasons and the brand list of keywords should not very that much over time and if it does it will be on low session keywords.
Besides that, they can also use the Brand rule option for other terms.

cards.title: Why do we crawl for 100 results?
cards.text: So we can get the top 100 results with a single crawl.Besides that, one important difference would be the results grouping - it's more likely that results from the same site will be grouped and/or rem
cards.html:
So we can get the top 100 results with a single crawl.


Besides that, one important difference would be the results grouping - it's more likely that results from the same site will be grouped and/or removed when viewing 10 results/page


It also makes sense from a UX perspective as it would be redundant to have a 10-results SERP showing a couple of features and 4-5 results from the same site

cards.title: Why do we overwrite the data of a draft campaign when it's converted to daily tracking?
cards.text: Because of a failsafe mechanism, "when a campaign is added after the daily rank crawling start (6 AM on their timezone), we also write ranks for the next day." This can lead to us displaying 2 days of
cards.html:
Because of a failsafe mechanism, "when a campaign is added after the daily rank crawling start (6 AM on their timezone), we also write ranks for the next day." This can lead to us displaying 2 days of data in a draft.

More shaping is in the making; see here.
Another related task here

cards.title: Why do we see differences between the CTR in OT and the one in GSC?
cards.text: Although our CTR (in Organic Traffic) and Google Search Console (GSC) CTR share the same name and reflect similar behaviour, they are calculated differently, so their values may not match.The GSC CTR
cards.html:
Although our CTR (in Organic Traffic) and Google Search Console (GSC) CTR share the same name and reflect similar behaviour, they are calculated differently, so their values may not match.

The GSC CTR is defined as clicks divided by impressions based on the data shown in GSC. For more details on how GSC calculates CTR, you can refer to their documentation.

Our CTR, however, is calculated using data we collect during our "not provided" distribution process. The key differences are:

  1. We do not store data for rows with 0 clicks.
  2. Our data is pulled from the keyword breakdown by landing page per day. This approach can result in different values compared to the keyword view in GSC, even if you try to recalculate the metrics in GSC.

cards.title: Why do we sometimes have unallocated traffic, though we have both GA+GSC?
cards.text: When a site has (tens or hundreds of) thousands of landing pages in Google Analytics, but a significantly lower number in Google Search Console, we (obviously) cannot "match" them.What we can notice i
cards.html:
When a site has (tens or hundreds of) thousands of landing pages in Google Analytics, but a significantly lower number in Google Search Console, we (obviously) cannot "match" them.

What we can notice is that these GA LPs are "thin traffic", for which GSC won't show keyword details with clicks.

We are still distributing them (correctly) as non-brand, but under "unallocated".

Note: There is a 10K LP limit in the exports we (CS) get from the platform. There is no limit on the interface, nor in processing the pages and their data.

cards.title: Why do you see a decrease in Visibility when adding keywords that bring additional search volumes to the pool of keywords?
cards.text: The Visibility Impact is initially calculated separately, the one for the ranks is not influenced by the search volumes and the one for the search volumes is not influenced by the ranks and only after
cards.html:
The Visibility Impact is initially calculated separately, the one for the ranks is not influenced by the search volumes and the one for the search volumes is not influenced by the ranks and only after this process is done they're calculated together.

Therefore, if you add keywords that bring additional search volumes (because they're bringing their own search volume to the already existing pool of keywords) but are ranking past top 20 (where Visibility is 0) they're actually affecting even more the Visibility because they have a higher search volume for terms they don't rank for.

cards.title: Why do you sometimes see a different number of clicks compared to GSC when selecting a longer timeframe? (also applies to GA sessions)
cards.text: We do daily distribution by matching the organic traffic and keyword data using the landing pages as common denominators to distribute the traffic data back into keywords.So, when you check the daily
cards.html:
We do daily distribution by matching the organic traffic and keyword data using the landing pages as common denominators to distribute the traffic data back into keywords.

So, when you check the daily clicks, they should correspond to the ones in GSC.
When you select a longer timeframe, there are some clicks that will get excluded from the total in SEOmonitor, as they have no or very few impressions. For one day's worth of traffic, they are important, but for a longer timeframe, some are insignificant.

Even in GSC, you always have "missing" data, when you refine it by query or landing page.

There's no way for us to know which ones will be excluded from a longer timeframe, as this happens when they have no impact per se.

If the total matches the one in GSC, the distribution has been correctly made.

Related to:

  • Understand how sampled data can affect your campaign
  • If they have GA connected - Why does my (localized) campaign show more traffic than the GA4 profile?

cards.title: Why does my (localized) campaign show more traffic than the GA4 profile?
cards.text: Google Analytics recently implemented data threshold in their interface. This results in limited access to data in a report or exploration.Data thresholds are applied to prevent anyone viewing a repor
cards.html:
Google Analytics recently implemented data threshold in their interface. This results in limited access to data in a report or exploration.
Data thresholds are applied to prevent anyone viewing a report or exploration from inferring the identity or sensitive information of individual users based on demographics, interests, or other signals present in the data.

The data we display comes from Google Analytics API, where they provide the full numbers; this is the reason why the numbers look like they do not match.

So it looks like your options would be:

  • implement in your GA profile the different indications offered by Google's support
  • try to create different GA views by country, in case you opted for localized traffic in your campaign.

Here is the info regarding how to make the checks in GA.

____________________________________________________________________________________________

Internal info. Do not share this directly with the client

This is an accurate and realistic explanation.

Because the main idea behind GA4 is for Google to save money on servers dedicated to supporting Analytics. Frankly, GA UA used to be too good for a free product.
...
In other words, plain simple summing might be too expensive for GA4 to conduct in that report, so it uses advanced algorithms that do sums faster, but less accurate.

Related:

  • Why do you sometimes see a different number of clicks compared to GSC when selecting a longer timeframe? (also applies to GA sessions)
  • Understand how sampled data can affect your campaign

cards.title: Why does the Chrome Extension only show keywords on which the landing page is not ranking (yet)?
cards.text: As the page is not yet ranking on any keyword, the Chrome Extension identifies relevant keywords on which it could rank in the future.
cards.html: As the page is not yet ranking on any keyword, the Chrome Extension identifies relevant keywords on which it could rank in the future.

cards.title: Why does the LP have traffic data, but the associated keywords have less or none?
cards.text: When checking a specific timeframe, the landing page view tells the compiled story of that period's dates.Analysing each day individually, you should be able to check that:the page is present in GSC f
cards.html:
When checking a specific timeframe, the landing page view tells the compiled story of that period's dates.

Analysing each day individually, you should be able to check that:

  • the page is present in GSC for a day when it does not register conversions in GA,
    and
  • it's not present in GSC on the day/s when the page registers conversions.

cards.title: Why does the Romania localisation has the EN language option but not DE or HU?
cards.text: For the google.ro engine, Google offers four languages: RO, HU, DE and EN.The reason why we decided only to add EN, is that some sites are added on the Romanian market but written in English. In such
cards.html:
For the google.ro engine, Google offers four languages: RO, HU, DE and EN.

The reason why we decided only to add EN, is that some sites are added on the Romanian market but written in English.
In such cases, the Magic Keywords option would identify Romanian keywords because we ask AI to return keywords in the same language as the search engine indicates.

So, the compromise was to add EN to Romania.
This was done only for a very small and limited number of other countries (e.g. Switzerland) even though, in practice, an English website might be created for any market in the world.

cards.title: Why does the Search Volume in Trends differ from the one in Topics?
cards.text: The reason why the 2 values are different is because we are not comparing the same data:The search volume from Trends - refers to the search volume of the last available month vs. that of the same mon
cards.html:
The reason why the 2 values are different is because we are not comparing the same data:

  • The search volume from Trends - refers to the search volume of the last available month vs. that of the same month in the past year.

  • The search volume in Topics is an average for the last 12 months.

  • The search volume in the app is also an average for the last 12 months

cards.title: Why does the YOY explainer only show 12 months?
cards.text: An integrity check is made in the application for SV to display YOY. To display YOY for last month, we must have Search Volume for at least 90% of the active keywords in the campaign. If we don't have
cards.html:
An integrity check is made in the application for SV to display YOY.

To display YOY for last month, we must have Search Volume for at least 90% of the active keywords in the campaign.

If we don't have Search Volume for at least 90% of the keywords, we display YOY as shown below.

The date the SV is updated can fluctuate quite a bit.

cards.title: Why don't I get Additional Conversions in my Forecast?
cards.text: Either your GA is not connected or there are no conversions registered there for your tracked keywords.Additional Conversions - The number of additional conversions estimated to be received, in total,
cards.html:
Either your GA is not connected or there are no conversions registered there for your tracked keywords.

Additional Conversions - The number of additional conversions estimated to be received, in total, through the forecast period, if the targeted ranks are reached, and corresponding to the additional number of visits estimated.

cards.title: Why don't my archived keywords go to the Vault?
cards.text: Most probably, you're in a draft campaign.It's expected behaviour that the archived keywords in a draft campaign don't go to the Vault.You can still upload keywords to the Vault manually, though.
cards.html:
Most probably, you're in a draft campaign.

It's expected behaviour that the archived keywords in a draft campaign don't go to the Vault.
You can still upload keywords to the Vault manually, though.

cards.title: Why don't we show Not Provided segmentation for Custom Segments?
cards.text: Custom segments don't access the Not Provided solution because we cannot correlate the keywords to the Landing Pages, and so they remain unallocated.Custom Segments are created by filtering keyword at
cards.html:
Custom segments don't access the Not Provided solution because we cannot correlate the keywords to the Landing Pages, and so they remain unallocated.

Custom Segments are created by filtering keyword attributes. The keywords and their attributes come from the allocated traffic. And Unallocated is the traffic that could not be distributed to keywords, so we can’t further segment it.

cards.title: Why in different folders you can't have a group with the same name?
cards.text: you can only assign keywords to groups in the CSV therefore when using this upload method the app would not be able to properly identify the group and assign the keywords.API calls only require the gr
cards.html:
• you can only assign keywords to groups in the CSV therefore when using this upload method the app would not be able to properly identify the group and assign the keywords.

  • API calls only require the group name so same as above, the app won't be able to identify which group you want data from.

cards.title: Why is my keyword ranking 99+ when I see it registering clicks in GSC?
cards.text: It’s totally normal. The rank is a snapshot in time from a single location. The clicks in GSC are the sum of all clicks done in a timeframe from all locations.GSC also registers the clicks from person
cards.html:
It’s totally normal. The rank is a snapshot in time from a single location. The clicks in GSC are the sum of all clicks done in a timeframe from all locations.

GSC also registers the clicks from personalized results (not incognito). If I’m a frequent user of seomonitor.com, it might include results from SEOmonitor.com at the top for me. That would not be reflected in the rank.

cards.title: Why is the Brand Folder separate and you can't move the keywords it contains?
cards.text: There are three reasons the app doesn't allow users to move the brand keywords from the automatically created Brand Folder:The brand queries are going to be navigational, that's why they usually have
cards.html:
There are three reasons the app doesn't allow users to move the brand keywords from the automatically created Brand Folder:

  1. The brand queries are going to be navigational, that's why they usually have Full-site links - if I don't scroll, I wouldn't even see any other site. Google understands that most people want to get their info and go to that site. Bear in mind that Apple is considered "brand", iPhone is not, for example. 

  2. They will also (usually) have high search volumes.

  3. You would not be doing SEO on them. You'd probably just want to keep an eye on them and make sure they're not moving anywhere from the 1st rank

All of the above would generate some big problems if we were to mix the brand keywords with the rest of the portfolio:

  • Because they (almost) always rank #1, the Visibility on them would be 100%.

  • Mixing them would mean that the overall portfolio visibility would be artificially increased, with a high baseline, allowing for only a few "degrees" of movement, as the spectrum is decreased.

  • This "mix" would also make the Competition module irrelevant, as you'd always be #1 and the competitors would not be ranking on your brand keywords. The high search volumes would also mean that if your visibility would be artificially increased, theirs would be artificially decreased, proportionately.

cards.title: Why is there a difference between the number of the keywords the folder counts and the added number of the groups it holds? (Rank tracker)
cards.text: The folder counts the overall number of unique keywords.So if you have a folder that holds 3 groups, with 10, 7 and 5 keywords and there is a keyword that you are tracking in all 3 groups, the folder
cards.html:
The folder counts the overall number of unique keywords.

So if you have a folder that holds 3 groups, with 10, 7 and 5 keywords and there is a keyword that you are tracking in all 3 groups, the folder will count a total of 20 keywords, instead of 22 as one might expect when adding the numbers displayed by each group.

cards.title: Why is there a discrepancy between the GA4 and traffic in the revenue metric?
cards.text: Users may notice a discrepancy in conversion value (revenue) between the platform and GA4 when using the "Goals" conversion type in their traffic configuration settings.This occurs because GA4's API d
cards.html:
Users may notice a discrepancy in conversion value (revenue) between the platform and GA4 when using the "Goals" conversion type in their traffic configuration settings.

This occurs because GA4's API does not allow revenue to be fetched by individual events when "Goals" is selected. Instead, it pulls the total revenue from all events, which may not align with the user's expected revenue data.

To get more accurate revenue tracking, users should consider switching to the "E-commerce" conversion type. This setting aligns with GA4’s 'purchaseRevenue' metric, which Google defines as:

"The sum of revenue from purchases minus refunded transaction revenue made in your app or site. Purchase revenue sums the revenue for these events: purchase, ecommerce_purchase, in_app_purchase, app_store_subscription_convert, and app_store_subscription_renew. Purchase revenue is specified by the value parameter in tagging."

By using "E-commerce" as the conversion type, users will see revenue data that better reflects purchase-related transactions, ensuring a more accurate comparison between the platform and GA4.

cards.title: Why the image doesn't adjust when I resize the window
cards.text: You might want to check the resolution, as we don't support resolutions under 1280.
cards.html: You might want to check the resolution, as we don't support resolutions under 1280.

cards.title: Why there are sometimes big differences between the sessions in SEOmonitor and UA 360?
cards.text: The GA API sometimes returns the wrong data for longer intervals (so not the small differences that are normal but rather very big discrepancies between GA and SEOmonitor) but it retrieves them correc
cards.html:
The GA API sometimes returns the wrong data for longer intervals (so not the small differences that are normal but rather very big discrepancies between GA and SEOmonitor) but it retrieves them correctly for a single day.


We do have a solution for this, you can switch the user's campaign to UA 360 from admin, which will enable day-by-day processing only for this campaign and only for UA 360:

cards.title: Why users might see little or no progress in the first month of tracking?
cards.text: In the beginning, you start planning the actions, then you start executing by creating content and fixing technical issues which takes time.After these actions are followed through, the updates will b
cards.html:
In the beginning, you start planning the actions, then you start executing by creating content and fixing technical issues which takes time.

After these actions are followed through, the updates will be published, Google needs to find and index them, and only after that, the ranks will start improving and the traffic will accumulate over time.

cards.title: Will GA4 bring changes to the Traffic metrics?
cards.text: NO. Even though the way the metrics are defined or calculated by Google Analytics might change, their names will remain unchanged.As of October 2021, Google Analytics began updating the calculation me
cards.html:
NO.
Even though the way the metrics are defined or calculated by Google Analytics might change, their names will remain unchanged.

As of October 2021, Google Analytics began updating the calculation method for session metrics in your standard and custom reports and in Explorations and Looker Studio to more efficiently count sessions with high accuracy and low error rate.

So, what counts as a session in GA4, for example?

A session initiates when a user either:

  • Opens your app in the foreground

  • Views a page or screen and no session is currently active (e.g. their previous session has timed out)

By default, a session ends (times out) after 30 minutes of user inactivity. There is no limit to how long a session can last.

Some more details can be found in Analytic's help centere here.

cards.title: Will the data be reprocessed if I update the AI Search brand rule terms?
cards.text: YESBut we don't redo the AI Search crawls; we're only taking the already existing AI responses and re-parsing them for brand detection. This does not use AI or any other external resources, so it's ve
cards.html:
YES

But we don't redo the AI Search crawls; we're only taking the already existing AI responses and re-parsing them for brand detection.
This does not use AI or any other external resources, so it's very fast.

The interface doesn't display a processing state while this happens.

cards.text: Yes, they will have to re-do them; however, we will not sunset the old version for a while, so they have enough time to do so. (the timeline has not yet be decided)
cards.html: Yes, they will have to re-do them; however, we will not sunset the old version for a while, so they have enough time to do so. (the timeline has not yet be decided)

cards.title: Wizard - Competitors step - competitors shown/not shown
cards.text: Our system is designed to check for competitors in Topics or SerpStat, and to show the findings in this step of the Wizard.The user has the option to select more competitors, to deselect them or add o
cards.html:
Our system is designed to check for competitors in Topics or SerpStat, and to show the findings in this step of the Wizard.

The user has the option to select more competitors, to deselect them or add others manually.

There may be cases when we don't find any competitors, hence not suggesting any, so the user would need to add them manually.

When no or less than 10 competitors are found, placeholders will be displayed (gray circles) that can be filled with the manually added options.

cards.title: Wizard - automatic search, number of Keywords displayed
cards.text: When the Automatic keywords research is used, we display the number of Found keywords at this stage (in Topics and other used tools). The second part of the message 'we'll automatically add the most r
cards.html:
When the Automatic keywords research is used, we display the number of Found keywords at this stage (in Topics and other used tools).

The second part of the message 'we'll automatically add the most relevant ones' applies afterward, when our filters and cleanup is made.

Conclusion: the number of Found keywords may not match with the number of keywords added in the campaign.

cards.title: W​hen do we assign "Google Ads Reaggregation" to keywords
cards.text: W​e assign this to keywords that have had a sudden change in Search Volume in Google Ads from one month to another from 10 to 1000 or from 1000 to 0, for example.If a keyword has both re-aggregated an
cards.html:
W​e assign this to keywords that have had a sudden change in Search Volume in Google Ads from one month to another from 10 to 1000 or from 1000 to 0, for example.

If a keyword has both re-aggregated and Google Ads Reaggregation we show it as Google Ads Reaggregation.

This usually happens when keywords get re-aggregated.

When more than one such event happens for a keyword, we only save the latest. This alters the Visibility for the days and timeframes when the previous ones happen because they get un-flagged.

For example: We had a case where a keyword had multiple switches in a short period of time:

16 - 26 May, the "platform bed frames" shows ﻿, an SV increase from 2.4k to 2.9K ﻿https://take.ms/u2L0rN.

27-28 May, it shows another increase from 2.9 to 90.5k ﻿https://take.ms/9ENyw ﻿

Then in June there was another event, which is the only one we can see in the explainer:

- This is up for discussion for Product Backlog here.

cards.text: Everything below +200% is represented in +-%.Everything between +200% and +999% is represented with x2-x10: Everything above +1000% is represented with an icon for trending icon (“newcomer”):
cards.html:
• Everything below +200% is represented in +-%.

  • Everything between +200% and +999% is represented with x2-x10:

  • Everything above +1000% is represented with an icon for trending icon (“newcomer”):

cards.title: [ONBOARDING] Alex Case Study - SID: 47694
cards.text: SID: 47694NAME: LittlewoodsirelandCASE: How can we look at the performance, to correctly understand and assess it?RESPONSE:VS score has decreased over the past 12 mo however we can see in the VS expla
cards.html:
SID: 47694

NAME: Littlewoodsireland

CASE: How can we look at the performance, to correctly understand and assess it?

RESPONSE:

  • VS score has decreased over the past 12 mo however we can see in the VS explainer the reasons for this decrease:
    a. Search trend changes.
    b. We have multiple KW added annotations as well as Google Algo updates.

  • We have multiple KWs that fell from the 1st/2nd page to 90+
    a. There are 10+ events for some of the KWs in regards to landing pages changes which inevitably affected the rank.

  • Most impacted group was Clothing and Footwear which covers a total of 1.1M SV out of the 1.8M.
    a. We can see multiple annotations for words added and also changes. 
    b. Most KWs fell from the 1st page due to LP changes that have a decent SV amount.
    c. SV explainer outlines all of the above mentioned.

    By analysing the forecasting done for the customer and by the data provided above, overall the performance of this account seems it has taken a dive. However, as per forecasting, even if the SV are not the ones forecasted, the visibility score is on target.

OBSERVATIONS AFTER REVIEW:

For a performance review, the best course of action would be to segment the graph to isolate losses or wins.

  • Set advanced filter -> date added -> choose a date -> Greater/Less then will show you KWs that were before/after. This will allow you to track the performance of specific KWs over a specific timeframe.

  • VS Score -> KWs get increased/decrease SVs. Example: 100 rank 1-20 KWs SV 1Mil. 100 rank 99+ KWs have 1Mil SV. Total Campaign 2Mil SV. VS score will be 50% as we have 1 mil of good rank KW vs 1 mil of bad rank KW from the total of 2Mil. If we get 1 more KW added to the 99+ ranks with another 1Mil SV for a total of 3MIl SV ( 1Mil good ranks, 2Mil bad ranks) the VS will decrease to 33.33% as only a 1/3Mil SV have an impression share rank 1-20.

  • Organic Traffic performance/overall review - We have 3 important aspects we should look at. Grey bars are the SVs for the respective mo. The Blue bar is the traffic that you had and the VS line. Overall the Traffic should be at the same level of the SV. If it is lower then there were some issues there that can be looked at. The VS and Traffic bar can be lower than the SV bar or the VS can be constant while the traffic bar will fluctuate based on the SV or outside of it (further research would be needed to see exactly what happened).

Links to help better understand the last point:

https://help.seomonitor.com/en/articles/876937-understanding-the-non-brand-organic-traffic-trends

https://help.seomonitor.com/en/articles/865939-the-brand-non-brand-segmentation-explained

cards.title: [ONBOARDING] Alex Case Study - SID: 55545
cards.text: SID: 55545NAME: P&O FerriesCASE: Lack of sessions and overall reviewRESPONSE: The lack of session on the graph and the organic traffic even though we don't have a missing GA warning is due to the fact
cards.html:
SID: 55545

NAME: P&O Ferries

CASE: Lack of sessions and overall review

RESPONSE:

The lack of session on the graph and the organic traffic even though we don't have a missing GA warning is due to the fact that upon checking Bo$$ulica we can see that a "default" GA profile was linked on behalf of Upswing in order to import and set-up their campaign.

We also found a word that seemed to not be related to their site: Pando. The KW has a high SV and if it is not relevant for their campaign can negatively influence the results as it would not reflect the real status of the KW Group/overall campaign.

You can access the KW page and try to set a time period in the past to see when the KW was initially added as that is the max date in the past you can select.

OBSERVATIONS AFTER REVIEW:
