cards.title: [Onboarding] Alex - 10-15 June 2019
cards.text: Diverse Entrepreneur - College Student in Economics. Happy about the tool, questions about the credits duration. Will recommend tool to her colleagues as there is interest in SEO in her school circle.
cards.html:
Diverse Entrepreneur - College Student in Economics. Happy about the tool, questions about the credits duration. Will recommend tool to her colleagues as there is interest in SEO in her school circle.


Ken Marshall - Freelancer looking for a tool that will reduce the manual work that accompanies SEO. Our tool seemed to fit him like a glove, overall positive feedback.


Observations during calls - the presentations seemed a bit inconsistent. Between the two calls, they didn't seem to follow a pattern or an order of presentation. Ideally you would be able to read the person on the other end and adapt to it however it seemed like there were more jumps from one topic to another rather than an organic and cursive presentation. As an example, one call had Signals presented while another did not. Neither calls discussed the content performance module.

Tool issue discovered during call - While talking about the competitor module, Cristina showed the customers the Magnifying glass option which lead to the compare tab. For both calls the compare tabs showed the same rank for both the competition and the customer. Also the customer rank is different than what it shows in the compare tab. Slightly weird if any of the customer noticed the issue during a pitch call.


Donna - colposcopie / All KWs rank 4 / competition compare has it as rank 5. What is the update time frame of the competition module? - Fixed

———————————

Finance Workshop

Tool information is pretty self explanatory and easy to use from my point of view. There were a few questions from my end for a few items:

Webhook - the “api” thru’ which chargebee pulls information from SEO.

Payment History - shows all of the payments that have been made in order to fulfil current invoice. (DWF - total invoice 6k - made 4-6 bank transfers to a total of 6k as required)

Can a SEO user have multiple subscription plans on the same chargebee account - no, the user can have multiple customers for that one account in different campaigns however we only track his 1 account and have 1 plan for him. 

Everything else was pretty standard and a situation of “I need to hit my head on the wall to figure out how painful it is”.

———————————-

Continued playing around in Elefant campaign and Donna medical, looking at the issues that had been present in the past and see if they had been adressed.

———————————-

Eland Consulting Call

Uses internal ranking tool / SEMrush - Overall a great presentation. They were so impressed that they started creating the account with us on the call.

———————————

Call Maria - Holiday Autos

Read articles / Video - Saw the vid, didn’t have time for the articles regarding the main metrics.

Using SEMrush all KWs are equal and doesn’t take into account the SV.

Maria: How to use smart groups - clear presentation explained filters like “and” / “or” functions.

User: How to track words that are not in smart groups?

  • My initial understanding of the question was incorrect as I assumed he was asking where he could see the KWs that were not part of smart groups.

  • However the questions itself was related to historical data for the respective Keywords which Smart Groups only keep the last 30 days. The user would have to manually look up the word for the respective period to see rank changes

Normal group - has historical data

Smart group - contains the last 30 days.

Move keywords to normal groups

Add 1 KW to multiple groups

How to see in which group my kw is - download CSV for all groups or click individual KWs for a breakdown of groups at the top left. Later edit: You can also view which groups the KWs are part of if you click on the black folder in front of the KW from the list.

Overall a great call, no negatives or additional observations to add.

———————————

CS Meeting - same old. 


Main point of discord was the additional research to be done in order to provide a more detailed and accurate answer to the customers. The question came in the form of an import when the customer asked why there are discrepancies between what he saw in SEMrush and what we present to him. Main concern was in regards to position 0 and how exactly SEM rush calculates the featured snippet. After engaging SEMrush support Mircea was able to obtain an answer to the question.


Maria saw this approach as not being OK, my assumption being the fear that they would have to do this on a regular basis. The conversation ensued as to why “they” would need to do this and why the customer can’t just accept it the way it is. After some minutes the subject was changes but I felt like we needed to revert back as there was no clear conclusion to why there is a problem with this approach. There was no clear win here.

——————————-

Maintenance meeting 


Trainline mobile ranks had been discussed again, there was a discrepancy between the ranks displayed in our tool and actual ranks. Dragos fixed it and started a re-crawl. Unfortunately it took two scrawls to have it fixed eventually.

Another pending issue was the fact the the migrations were done slow or incorrectly. Dragos proposed a workshop in order to better align the team and explain the situation . Each member is supposed to detail their understanding of the tool and compare notes after the meeting.

Had a misunderstanding in regards to the crawl on demand function. Is it available to all users or admins only. Clarifications came in that its widely available but only for the current day. 

———————————

Migration steps - had a meeting where Ana presented the migration process overall. Unfortunately there were no brand new cases as the information submitted was incorrect from the customer side. She did show me the theoretical process and I was able to assist in double checking if some of the customer migration were done correctly. Went thru about 5 customers, all good.

———————————

1-on-1 with Raluca

A quick discussion about our backgrounds and the overall business vision in our day to day activities. 

End of Log.

cards.title: [Onboarding] Alex - The cataclysmic event that has been my first week without Mada.
cards.text: Spent time with the CS Peeps. Ana - covered some basic KWs not found "issues" (aggregations) and a more complicated scenario where the customer imported multiple CSV files or a single one where clearl
cards.html:
Spent time with the CS Peeps.

Ana - covered some basic KWs not found "issues" (aggregations) and a more complicated scenario where the customer imported multiple CSV files or a single one where clearly something had gone wrong as all the Groups/Folders where numbers and all the KWs had numbers instead of names. They requested assistance in clearing their campaign aka delete the KWs and Folders. Ana was tasked with manually deleting all of the customer groups manually as there isn't a select all groups function for deletion.

Participated in the Ladbrokes call.

Key points:

  1. Most interesting aspect from the tool for them was the competition dashboard and how they can customize it to clearly track the competition on specific keywords and their visibility on them.

  2. One issue that they had was the uploading of keywords where there was some confusion as to how it can be achieved and that it took a prolongue period of time to do so. My takeaway from the conversation was that the person in charge of this didn't put in the effort to actually do the work since Ana insisted on showing us where exactly he had problems with the upload however the person in charge of it "Andrew" refused at 1st to do so invoking different reason while ultimately dropping the subject.**

  3. Their major complaints have been the slow loading and responsiveness of the tool itself. It did not help that Ana had a hard time on her laptop due to the fact that she suffered from the same issues they were complaining about while on my laptop everything was running smooth.

  4. Dudi re-iterated a few times that he loves the tool however the people using it are not found of its use due to the responsiveness issues and sluggish response. At this point and time he is the manager and they are using it because of his enforcement however he does not want to be the person that frustrates his team.

Observations from the customer:

  1. While presenting Topics, Dudi asked to look up the words "Horse Racing" and pointed out that nowhere in our KW list does it show Chelsea Stadium (one of the key horse racing locations) and that the algorithm we use might not be fully fledged or complete from his point of view.

  2. The second part was focused on the words "fa cup". With the championship just finalized, Dudi pointed out that the search trends for the words in topics did not show an increase for May when the championship was in full swing. Meaning that once again our algorithm might not be up to date with the search trends for specific words as it seems to have been this case.

Positives:

  1. Customer does want to keep using our tool.

  2. He is willing to talk about SEOmonitor at conferences.

  3. He wants to work with us on improving our algorithms to help them and also us in the long run. "He feels like we can both grow from this improved collaboration".

Negatives:

  1. From my point of view, the customer might drop us if we cannot fix the issue they have due to team frustration.

Mircea - more complex cases most relevant one: missing keywords from groups although they showed as being tracked. They were not aggregated and Mircea opened a Trello card for the issue. Also the landing pages for the client showed a different customer. An older trello card was submitted for a similar issue and Mircea added the customer to the respective card. A temporary fix was provided by Dragos to no longer show the page for the respective customer until a good solution could be identified to fix the ongoing problem.

When Mircea went back to check how the landing page would look like, the missing KW could be located so in a nutshell the missing KW was no longer an issue and we are working on a better solution for the missing Landing page capture.

In my "free time" i kept browsing the Elefant campaign and identified that they had a severe drop in session in the past few days. After consulting with Mircea as to why that would happen and asking the CS team overall why there was an annotation with "pages offline" on their graph (to which no one seemed to have a clear answer), we went to Larisa to check if they were aware of the drop. Larisa informed us that it was due to an issue on their end with a migration to a different server. The overall issue was dropped as we had a clear answer it was an issue on the customer end and not ours however there were a few key points that I learned.

  1. There is a dashboard called /onpage that shows you the traffic for each landing page and if there are any issues with it. - correction /onpage displays only pages with errors.

  2. Each landing page has a section called Error Code.
    a. When looking up the error codes individually on google, i was able to understand how exactly google treats these pages as most of the error logs were linked to pages not being found or unable to reach them due to a proxy issue. In layman's terms, that easily translates in the pages being "offline" as the annotation on the graph had told us.
    b. Upon our review of the landing page, we could see a high volume of traffic that was being missed by Elefant and in the followup discussion with Larisa we could see a discrepancy between the numbers seen by her on the respective page and ours. Mircea was unaware as to why that would happen and I assumed it was because we were admins and she was not. This was confirmed by Marius as on the /onpage tab we only display clear error codes such as 404 not found and not codes like 599 which talks about the fact that the page could not be reached due to a proxy error or firewall blocking access (something that is not clearly defined and would reside solely on the customer side). In a nutshell Larisa would only see the 404 error codes while we could see the 599 as well, leading to a discrepancy between the two profiles identified earlier.

Meeting with Larisa:

  1. During their yearly review presentation for the customer, we noticed that a huge chunk of the customers traffic was missing from June to November in 2017. Due to that gap where the customer had 0 traffic, the girls decided to use GA for their review which did have traffic registered for that period. It was clearly a tool issue at this point.

  2. While preparing the customer documentation, they noticed that even with their assistance, the traffic to the website had gone down instead of increasing. After further research, it was uncovered that the customer had huge traffic on his blogs which were no longer being maintained and no effort on his end was made to respect the plan they had put forth for his website. A few filters later in GA, Larisa isolated the Blog pages from the rest of the site and there was a clear increase in traffic for the landing pages they had been focusing on however the drop in traffic overall still showed a decrease. The goals set forth by Upswing were reached for the particular targets even if the traffic was lower based on previous years.

  3. There isn't much more to add here due to the fact that GA was used instead of our tool.

    Negative points:
    1. There were some issue in selecting the timeframe they wanted as the calendar date selection can be confusing when looking at a longer period of time (Years).

After asking the CS team in regards to why that gap could have occurred there were no clear responses on general ideas as to why that might have happened until we had the conversation today.

Maintenance Meeting: Clarification on what is critical and a priority in regards to the Trello Cards and how the CS team still have issues logging correct tickets.

End of story!

cards.title: [Template] DEMO Call - Cartrawler Template Example (Alex' Onboarding)
cards.text: Audience: arodgers@cartrawler.com (email with which he created the account - andynrodgers@gmail.com) Purpose: DemoCall prep:Questions submitted by the customer: I am interested in an explanation of vi
cards.html:
Audience: arodgers@cartrawler.com (email with which he created the account - andynrodgers@gmail.com)

Purpose: Demo

Call prep:

Questions submitted by the customer:

  • I am interested in an explanation of visibility score.

  • How SEO Monitor deals with the grouped search volume that Google provide. E.g. Car Hire and Hire Car show the same volume as they are grouped together.

  • How I can see savings on PPC.

  • How I can see what GSC profile is being referenced (as I never specified one at set up).

  • Which goals and revenue figures are being referenced (as again I never specified one at set up).

Documentation sent to the customer: Yes (not sure which ones).

Call Summary:

He did watch the videos sent however the documentation was not given a thorough read for the ones with the main metrics.

Overall a good call where all of the modules where discussed and explained to the customer along side the VS and other core metrics that we use. The customer had multiple follow-up questions along the way which will be addressed below.

He was impressed by Topics in regards to the curation that we do compared to other tools like SEMrush where you are provided a huge amount of data which you then have to manually research.

There was some confusion around aggregation which was addressed during the call. There was also an in-depth conversation around folders/smart groups which led to numerous questions. All call questions are listed below.

Customer Questions during the call:

Q1: How do you know that the connected GSCs are the correct ones
A: If multiple GSC profiles are detected, there is a warning in our tool that tells you that you should connect other profiles as well. This can also be checked in Account Setting -> Website settings. Article Link.

Q2: How is the brand traffic identified and how does it know that the unallocated is non-brand?
A: Based on the Brand rules set up during the the registration process, our algorithm will filter the words set by you in to brand and non-brand. In cases where some brand words do slip through, you can mark them as brand to have them removed from the non-brand section. Article Link.

Q3: How can I see in what group my keywords are, move my keywords into other groups and is the same keyword counted multiple times if in different groups (pay wise), add new keywords.
A: There are multiple options for seeing in which group the keyword are situated in: Open the specific keyword page and check in the top left (Group Tag), hover over the folder in the Keyword list in campaign view or download a CSV to see all Keywords and all groups. You can move keywords by selecting it in the list and pressing the Move keyword button at the button and we do not charge for word duplication but for each individual keyword. This means you can duplicate words across groups and not be charged for them. Keywords can be added from the Research module via copy paste, automatic search, CSV import or from the Topics platform if you have a subscription (this also applied to the Topics Extension). Groups Article Link / Research Keywords Link

Q4: If I need to track more markets and for different languages, how does that work?
A: Unfortunately we cannot have one campaign with different languages and markets. This will require separate campaigns for each individual requirement. Article Link.

Q5: Will I know when Google re-aggregates keywords?
A: Whenever there is an aggregation or any significant changes from Google, there will be annotations on the graph to clearly indicate it. There is also the option to click on the square to the right of the keywords for a pop-up listing all aggregated words and also the event column should indicate that changes have occurred for that specific keyword.

Call Observations: Customer had a large number of questions some of them repeating over the course of a few minutes even if the explanation for them was given which could indicate he was not able to assimilate the information fast or well enough even if it was explained in a clear and simple manner (reading the documentation before hand would have definitely helped).

Call Feedback: Perhaps a review of some of our documentation or quick glance over our help page to show the customer where he can get the answers to most of his questions could have helped.

cards.title: [Template] DEMO Call - Onstrategy - 25 June 2019 (Mada Onboarding)
cards.text: Example of template usage: Link.Call recordings can also be linked for ease of access: link1; link2;Audience: ShannonPurpose: Demo/Training/Q&ACustomer Initial Feedback:Positives:  - Great potential:
cards.html:
Example of template usage: Link.

Call recordings can also be linked for ease of access: link1; link2;

Audience: Shannon

Purpose: Demo/Training/Q&A

Customer Initial Feedback:

Positives:

  - Great potential:

  - Surpasses SEMrush in more than just a keyword ranking application.

  - Visually much better than SEMrush

  - I was very excited to use more of the features, but got stuck (details

  below)

Negatives:

  - A bit overwhelming with the number of features.

  - I really did not know where to start and then got stuck, even

     though we did do a demo.

  - Emailed customer service about a few issues and never got a response.

  (not sure if this was on their end or our’s?)

 Keyword reporting:

     - This is the *number one* feature we need.

     - There was no way to tag keywords (I need that functionality for my

     reporting or organizing purposes)

     - The keyword report was only in CSV form.

     - That was fine, except it added another step to my reporting.

     - That, coupled with no tagging, meant that I went back the

        SEMrush and stopped investigating this tool.

     - This is where I stopped using SEO monitor and went back to SEMrush.

Call Summary:

They’re launching a new app, so they’ll be working around that in the near future.

Using SEMrush a lot. 5’ reporting. #eyeroll

The main issue is due to how the (weekly) KW reporting is being done. Customer is used with putting tags on words in a “bucket” and then exporting it to Excel with the tags attached. That makes it easy to identify them and pull them out.

For the past 10 years, the customer has been using excel as a presentation method to the team due to the fact that KW research companies have come and gone and excel seems to be the most reliable. We only offer CSV (presumably for technical reasons).

The customer uses specific keywords, that they use to report on. Customer inquired about the export method and we offered two solutions

  1. one of them being the fact that they can use the export all keywords

  2. the regular groups export.

Keyword reporting:

Mada provided a quick explanation of how the weekly reporting works on our end. They can select the specific groups that they want to receive reports on via PDF format.

There’s also the standard “export”, that they knew, but didn’t like the default CSV format.

Smart groups have been presented to the customer and how they work.

Customer inquired about the viability of our work considering that companies like this have come and gone. Made provided reassurance that we can handle anything coming our way :)

Asked if we’re sharing industry knowledge, similar to how Moz is writing on their blog.

Any blog posts from our end would be related to our tool and how we are doing, instead of focusing on external materials since there are numerous other sources for that.

A short discussion about the algo changes done by Google in regards to the medical field and attempted to show the customer if the annotation was also present on charts - pending result - GREAT SUCCESS! It worked, cue applause.

Presented the possibility of having manual annotations on the graph to represent the launch of a platform or changes to the website overall.

Explained to the customer how we handle not-provided and how we present the KW that could bring traffic and what is currently being tracked. Compared to SEMrush where we were asked how reliable is our tool compared to the competition (which is not). GA and GSC correlation were explained, which resulted in the results in organic traffic with brand / non-brand explanations as well.

Went more in-depth in regards to landing pages and the KW that respond to them, further detailing the link that we do between GA and GSC.

Competition insight module - standard explanation of how competition insight works and how different competitions can be set up per group. Pretty much the standard explanation of the competition insight all with export CSV option…. COMPLAINED A LOT OF CSV, DEAL WITH IT.

Customer Questions during the call:

Q1: How can the customer “tag” / “label” keywords?

A: After clarifications, it seems the customer was simply inquiring how they can group words together and have easy access to exporting specific KWs / groups, which we can fully support.

Q2: How does exporting works on our end?

A: We allow exporting in CSV for multiple modules and some specific areas.

Q3: How does SEOmonitor handle the infinite loop?

A: This was in regards to the “questions” feature snippet that could potentially provide you with all of the answers to your questions without the need to scroll down the SERP page to the other results. “Infinite questions and answers dropdowns”.

Q4: How do we handle algorithm changes as MOZ provide Blogs for updates?

A: SEOmonitor provides clear annotations on the VS graph within the tool that provide clarity.

Q5: Can we track multiple competitors per group?

A: Yes, this is standard functionality.

Call Observations: Overall the customer was simply overwhelmed with all of the information that we provide in the tool but is really excited to start working with us. The overall call refresher went great!

Call Issues: The magnifying glass (in Compare) did not work during customer presentation/compare. But it did work on the Keyword page in the Competition tab.

Call Feedback: Possible issue with Hangouts that could generate speed issues. The platform loaded fine before the call; however during it, it was sluggish.

cards.title: [Template] Training Call - Performics 17 June 2019 - (Alex' Onboarding)
cards.text: Audience: Manager + 10 SEO team members. (PoC - Person of Contact - name preferably)Purpose: Onboarding/Training - SID: 56116Call prep:Questions submitted by the customer: None. (to my knowledge)Docum
cards.html:
Audience: Manager + 10 SEO team members. (PoC - Person of Contact - name preferably)

Purpose: Onboarding/Training - SID: 56116

Call prep:

  • Questions submitted by the customer: None. (to my knowledge)

  • Documentation sent to the customer: None. (to my knowledge)

Call Summary:

The call started off with a Dashboard overview for the customer. The visibility score was explained as well as the different metrics that get tracked (on target, budget etc). Customer asked how Visibility score works.

SEOmonitor Help page was referenced as it contains multiple articles that are highly beneficial for the customer in fully understanding the more technical aspects of each function.

We moved into Managed where the Sidebar was discussed and all of its functionalities where explained, including group/folder functions and the Visibility Impact pop-up, moving KWs and how the archive option works.

Search volumes on the sidebar folder icons and also how the YoY worked which lead the conversation into historical data. Migrations do contain historic ranks while newly imported/added words do not have any historic ranks.

The KWs event column was detailed as it can show you issues or events that impacted your rank for respective KWs.

The difference between Opportunity and Difficulty was explained where oppty represents the search volume increase you would benefit from if the KW reaches top 3 while the difficulty explains how hard it would be to get there.

We reverted back to the Organic Traffic where the importance of having the GA and GSC connected was brought up as it is a key point that needs to be completed in order for us to pull data and display it. This is also where the unallocated algorithm comes in as it allows us to mix and match the data from GA and GSC in order to correlate searched KWs with Landing pages.

In the Research module the methods of adding KWs were discussed, manually look up the KWs, automatically add them or use the import tool. Topics was also presented, the logic behind it and main difference between the KW research in SEMrush and Topics. Where SEMrush will provide you with all the words related to your search while our tool will properly curate the search and only provide the relevant results (no SV under 50, typos etc).

Competition Module was an overall easy discussion as there weren't many questions addressed aside from the standard how many competitors can I have listed, have them based on groups etc.

Content Performance was also discussed where we presented the different metrics on the page that could help in letting you know what articles brought more traffic overall to your landing pages. This is where the customer brought up the idea of using it for KPIs in regards to the articles written for customer however I intervened to let them know that the Content Performance Module is a key part of our tool however at this point and time development focus has been redirected to other modules and if they encounter any issue with it they should let us know as we can offer them full support and improve it based on the received feedback. They can give it a shot and see if it fulfils their needs.

Customer Questions during the call:

Q1: Hows the visibility calculated?
A: We have an algorithm that takes into account RANK+SV and assigns an Impression score. The average of all KWs impression shares will result in the visibility score. Article Link

Q2: How do we process the unallocated data that was encrypted?
A: A general number of unallocated that is reached normally, would be around 30%. -> Higher might mean there could be more GSCs profiles that need to be connected. It is pretty much impossible to break down everything and have 100% unallocated. -> Landing pages are too “thin” in GSC in order to be correlated (too few results too far between to be considered relevant or dependable data). Article Link

Q3: How fast does the unallocated data breakdown happens?
A: During the initial set-up in our tool, this process can take from a few minutes to a few hours depending on the amount of information present in GA and GSC (this will depend on the customer size). Otherwise updates are received overnight (clarifications to be obtained from the Devs as to the exact process on an established account) from the GA/GSC. There is no clear indication (loading bar, gears moving) of the ongoing process however if it takes more than 3 hours the customer should reach back as there might be issues with the process. No article can answer this.

Q4: How does forecasting Top 3/5/10 work exactly?
A: So when you select TOP 3 for forecasting in a group, what happens is that we calculate the average CTR for position 1/2/3 for those KWs and then use it as a reference point in the forecasting. Meaning that if the potential average CTR of the top 3 positions would be X CTRs, we would forecast a steady growth up until the X mark for them. Article Link.

Q5: We have 1 account but can we have multiple users per campaigns?
A: Yes, it is one account but it can have multiple users that can have access to all campaigns or restrict it. -> Account Settings, User settings. Article Link.

Q6: How does the migrations process work?
A: You can reach out to our customer service team via chat or we can introduce you to them and they can provide all of the information needed to successful accomplish this. There are a few templates that need to be filled out from your and we take care of the rest.

Call Observations: The call was, from my point of view didn't seem to follow any structure or the organic flow of the tool itself or any other type of structure. We frog leaped from one topic to another counterintuitive to the overall tool flow. If the Content Performance Module isn't a module we would like to advertise to the customer, perhaps we should try to avoid it or be frank from the get go that it isn't fully supported.

Call Feedback: Better prep in regards to the flow that would be followed and trying to lead from one thing to another in a more comprehensive way that would also organically lead from one topic to another.

cards.text: Audience: Kevin and SeanCall Recording: https://drive.google.com/open?id=1PI1TNWVeb7EMjI7AvC4nudwH-fBhLO6hPurpose: TrainingCall prep:Questions submitted by the customer: Listed below.Documentation sen
cards.html:
Audience: Kevin and Sean

Call Recording: https://drive.google.com/open?id=1PI1TNWVeb7EMjI7AvC4nudwH-fBhLO6h

Purpose: Training

Call prep:

  • Questions submitted by the customer: Listed below.

  • Documentation sent to the customer: No.

  • Existing tasks/feature requests for/from the customer: No.

Call Summary:

#1 SV for dangerous KWs - we are working on providing a solution in a roughly 2 weeks (EoM) - this will allow them to upload the SVs manually.

#2 Mismatching SVs between SEOmonitor and Keyword planner for the UK. KWP would have volumes of 1k or hundreds for keywords where we only present 10. Multiple campaigns might be effected. (Customer set up the search domain incorrectly - google.co.ck instead of google.co.uk)

#3 Rank Issues - due to the infinite Q loop, our crawlers did not properly read it and provided incorrect ranks for those KWs. The Bots have been updated to read it correctly even if they are reflected in the infinite loop.

#4 Also update annotation - SEJ did not post any updates for the June algorithm and due to that we had no annotations on the graph. We will make MOZ as a backup if SEJ does not provide algorithm updates. Hopefully this sprint, important for the customer to solution it.

Customer Questions during the call:

Q1: How to set up users?
A: User management -> set-up the users depending on the types you need as there are multiple access roles.

Call Observations: Customer is overall satisfied with our tool as well as being impressed by the status functionality (Bar).

Issues with the tool during the call: Tool was once again slow while using Zoom and recording the call.

Call Feedback: None.

cards.html:

Audience: Brad

Purpose: Training

Call Summary: Customer was interested in the forecasting module exclusively. A walkthrough of the Forecast module was presented. The overall flow of the calls was of a bad quality due to how the information was relayed back and the accuracy of it.

Customer Questions during the call:

Q1: How is the difficulty for the groups calculated in forecasting?
A: The Group level difficulty is an average between the line level of each kws' difficulty.

Q2: Where is the conversion rate in forecasting pulled from?
A: The conversion rate is pulled from the GA and also have a formula applied to it based on the visits the site is going to receive. (estimated)

Call Feedback: The call was all over the place and a more structured discussion should have occurred. The key points of the Forecast graph were not explained in detail and there was no clear conclusion as to how the Forecast can help the customer. The information in regards to the GA was not provided correctly while the information in regards to the difficulty meter was not provided correctly in its entirety.

cards.title: [internal] Can an account be set up with a non-business/professional email?
cards.text: Currently (last update Dec.2023), users cannot create an account using a non-business/professional email:gmailyahoohotmailaolicloudyandexprotonmailBut there is an internal workaround that can be used
cards.html:
Currently (last update Dec.2023), users cannot create an account using a non-business/professional email:

  • gmail
  • yahoo
  • hotmail
  • aol
  • icloud
  • yandex
  • protonmail

But there is an internal workaround that can be used for leads/prospects that are really worth skipping the rule.

Add them as guest of an active account, and then remove their access.

This is because, in SEOmonitor, any type of user is actually an account on its own.
It can become active if paid for or stays expired and just gives access to another or other paid/trial account/s.

For example, you add them as guest (view-only) on one of your own campaigns.
(later edit: removed the following, please don't add on the old Demo anymore on a campaign in the Demo account. )

  • They will receive a message to reset the password,
    • and then be able to access SEOmonitor.
  • At first, they will see the campaign you give them access to.
  • This is when you can remove their access,
    • and they will automatically be redirected to the Wizard.
  • Now, they can add their own campaigns,
  • benefit from the trial period,
  • and also subscribe.

cards.title: ​Are Instagram product links detected as backlinks?
cards.text: If an Instagram influencer points a link to a product on the user's webiste, we won't show it.The reason is because Ahrefs won't show it. Their official answer is copied below:We do not report the maj
cards.html:
If an Instagram influencer points a link to a product on the user's webiste, we won't show it.

The reason is because Ahrefs won't show it. Their official answer is copied below:
We do not report the major portion of Instagram links as they are placed on orphan pages, we simply do not crawl pages in Instagram as there are no links that will tell us to crawl some more pages. If somebody links to an Instagram account, we can follow this link and check Instagram page and report backlinks from there, but this is a very small number that will be reported by us.

cards.title: ​Campaign currency change
cards.text: As admin, from din website settings you have the option to change two currency options:- Analytics currency - doesn't change the numerical values, and doesn't calculate the conversion in that currency
cards.html:
As admin, from din website settings you have the option to change two currency options:

- Analytics currency - doesn't change the numerical values, and doesn't calculate the conversion in that currency (it is just a display setting)

- AdWords currency - changes only the currency of AdWords data (CPC) and starts a data reprocessing of AdWords data for all the keywords from the campaign.

PS: There is not need to reprocess the data (the actual options in red from the admin section)

LATER EDIT:

The currency must be changed in both fields to the appropriate currency acronym (Dollars = USD / Pound = GBP)
Pitching campaigns without GA are automatically set to EURO (adding a GA to them will have the currency overriden with the currency present in analytics)
Normal campaigns (and converted pitching campaigns with GA) automatically pull the currency from the GA (if we manually update the currency, on the next analytics update it will revert back the currency)

cards.text: Started from the API call: GET /compare/keywords/{site_id}/{start_date}/{end_date}/{competitors}/{group_id}How the actual ranks are displayed: If your website is added as a competitor: the API call wi
cards.html:
Started from the API call: GET /compare/keywords/{site_id}/{start_date}/{end_date}/{competitors}/{group_id}

How the actual ranks are displayed:

  • If your website is added as a competitor: the API call will return the exact rank value up to rank 98, and a higher value will be returned as 99+

Extras:

  • All SEOmonitor reporting options: CSVs, Sheets Addon, Looker / Data Studio, API

cards.title: ​Dashboard - Visibility Score and Trend ; how do we display & calculate them?
cards.text: Obsolete now - since the new Dashboard does not have these metricsVisibility scores over 10% lose the decimals - 18.8% becomes 18%Visibility Scores below 10% keep decimals - 5.2% remains 5.2%Pitching
cards.html:
Obsolete now - since the new Dashboard does not have these metrics

Visibility scores over 10% lose the decimals - 18.8% becomes 18%

Visibility Scores below 10% keep decimals - 5.2% remains 5.2%

Pitching campaigns will have the N/A display because these don't have a trend.

Average Visibility Score from Dashboard is calculated: the visibility score for the current day - (minus) media from the last 8 days.

The idea behind it is that if one of the days from the last week had a major change in Visibility, that would be reflected in the Dashboard, but since it would be just an event and not the normal trend of the visibility, might be misleading at a first glance, when you check the Dashboard data.

So with this Average, we make sure the user can see at a first glance, what the normal trend is, and only continuous or permanent change will appear as a significant change in the Dashboard as well.

However, since this can seem a bit confusing, we are planning in the future a change so that the users can select their own type of period for the trend & average in the Dashboard or if they prefer to see the same numbers as in SEO Campaign for the current day.

cards.title: ​Demo Call - FLIXBUS (Ana)
cards.text: Audience: paul.tyler@flixbus.comPurpose: Demo quick call - SID 60089Call prep: N/AQuestions submitted by the customer: NoneDocumentation sent to the customer: None Existing tasks/feature requests for/
cards.html:
Audience: paul.tyler@flixbus.com

Purpose: Demo quick call - SID 60089

Call prep: N/A

  • Questions submitted by the customer: None

  • Documentation sent to the customer: None

  • Existing tasks/feature requests for/from the customer: None

Call Summary:

The call was a short one, around 25 minutes, as the client had another meeting to attend to.

The main topics were: adding keywords, ranks and their frequency, SERP features identifications, competitor details, and API usage.

In SEO Campaign was discussed the option to add keywords, by pasting them or by uploading. The limitations were explained, special characters for keywords, groups, and folder, as well as the total number of keywords to be tracked in a single campaign (100K).

There was an interest in SERP features: the detection of it, the display in our app and the option to filter them for his site or competitors, as well as creating a smart group based on it.

Here it was shown the export, CSV and SERP CSV.

In Research a very quick overview was done for adding keywords.

In Competition Insights there was a short discussion on how many competitors can be added, on All keywords and for each group.

A short explanation that we provided free of charge information on keywords ranks for all competitors, and that keywords from our database can be uncovered for each competitor.

Customer Questions during the call:

Q1: How can I add a large number of keywords? Is there a limit to it?

A: Keywords can be added in multiple ways: paste and upload (from SEO Campaign and Research), or from our database. More details in this article(Possible link to Articles if they exist or guru cards for issues)

Q2: How often do we crawl for ranks?

A: The crawl is done daily, for both desktop and mobile.

Q3: Do we detect SERP features?

A: Yes, the SERP features are detected and displayed in our app, in top stats, and on keywords table. We even offer the option to create a smart group with these criteria.

Q4: Do we offer ranks details for other search engines?

A: No, we are focused on Google search engines, and don’t offer other option at the moment.

Q5: Do we offer API and how much it costs?

A: Yes, we have the API option and it’s free of cost. The API token can be generated from the Account setting section, where the API documentation and even DataStudio guidelines can be found.

Call Observations: The client was very happy with the crawl frequency, detection of SERP features and free API.

It seemed unsatisfied that we don’t offer rank details for other search engines.

Issues with the tool during the call: Nothing major, just slow loading (I blame it on the fire alarm that we had in the morning that interfered with our internet)

Call Feedback: I would take another 24 hours to send questions to the user, and time to study his full story sessions.

cards.title: ​Details on 'localized group' option in the same campaign
cards.text: There won't be a limit of how many keywords can be localized or number of locations, for the same campaign.You will also be able to have different language groups, for countries with more than 1 langu
cards.html:
There won't be a limit of how many keywords can be localized or number of locations, for the same campaign.

You will also be able to have different language groups, for countries with more than 1 language (like in Belgium, Swisserland etc.).

cards.title: ​Difference between Brand keywords and keywords labeled as brand (of mine or of others)
cards.text: * removal of Brand words - The Brand keywords cannot be removed, because it was our original intent to clearly separate the brand words from non brand words.The only way to remove them would be to del
cards.html:
* removal of Brand words - The Brand keywords cannot be removed, because it was our original intent to clearly separate the brand words from non brand words.

The only way to remove them would be to delete them from the campaign.

*purpose of separating these Brand words - If brand and non-brand words are mixed together, the visibility score would not be as relevant - since the brand words will usually rank 1st, it would artificially distort the score.

The Brand folder is conceived with the idea that all keywords recognized as your brand by Google should be excluded from your actual campaign Visibility.

Thus, besides the keywords you define as brand rule when you set up the campaign (and which decide how the traffic is split), we automatically recognise some others, some of which we’d automatically pull in the Brand folder. These follow certain rules and will be based on how Google sees them in the SERP (like if your site is listed in all of the first results).

  • Brand label There’s a Brand label (in green or red) that appears next to some keywords. This is also related to the SERP and whether there are full sitelinks present OR a knowledge graph and small sitelinks, for example. So, if it’s your campaign in the full sitelinks, the label is green, signalling that it is seen by Google as your brand. If there are full sitelinks, but there’s another site listed, it would be in red, so a brand of others.

SERPs are to be attached as visual aid and explanatory proof when discussing about brand words/brand labels

  • mark as brand - A 3rd category would be the ones that meet a smaller set of criteria, but which could be 'marked as brand'. These are keywords that you’re ranking 1st for AND (contain your current brand keyword OR your site in the first positions). You’ll see that there’s the Mark as brand button on the keyword page.

cards.title: ​Different number of non-brand keywords for the same Landing Page in 2 sections of the app
cards.text: Differences between SEO Campaign section, the Landing page tab, and keyword page, in the Competition tab.The number non-brand keywords in the SEO Campaign section, in the Landing page tab, is differen
cards.html:
Differences between SEO Campaign section, the Landing page tab, and keyword page, in the Competition tab.

The number non-brand keywords in the SEO Campaign section, in the Landing page tab, is different to the one displayed on the keyword page, in the Competition tab (http://take.ms/KKJFC).

The difference is because in the SEO Campaign section, in the Landing page tab (http://take.ms/Rtgy9), the list of keywords is the one of the tracked keywords that have that specific landing page at the time of the latest crawl, and on the keyword page, in the Competition tab, the keywords are the ones from our Topic Explorer database that match that specific landing page.

cards.title: ​Do we have a user agent for content crawling, and what are our IPs?
cards.text: We are crawling Google's cached version of our client's site in the majority of cases. On the rare occasions when we can't detect the content using Google's cache, we will crawl the user's site direct
cards.html:
We are crawling Google's cached version of our client's site in the majority of cases.

On the rare occasions when we can't detect the content using Google's cache, we will crawl the user's site directly using the following user agent: Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; SEOmonitor/1.0)

If the client further asks what the IPs are, we tell him that there are many and they vary, but he can whitelist our user agent.

Note: some clients may ask about a "static IP address," and we can provide the above information.

cards.title: ​Do we migrate historical data from SearchMetrics?
cards.text: Yes we do, but before we provide a full promise to the client, please mention this aspect:While SearchMetrics migration developments are fully implemented, we have had a particular type of issue with
cards.html:
Yes we do, but before we provide a full promise to the client, please mention this aspect:

While SearchMetrics migration developments are fully implemented, we have had a particular type of issue with this tool. They are quite demanding in reference to API units, so with your current resources we may not gather all historical data that you currently have with them. They may ask for extra resources when the API calls are done. 

Should you provide us with the login credentials, we will of course run a test to calculate whether the existent resources are enough, what percentage of data they could cover, or how many resources would be necessary to export all of the existing data.

cards.title: ​Do you have ranks & VS in the same day you connect the GA to a pitching account?
cards.text: Yes, the processing of ranks and VS start in the same day you connect the GA (and GSC).
cards.html: Yes, the processing of ranks and VS start in the same day you connect the GA (and GSC).

cards.title: ​Does SEOmonitor do audit?
cards.text: SEOmonitor doesn't do audit.the only type of data you get, regarding the landing page's content (title, metadescription, canonical, backlinks etc.) are the one from the keyword's page (which you can a
cards.html:
• SEOmonitor doesn't do audit.

  • the only type of data you get, regarding the landing page's content (title, metadescription, canonical, backlinks etc.) are the one from the keyword's page (which you can access by clicking on each keyword) -> Landing page tab or a small preview of this when you click on the 'i' icon, from the 'Page' column when you are on all keywords view, also in SEO Campaign.

Initial question from the user:  are you able to look at sites with javascript rendering in seomonitor's audits?

cards.html:

Usually in Chargebee, this will appear as a "Do not honor" at some point on the invoice.

When a new credit card is added, a check to the bank is done that appears as a new transation. Its status depends on the bank, and there isn't anything that we can do from our side.

cards.title: ​Estimated Additional Visits, Estimated Cost and Estimated Additional Revenue - from the top CSV export in SEO Campaign or in Keyword Page
cards.text: Are internal estimation made we make on how many:visits costrevenuethat keyword will bring, if it reaches top3.Update (20.10.2020) These calculate the ranking position difference you're at right now t
cards.html:
Are internal estimation made we make on how many:

  • visits

  • cost

  • revenue

that keyword will bring, if it reaches top3.

Update (20.10.2020) These calculate the ranking position difference you're at right now towards reaching top 3 through sv and ctr. Due to the fact that sv is average, these DON'T take into consideration seasonality labels.

When it comes to small numbers for search volume, which is part of the calculating formula for the e, we round up the number.

​(this is the data the question refers to https://monosnap.com/file/CoulxJQXavt2lcxnAEW3fS6KR091YS)

How reliable are they?

Just as reliable as an estimation can be.

cards.title: ​Feature snippets for you and your competitors - the displayed rank
cards.text: If you are present in the SERP feature snippets, your rank display will be:1 for the header feature snippertswebsite rank, and highlight of feature snippets for their presence in the right sidebarIf t
cards.html:
If you are present in the SERP feature snippets, your rank display will be:

  • 1 for the header feature snipperts

  • website rank, and highlight of feature snippets for their presence in the right sidebar

If the competitor has featured snippets, its ranks will be position 1, and you are right after him your position will be 2 (for ex competitor feature snippets 1 + your website on 9 => seomonitor rank display 10). -- this took effect on 7th Feb 2020.

​Additional info drom Dragos - CS only
for very small keywords, like the ones with search volume 10, they won't be crawled daily, so it will take effect later.

(you can check them by clicking on the recrawl button, the date on the serp it was shown for today but it s because it was crawled for another website).

cards.title: ​Forecast SV after calibrating YoY
cards.text: If a campaign has calibrated the YoY it must have the same values in the Search Seasonality column as in the graph display, for each month.If the YoY was calibrated after the scenario was updated or t
cards.html:
If a campaign has calibrated the YoY it must have the same values in the Search Seasonality column as in the graph display, for each month.

If the YoY was calibrated after the scenario was updated or the Objective was set, then different numbers are expected to be displayed in those 2 sections mentioned above.

Later edit December 2020:

For the 2021 Forecast, whether the campaign is calibrated or not, it will still relate to the "new normal" by taking into account the average monthly search volumes for the past 12 months and the YoY trend, for example October 2019 - October 2020 or November 2019 - November 2020.
Therefore, the calibration won't make much of a difference for the 2021 Forecast set up.

cards.title: ​Google Analytics data - who can access it and where it's stored
cards.text: Specific questions for GA data:1. Who can access GA data in SEOmonitor? - Anyone who has access to that website (owner and subusers), as well as SEOmonitor employees (they have a confidentiality agree
cards.html:
Specific questions for GA data:

1. Who can access GA data in SEOmonitor? - Anyone who has access to that website (owner and subusers), as well as SEOmonitor employees (they have a confidentiality agreement).

2. Where are we storing those data? - On our database servers, 3 backups in 2 countries:

  • 1 hot standby replica (DE)

  • 1 weekly dump (DE)

  • 1 copy of the weekly dump (US)

3. Does SEOmonitor encrypt GA data? - No

cards.title: ​How and when are Snippets displayed on the graph from keyword page?
cards.text: The Feature Snippets displayed on the keyword page (https://take.ms/SOCtY) and on Keywords list (https://take.ms/LENUI)  are displayed based on the current day state of the keywords, so the last avail
cards.html:
The Feature Snippets displayed on the keyword page (https://take.ms/SOCtY) and on Keywords list (https://take.ms/LENUI) are displayed based on the current day state of the keywords, so the last available rank.

Now the crawl of ranks starts from the 8am the previous day and end current day, at 8 am. E.g. ranks for 1st November, start from from 8 am on 30th October. If they are finished by the end of 30th, you can see the SERP snippet in both places mentioned above on the 30th.

And on the graph from the keyword page - https://take.ms/UE4Xfq, you will only see it displayed on 1st of November.

Original text: snippet urile de sus si din manage sunt pe state ul actual al cuvintelor, care se scrie la ultimul rank, acum rank urile incep in ziua anterioara la ora 8 dimineata si se termina in ziua urmatoare la ora 8 dimineata deci rankurile pentru 1 incep pe 30 la 8 dimineata , daca apuca sa l faca pe 30 o sa vezi serp feature ul ep 30 dar pe grafic o sa apara pe 1.'

cards.title: ​How do Signals card behave?
cards.text: Q: How long do we keep cards?A: We keep them for 7 days, if no action is taken they get deleted. Cards with actions  (comments, saved/pinned, shared) don't.Q: How long do we display a card, already se
cards.html:
Q: How long do we keep cards?

A: We keep them for 7 days, if no action is taken they get deleted. Cards with actions (comments, saved/pinned, shared) don't.

Q: How long do we display a card, already seen by others?

A: We keep all cards for the user, no matter if colleagues saw them.

Once marked as seen (and no action was taken on them), they won’t appear again in other feeds (signals view, mobile app, dashboard etc.)

Q: Can you interact with the shared card link? ex. https://signls.io/share_fdl/41501425/edefb8e8407cb44b4ac3114d89f4a673583ec061

A: At the moment, the card detail view is functional and interacive as long as the user has access to that campaign. In the browser view, it’s just a static render, but we have an upgrade on the way to make it interactive.

cards.title: ​Misspelled words and labels
cards.text: First of all, it is important to emphasize that the Misspelling label is just a mere "warning", so there's nothing to worry about: If the clients want to disregard them, they can do so.They appear in
cards.html:
First of all, it is important to emphasize that the Misspelling label is just a mere "warning", so there's nothing to worry about: If the clients want to disregard them, they can do so.

They appear in 2 cases

  • one in which Google explicitly identifies the keyword as a "misspelling", believing the intention of the user is to search for a different query and, in turn, offering results for the said query; an example for this is where we have the "showing results for..." warning at the top (sometimes appearing as "did you mean...").

  • the other one is the one where Google doesn't even give a warning anymore, but just directly provides results for a variation of the search query, as it identifies that as the more relevant one..

For the latter, we have a "fallback" mechanism, where we check that: EVERY keyword (3-letter minimum) from the query would be a part of the title OR description of minimum 5% of the organic results.

It works with the keyword being separated by the other keywords in the query by: space, comma, full stop. We'll be adding: apostrophes, quotation marks, semi-colons, dashes, and forward and backward slashes.

Extra info (added by Ana, 28 Aug)
The N/A display to the correct version will appear when the correct version is not shown in the SERP, but it is detected by our algorithm

cards.html:

just for the competitors because there’s no reason to try to estimate your non-brand organic traffic as you already know it precisely

cards.html:
For timeframes that include months that have both 30 and 31 days, we'll see more dots on the VS line, on monthly granularity, than the actual number of months.

This is due to the fact that we count a month as 30 days (not 31)

Example: if I have 1 December 2019 - 31 January 2020, I will see 3 dots on VS line, rather than 2 - https://take.ms/qjTrO.

cards.html:
The Track subdomain option is available after the campaign is set up. The added subdomain will be taken into consideration for ranks display in the following days (not the day of adding the campaign).

For Pitching campaigns this option will not have any effect on the ranks display because the ranks are already crawled and only the domain has been taken into consideration. Even adding a subdomain afterwards won't have any effect because the ranks are crawled only once, when the campaign was added.

cards.title: ​Things to avoid in discussions with customers
cards.text: Please add more as you encounter them:only use our competitor's names in technical discussions - for example, we say we do migrate from Semrush - but we say we're taking search volumes from clickstrea
cards.html:
Please add more as you encounter them:

  • only use our competitor's names in technical discussions - for example, we say we do migrate from Semrush - but we say we're taking search volumes from clickstream data; we don't advertise semrush or competitors

  • don't mention our internal mechanisms, never use bots - try to find a contextual synonim: algorithms, processes, systems

cards.title: ​Topical Authority  - keywords taken into consideration to calculate it
cards.text: We consider as authoritative landing pages the pages that are promoted by Google, at the top of the search results for certain keywords. The higher the rank and the search volume, the higher is the au
cards.html:
We consider as authoritative landing pages the pages that are promoted by Google, at the top of the search results for certain keywords. The higher the rank and the search volume, the higher is the authoritative landing page.

Our algorithm is simple, and it's based on the non-brand keywords that respond to the landing page, no matter if the keywords are tracked or not.

We call it "Topical Authority" because the keywords that rank for a landing page are generally related to the landing page subject as well.

Note: Our Chrome Extension allows easy exploration of these keywords.

cards.title: ​Topics - Search Volume display
cards.text: The Search Volume value is the average search volume of the last 12 months.In the tool tip there will be displayed the trend, available for the same month as the check's month, being highlighted.
cards.html:
The Search Volume value is the average search volume of the last 12 months.

In the tool tip there will be displayed the trend, available for the same month as the check's month, being highlighted.

cards.title: ​Trends.seomonitor.com for sites
cards.text: When adding any URL to https://trends.seomonitor.com, we don't change it in the upper box, but we copy only the domain in the lower box (https://take.ms/4DOHN).We remove anything besides the main doma
cards.html:
1. When adding any URL to https://trends.seomonitor.com, we don't change it in the upper box, but we copy only the domain in the lower box (https://take.ms/4DOHN).

We remove anything besides the main domain (http, www, any subdomain or folder of the website).

e.g.  https://www.m.vodafone.co.uk/network/5g/why-vodafone will become vodafone.co.uk.

  1. The Search Volume you see in Adwords is not relevant, since currently it shows you the estimated data (as our Adwords account, where CS has access, is not sufficiently 'mature' to show you exact data). The tool takes the data from another account, which offers us the exact Search Volume of the keywords.

  2. When you see keywords with this display https://take.ms/K4TJAl, that is normal; if last year it had no Search Volume and now it does, we can't calculate a percentage in growth (from 0 to 100 let's say). We kept such keywords in the list, so that the user can see these newcomers, which are relevant to its business.

cards.title: ​Unallocated non-brand with landing pages that have /courses or /logout
cards.text: There are cases when the landing pages of the user are for an app or after login session. In the GA profile, the landing pages that are matched with courses Id, assigned or logout is not considered as
cards.html:
There are cases when the landing pages of the user are for an app or after login session.

In the GA profile, the landing pages that are matched with courses Id, assigned or logout is not considered as organic visits. 
First, we need to check if they are indexed by Google (and they are not):

  • coursed id: http://take.ms/fVi0Je

  • assigned: http://take.ms/9Ld2Y 

  • logout: http://take.ms/mgH3II

In case the user can't filter them in your GA account, based on similar cases, our team has developed the option that allows us to filter them in our app, so that you can see more accurate data in your account. 

Note: The landing pages for login are indexed, but the ones that are matched with courses Id, assigned or logout are not considered as organic visits. In the overall sessions data, they are taken into consideration and counted.

cards.title: ​Visibility - weekly or monthly granularity
cards.text: In SEO Campaign and Organic Traffic:On the graph, on weekly/monthly granularity, the Visibility is the average for the period (week/month): weekly granularity: the values are the average Visibility of
cards.html:
In SEO Campaign and Organic Traffic:

On the graph, on weekly/monthly granularity, the Visibility is the average for the period (week/month):

  • weekly granularity: the values are the average Visibility of the daily Visibility for Monday to Sunday that week, if a full week, or shorter based on the timeframe selected.
  • month granularity: the values are the average Visibility of the daily Visibility for the full month (1st to the end of the month, if a full one, or shorter based on the timeframe selected.

You have the exact interval in the tooltip (mouseover on the graph) and the corresponding total number of sessions for that timeframe.

In Analysis:

When you have the weekly or monthly granularity selected, the Visibility Score is not an average, but the daily Visibility for the date shown on the graph.

  • week granularity: last day of each week in the selected timeframe
  • month granularity: last day of each month in the selected timeframe


!!! On daily granularity, the Visibility in top stats and in the last point on the graph (last day of the selected timeframe) are the same.

cards.title: ​Visibility Score export for a specific timeframe, in a smart group, will have each day of the time frame data or only the data on the date when it was requested?
cards.text: Changing the timeframe in the Calendar will not change the Visibility Score data when requesting an export. The export will have only the data for the keywords that now correspond to the filter.(This
cards.html:
Changing the timeframe in the Calendar will not change the Visibility Score data when requesting an export.

The export will have only the data for the keywords that now correspond to the filter.

(This is because the keywords of each smart group are calculated on-the-fly, including for exports.)

cards.title: ​Visibility Score in the Proposal Builder
cards.text: The Visibility Score you see in the Proposal Builder is always for the day the report is made , while in the app you see it for the last day selected in the timeframe.In the Proposal Builder there isn
cards.html:
The Visibility Score you see in the Proposal Builder is always for the day the report is made , while in the app you see it for the last day selected in the timeframe.

In the Proposal Builder there isn't the option for timeframe, it is always today.

Also, please note that the Visibility Score from the Proposal Builder is always rounded up (e.g. 18,1 becomes 19)

cards.title: ​What are the Brand criteria for Topics?
cards.text: In rezultatele din Topic Explorer intră doar cuvintele care au (site_links < 2).Proprietatea site_links se stabileste în funcție de rezultatele de pe primele poziții din SERP și se clasifică astfel, i
cards.html:
In rezultatele din Topic Explorer intră doar cuvintele care au (site_links < 2).

Proprietatea site_links se stabileste în funcție de rezultatele de pe primele poziții din SERP și se clasifică astfel, iar prioritatea stabilirii valorii este în ordine:

  • NOT BRANDS

  • site_links = 0 - primul rezultat nu conține decât titlu și descriere, fără alte briz-briz-uri

https://api.monosnap.com/rpc/file/download?id=7gYSJS9Ni09rbGDCECF8Jb8CGHSBIc

  • site_links = 1 - primul rezultat conține link-uri (mici), sub descriere

https://api.monosnap.com/rpc/file/download?id=uypFmvYpTwKPm7kvxzH3n1vyT6OAMS

  • BRANDS

  • site_links = 2 - primul rezultat conține link-uri cu descriere (mari), sub descrierea rezultatului

https://api.monosnap.com/rpc/file/download?id=Wxy78VFwWco5SntYe3UtzaYJlaDgUJ

  • site_links = 3 - primele 4 rezultate aparțin aceluiași domeniu

https://api.monosnap.com/rpc/file/download?id=VjVAyJLdYWQs3pPdZm05t0w5xhDMpa

  • site_links = 4 - cuvântul conține un alt cuvânt cu site_links = 2, de exemplu "minifrigider emag" sau "raiffeisen carduri"

  • site_links = 5 - cuvântul are primele trei rezultate aparținând aceluiași domeniu, plus un Knowledge Graph

  • site_links = 6 - cuvântul are un Knowledge Graph de unul din următoarele tipuri:

  • Company

  • Video Game

  • site_links = 7 - cuvântul are site_links = 1 și primele 2 rezultate aparțin aceluiași domeniu

  • SPECIAL NOT BRANDS

  • site_links = -2 - este un cuvânt care are site_links = 2, dar primul link nu e http://homepage.tld/, sau http://homepage.tld/[2 litere]/ sau http://domain.tld/[2litere]-[2litere]/ - aici intră de obicei cuvinte cheie care desemnează produse, gen macbook pro, samsung galaxy, placa de baza asus, etc. dar si exceptii de genul "subliminal dex", "bucuresti iasi km", "cinema cluj", "facebook ajutor".

cards.title: ​What are the types of Brand results (Topics)
cards.text: ITALICS = VERIFY EXTRAIn the Brand folder we have more types of brand words.the ones initially briefed by Cosminautomatically drawn in the folder - label attached ----> site_links 3automatically drawn
cards.html:
ITALICS = VERIFY EXTRA

In the Brand folder we have more types of brand words.

the ones initially briefed by Cosmin

  1. automatically drawn in the folder - label attached ----> site_links 3

  2. automatically drawn in the folder - no label

  3. manually drawn in the folder - no label

(Each type will have a different level of unmark potential, pls check that as well with Product)

The categories above come from the below list, but we need to corelate them:

The Brand categorization is based on site_links properties, depending on the results of the first positions in the SERP. In Topics we only display keywords with site_links<2

  • BRANDS

  • site_links = 2 - the first result contains links with descriptions (big format) below the initial description of the link https://api.monosnap.com/rpc/file/download?id=Wxy78VFwWco5SntYe3UtzaYJlaDgUJ

  • site_links = 3 - the first 4 positions belong to the same domain https://api.monosnap.com/rpc/file/download?id=VjVAyJLdYWQs3pPdZm05t0w5xhDMpa

  • site_links = 4 - the keyword phrase contains a keyword with site_links 2, examples: "minifrigider emag" or "raiffeisen carduri"

  • site_links = 5 - the first 3 results belong to the same domain, while the 4th is a Knowledge Graph

  • site_links = 6 - the keyword has a Knowledge Graph of types Company or Video Game

  • site_links = 7 - keyword has site_links = 1 and first 2 results belong to same domain

The non-brand types:

  • NOT BRANDS

  • site_links = 0 - first result only contains title and description, nothing else https://api.monosnap.com/rpc/file/download?id=7gYSJS9Ni09rbGDCECF8Jb8CGHSBIc

  • site_links = 1 - first result contains small links under the description https://api.monosnap.com/rpc/file/download?id=uypFmvYpTwKPm7kvxzH3n1vyT6OAMS

  • SPECIAL NOT BRANDS

  • site_links = -2 - it's a keyword with site_links=2 but the first link is not http://homepage.tld/ , or http://homepage.tld/[de]/ or http://domain.tld/[ch]-[de]/ - these are usually keywords designating products such as macbook pro, samsung galaxy, placa de baza asus, etc. and exceptions such as "subliminal dex", "bucuresti iasi km", "cinema cluj", "facebook ajutor".

cards.title: ​What is canonical tag and how we display it?
cards.text: TO BE UPDATEDA canonical tag (aka "rel canonical") is a way of telling search engines that a specific URL represents the master copy of a page. Using the canonical tag prevents problems caused by iden
cards.html:
TO BE UPDATED

A canonical tag (aka "rel canonical") is a way of telling search engines that a specific URL represents the master copy of a page. Using the canonical tag prevents problems caused by identical or "duplicate" content appearing on multiple URLs.

​The check is to be made on both the website and within the app:

  1. go to the URL --> right click --> select View Page Source --> search ctrl+F for canonical http://take.ms/NEICGe

  2. go to Manage, LP beta tab and check the indexability - if the page has canonical info, we should display it http://take.ms/HoLys

cards.html:

It is to be filled in before saving the forecast. If left unfilled in, the field should be hidden after saving the forecast.
