cards.title: ​What is the difference between google.com “global” and “U.S.”?
cards.text: A short explanation of the .com search engines available in step 2 of setting up a campaign. When setting up a campaign, and selecting google.com, there are 2 options for this search engine: google.co
cards.html:
A short explanation of the .com search engines available in step 2 of setting up a campaign.

When setting up a campaign, and selecting google.com, there are 2 options for this search engine: google.com (global) and google.com (US).

The difference between those 2 is that:

- for google.com (global) - we provide global search volumes

- for google.com (US) - we provide US search volumes

Note that all keyword rankings and search volumes will be provided based on this setting.

cards.title: ​What is the resource limit for trials?
cards.text: The only limit for users during the trial period is up to 30.000 keywords for the entire account (pitching keywords aren't taken into account).It used to be 20K but we had a lot of trial accounts that
cards.html:
The only limit for users during the trial period is up to 30.000 keywords for the entire account (pitching keywords aren't taken into account).

It used to be 20K but we had a lot of trial accounts that added large campaigns (more than 100K keywords added in one day). So in order to reduce the load on the swarm that these trial campaigns were creating we reduced the maximum number of allowed keywords for trial accounts from to 30K

​

cards.title: ​What options are availabe for keywords in groups and folders?
cards.text: In groups, there are 4 options - http://take.ms/Ia8jYremove from groupadd keywords to groupmove keywordsdelete keywordsIn folders, 2 options are available - http://take.ms/4QDaz - the reason is that a
cards.html:
In groups, there are 4 options - http://take.ms/Ia8jY

  1. remove from group

  2. add keywords to group

  3. move keywords

  4. delete keywords

In folders, 2 options are available - http://take.ms/4QDaz - the reason is that a keyword belongs to a group, not to a folder; the folder is the tag of multiple groups; the script wouldn't know in which groups a certan keyword should remain and in which not; the only possible outcome would be to remove/move all those keywords, but that is only a feature request at the moment, since the product was conceived with a different purpose

  1. add keywords to group (you then select the desired group)

  2. delete keywords

Special mentions:

  • a more recent addition - `p{Mn}` - “\p{Mn} or \p{Non_Spacing_Mark}: a character intended to be combined with another character without taking up extra space (e.g. accents, umlauts, etc.).”

  • foreign characters such as Arabic fall under the \p{L} code

cards.title: ​What types of Annotations do we have in SEOmonitor?
cards.text: (a se completa pana le avem pe toate explicate)the list of annotations can be found in the Annotations Info explainer in Manage - http://take.ms/fRrGIssues found (I)ex.: offline pages detected - http:
cards.html:
(a se completa pana le avem pe toate explicate)

the list of annotations can be found in the Annotations Info explainer in Manage - http://take.ms/fRrG

  • Issues found (I)

ex.: offline pages detected - http://take.ms/7FnoG

The LPs that are mentioned in the annotation are the ones that turned offline that particular day (and were not offline the previous day). The annotation is displayed only if the corresponding lost traffic represents more than 1% out of the total monthly organic traffic.

  • Content Changes - this category includes a series of modifications in
    de page, detected by the on-page module; examples: changes in H1, H2,
    H3, canonical, noindex, etc

  • Title Change - the title of the landing page changes

cards.title: ​What types of Organic Visits do we show in Manage?
cards.text: In Manage, we show Organic Visits under both the Keyword view and the Landing Page view - http://take.ms/z5FTvOn keyword view, the Organic Visits are the total non-brand visits generated by the monito
cards.html:
In Manage, we show Organic Visits under both the Keyword view and the Landing Page view - http://take.ms/z5FTv

On keyword view, the Organic Visits are the total non-brand visits generated by the monitored keywords. 


The landing page view shows all the non-brand visits generated by the landing pages of the monitored keywords (not just by the monitored keywords).


The use-case could be the following - if you optimize your monitored words, you optimize their landing pages, thus generating more visibility for the not-monitored keywords. Thus, this is a useful metric to evaluate the non-brand organic traffic performance following the optimization of a group of keywords.

cards.title: ​When do groups appear as Unnested in Forecast?
cards.text: In Unnested (https://take.ms/kTeWI) we put only groups (the rule adds only those that aren't smart groups or don't have subfolders).
cards.html: In Unnested (https://take.ms/kTeWI) we put only groups (the rule adds only those that aren't smart groups or don't have subfolders).

cards.title: ​When is YoY supposed to be displayed?
cards.text: Only when we have 13 months of Search Volume available.YoY search volume is calculated as search volume last month versus search volume the same month one year ago. That means we need 13 months of tre
cards.html:
Only when we have 13 months of Search Volume available.

YoY search volume is calculated as search volume last month versus search volume the same month one year ago.

That means we need 13 months of trend, and Google Ads gives us only the last 12. That’s why we need up to one month from the moment the keyword was added in order to compute the YoY search trend. We update search volumes once a month and that’s when we concatenate the new last 12 months of trend with the previous one and get the 13 months of historic search trends.​

This means that the YoY is only displayed up to 1 month after the campaign is added, since we can only get 12 months initially from Adwords. It’s up to a month because we update the search volumes at the end of each month (between days 24-31). So if you add a campaign right before that, it can be just a few days.

cards.title: ​Why do I see more keywords than I actually uploaded in a group?
cards.text: The close variations always stay/move together. You can’t have some close variations in a group and others in another group.Example case: You're adding 50 kws to a group but the group shows 58 - the 8
cards.html:
The close variations always stay/move together. 
You can’t have some close variations in a group and others in another group.

Example case:

You're adding 50 kws to a group but the group shows 58 - the 8 extra kws are close variations, either children or parents, depending on the status of the input keyword

a. if shown 50+8, it's 50 original kws + 8 children

b. if shown 52+6, it's 50 original kws + 2 parents + 6 children

The extra keywords are always keywords that were already tracked in the campaign

if you want to limit the original number, please check the Guru card with deleting variations - How to delete close variation keywords?

cards.title: ​Why do columns in Keyword and LP view differ?
cards.text: In Manage:a. KW view the mandatory columns are 3: SERP, SV, Rank  - http://take.ms/r1l5lin addition, you can choose from among 13 other columns - http://take.ms/jl1o1b. LP view for timeframes shorter
cards.html:
In Manage:

a. KW view

  • the mandatory columns are 3: SERP, SV, Rank - http://take.ms/r1l5l

  • in addition, you can choose from among 13 other columns - http://take.ms/jl1o1

b. LP view

  • for timeframes shorter than 90 days, we have 11 columns - http://take.ms/qRPo0

  • for timeframes longer than 90 days, we reduce the number of columns, for loading time optimisation to 6 - http://take.ms/f3SrBU

cards.title: ​Why is there 'Missing landing page' on LP view for my tracked keywords?
cards.text: You see 'Missing landing page' for some keywords with 99+ ranks - (like in this example https://take.ms/MbDEN). Since when we did the crawl we found no landing page that contains the domain's name in
cards.html:
You see 'Missing landing page' for some keywords with 99+ ranks - (like in this example https://take.ms/MbDEN).

Since when we did the crawl we found no landing page that contains the domain's name in top 100, we continue the search even further and when we still can't identify an indexed page for them, in Google, we display this message.

Usually the tracked keywords in this situation, have this warning message when you are on keywords view - https://take.ms/hzxxc.

Thus, you can obtain such results by filtering according to warnings --> no relevant pages. The shown results will always have a yellow info box.

cards.title: ​Why is there no value for Search Volume in Topics?
cards.text: The reason why you see such cases - https://take.ms/obSHL is due to the fact that some of the keywords are crawled on-the-fly and it takes a while (sometimes even more than several hours) until the se
cards.html:
The reason why you see such cases - https://take.ms/obSHL is due to the fact that some of the keywords are crawled on-the-fly and it takes a while (sometimes even more than several hours) until the search volume data is displayed (either until it is received from KT.io or, if they are from SEOmonitor, it can last until the SV is available in the app as well).

Note: The team will create a label/warning to inform people that the search volume is pending the update.

cards.title: ​​Does SEOmonitor access our historical data, when connecting GA or only data from the moment of the connection onwards?
cards.text: SEOmonitor accesses the historical data, and there is no way to go around that.We always get the initial one-month OT data upon connection, with the possibility of reprocessing it up to one year back.
cards.html:
SEOmonitor accesses the historical data, and there is no way to go around that.

We always get the initial one-month OT data upon connection, with the possibility of reprocessing it up to one year back.
The latter becomes available when you select a timeframe starting before the default one month.

[Context] The question came from a client who's URLs contain personally identifiable information (MSISDN, which is basically the phone number of their other clients), which can interfere with data protection laws.
